items.title: The three Quls, breathed into your hands
items.arabic:
قُلْ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ۝ ٱللَّهُ ٱلصَّمَدُ ۝ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ ۝ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُۥ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌۢ
قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلْفَلَقِ ۝ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ ٱلنَّفَّٰثَٰتِ فِى ٱلْعُقَدِ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ
قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مَلِكِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ إِلَٰهِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مِن شَرِّ ٱلْوَسْوَاسِ ٱلْخَنَّاسِ ۝ ٱلَّذِى يُوَسْوِسُ فِى صُدُورِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ
items.en: Say, "He is Allah, [who is] One, Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, Nor is there to Him any equivalent." / Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak From the evil of that which He created And from the evil of darkness when it settles And from the evil of the blowers in knots And from the evil of an envier when he envies." / Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, The Sovereign of mankind, The God of mankind, From the evil of the retreating whisperer - Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind - From among the jinn and mankind."
items.note: Cup your hands, recite the three suras into them, and wipe over your body, head and face, beginning with the front. The Prophet ﷺ did this three times each night.

items.title: Ayat al-Kursi
items.arabic: ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْحَىُّ ٱلْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُۥ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُۥٓ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَىْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَـُٔودُهُۥ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْعَظِيمُ
items.en: Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.
items.note: Whoever recites it on lying down, the Prophet ﷺ said, has a guardian from Allah and no devil comes near him until morning.

items.title: The last two verses of al-Baqarah
items.arabic:
ءَامَنَ ٱلرَّسُولُ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِۦ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ۚ كُلٌّ ءَامَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَمَلَٰٓئِكَتِهِۦ وَكُتُبِهِۦ وَرُسُلِهِۦ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّن رُّسُلِهِۦ ۚ وَقَالُوا۟ سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ۖ غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ
لَا يُكَلِّفُ ٱللَّهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ۚ لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا ٱكْتَسَبَتْ ۗ رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَآ إِن نَّسِينَآ أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَآ إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُۥ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِۦ ۖ وَٱعْفُ عَنَّا وَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَٱرْحَمْنَآ ۚ أَنتَ مَوْلَىٰنَا فَٱنصُرْنَا عَلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْكَٰفِرِينَ
items.en: The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and [so have] the believers. All of them have believed in Allah and His angels and His books and His messengers, [saying], "We make no distinction between any of His messengers." And they say, "We hear and we obey. [We seek] Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the [final] destination." Allah does not charge a soul except [with that within] its capacity. It will have [the consequence of] what [good] it has gained, and it will bear [the consequence of] what [evil] it has earned. "Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we have forgotten or erred. Our Lord, and lay not upon us a burden like that which You laid upon those before us. Our Lord, and burden us not with that which we have no ability to bear. And pardon us; and forgive us; and have mercy upon us. You are our protector, so give us victory over the disbelieving people."
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said: whoever recites the last two verses of al-Baqarah at night, they will suffice him.

items.title: Lying down in Your Name
items.arabic: بِاسْمِكَ رَبِّي وَضَعْتُ جَنْبِي، وَبِكَ أَرْفَعُهُ، فَإِن أَمْسَكْتَ نَفْسِي فارْحَمْهَا، وَإِنْ أَرْسَلْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا بِمَا تَحْفَظُ بِهِ عِبَادَكَ الصَّالِحِينَ
items.translit: Bismika Rabbī waḍa`tu janbī, wa bika arfa`uh, fa in amsakta nafsī farḥamhā, wa in arsaltahā faḥfaẓhā bimā taḥfaẓu bihi `ibādakaṣ-ṣāliḥīn.
items.en: With Your Name my Lord, I lay myself down; and with Your Name I rise. And if You take my soul, have mercy on it, and if You send it back then protect it as You protect Your righteous slaves.

items.title: Entrusting the soul that He made
items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ خَلَقْتَ نَفْسِي وَأَنْتَ تَوَفَّاهَا، لَكَ مَمَاتُهَا وَمَحْياهَا، إِنْ أَحْيَيْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا، وَإِنْ أَمَتَّهَا فَاغْفِرْ لَهَا. اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ العَافِيَةَ
items.translit: Allāhumma innaka khalaqta nafsī wa anta tawaffāhā, laka mamātuhā wa maḥyāhā, in aḥyaytahā faḥfaẓhā, wa in amattahā faghfir lahā. Allāhumma innī as'aluka ‘l-`āfiyah.
items.en: O Allah, You have created my soul and You take it back. Unto You is its death and its life. If You give it life then protect it, and if You cause it to die then forgive it. O Allah, I ask You for strength.

items.title: Refuge from the punishment of the Day
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ قِنـي عَذابَـكَ يَـوْمَ تَبْـعَثُ عِبـادَك
items.translit: Allāhumma qinī `adhābaka yawma tab`athu `ibādak.
items.en: O Allah, save me from Your punishment on the Day that You resurrect Your slaves.

items.title: In Your Name I die and I live
items.arabic: بِاسْـمِكَ اللّهُـمَّ أَمـوتُ وَأَحْـيا
items.translit: Bismika Allāhumma amūtu wa aḥyā.
items.en: In Your Name, O Allah, I die and I live.

items.title: The tasbih of Fatimah (33, 33, 34)
items.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
items.translit: Subḥānallāh, Wa ‘l-ḥamdu lillāh, Wallāhu Akbar.
items.en: Glory is to Allah (thirty-three times in Arabic), Praise is to Allah (thirty-three times), Allah is the Most Great (thirty-four times)
items.note: SubhanAllah thirty-three, Alhamdulillah thirty-three, Allahu Akbar thirty-four. The Prophet ﷺ taught this to Fatimah instead of a servant, and said it is better for them than a helper.

items.title: Lord of the seven heavens
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ رَبَّ السّمـواتِ السَّبْـعِ وَرَبَّ العَـرْشِ العَظـيم رَبَّنـا وَرَبَّ كُـلِّ شَـيء فالِـقَ الحَـبِّ وَالنَّـوى وَمُـنَزِّلَ التَّـوْراةِ وَالإنْجـيل والفُـرْقان أَعـوذُ بِـكَ مِن شَـرِّ كُـلِّ شَـيءٍ أَنْـتَ آخِـذٌ بِنـاصِـيَتِه اللّهُـمَّ أَنْـتَ الأوَّلُ فَلَـيسَ قَبْـلَكَ شَيء وَأَنْـتَ الآخِـرُ فَلَـيسَ بَعْـدَكَ شَيء وَأَنْـتَ الظّـاهِـرُ فَلَـيْسَ فَـوْقَـكَ شَيء وَأَنْـتَ الْبـاطِـنُ فَلَـيْسَ دونَـكَ شَيء اقـْضِ عَنّـا الـدَّيْـنَ وَأَغْـنِنـا مِنَ الفَـقْر
items.translit: Allāhumma Rabbas-samāwātis-sab`i wa Rabba ‘l-`Arshi ‘l-`Aẓīm, Rabbanā wa Rabba kulli shay', fāliqa ‘l-ḥabbi wan-nawā, wa munzilat-Tawrāti wal-'Injīli, wal-Furqān, a`ūdhu bika min sharri kulli shay'in anta ākhidhun bināṣiyatih. Allāhumma anta ‘l-awwalu falaysa qablaka shay', wa antal-ākhiru falaysa ba`daka shay', wa antaẓ-ẓāhiru falaysa fawqaka shay', wa antal-bāṭinu falaysa dūnaka shay', iqḍi `annad-dayn, wa aghninā mina ‘l-faqr.
items.en: O Allah! Lord of the seven heavens and Lord of the Magnificent Throne. Our Lord and the Lord of everything. Splitter of the grain and the date-stone, Revealer of the Torah and the Injeel and the Furqan (the Qur'an), I seek refuge in You from the evil of everything that You shall seize by the forelock. O Allah You are the First and nothing has come before you, and You are the Last, and nothing may come after You. You are the Most High, nothing is above You and You are the Most Near, and nothing is nearer than You. Remove our debts from us and enrich us against poverty.

items.title: Praise for being fed and sheltered
items.arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا، وَكَفَانَا، وَآوَانَا، فَكَمْ مِمَّنْ لاَ كَافِيَ لَهُ وَلاَ مُؤْوِيَ
items.translit: Alḥamdu lillāhil-lathī 'aṭ`amanā wa saqānā, wa kafānā, wa 'āwānā, fakam mimman lā kāfiya lahu wa lā mu'wī.
items.en: Praise is to Allah Who has provided us with food and with drink, sufficed us and gave us an abode, for how many are there with no provision and no home.

items.title: Knower of the unseen, refuge from my own evil
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ عالِـمَ الغَـيْبِ وَالشّـهادَةِ فاطِـرَ السّماواتِ وَالأرْضِ رَبَّ كـلِّ شَـيءٍ وَمَليـكَه أَشْهَـدُ أَنْ لا إِلـهَ إِلاّ أَنْت أَعـوذُ بِكَ مِن شَـرِّ نَفْسـي وَمِن شَـرِّ الشَّيْـطانِ وَشِـرْكِه وَأَنْ أَقْتَـرِفَ عَلـى نَفْسـي سوءاً أَوْ أَجُـرَّهُ إِلـى مُسْـلِم
items.translit: Allāhumma `ālima ‘l-ghaybi wash-shahādah, fātiras-samāwāti wa ‘l-ardh, Rabba kulli shay'in wa malīkah, ash-hadu an lā ilāha illā ant, a`ūdhu bika min sharri nafsī, wa min sharrish-shayṭāni wa shirkih, wa an aqtarifa `alā nafsī sū'an, aw ajurrahu ilā Muslim.
items.en: O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the evident, Maker of the heavens and the earth, Lord of everything and its Master, I bear witness that there is none worthy of worship but You. I seek refuge in You from the evil of my soul, and from the evil of Satan and his helpers. (I seek refuge in You) from bringing evil upon my soul and from harming any Muslim.

items.title: Recite al-Mulk and as-Sajdah
items.arabic: يقرأ ) السجدة، و(تبارك الذي بيده الملك)
items.en: Recite Surah (As-Sajdah) and Surah (Al-Mulk) in Arabic.

items.title: The last words before sleep
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ أَسْـلَمْتُ نَفْـسي إِلَـيْكَ، وَفَوَّضْـتُ أَمْـري إِلَـيْكَ، وَوَجَّـهْتُ وَجْـهي إِلَـيْكَ، وَأَلْـجَـاْتُ ظَهـري إِلَـيْكَ، رَغْبَـةً وَرَهْـبَةً إِلَـيْكَ، لا مَلْجَـأَ وَلا مَنْـجـا مِنْـكَ إِلاّ إِلَـيْكَ، آمَنْـتُ بِكِتـابِكَ الّـذي أَنْزَلْـتَ وَبِنَبِـيِّـكَ الّـذي أَرْسَلْـت
items.translit: Allāhumma aslamtu nafsī ilayk, wa fawwaḍtu amrī ilayk, wa wajjahtu wajhī ilayk, wa alja'tu ẓahrī ilayk, raghbatan wa rahbatan ilayk, lā malja'a wa la manjā minka illā ilayk, āmantu bikitābika ‘l-ladhī anzalt, wa bi-nabiyyika ‘l-ladhī arsalt.
items.en: O Allah, I submit myself to You, entrust my affairs to You, turn my face to You, and lay myself down depending upon You, hoping in You and fearing You. There is no refuge, and no escape, except to You. I believe in Your Book (the Qur'an) that You revealed, and the Prophet whom You sent.
items.note: Lie on your right side and make these the last words you say. The Prophet ﷺ said that whoever dies that night dies upon the fitrah, the natural way of Islam.

gatherings.name: Gatherings
gatherings.epithet: What to say while you sit, and the words that seal it
gatherings.title: Du'as for Gatherings: what to say in a sitting and the expiation that seals it, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
gatherings.meta_description: Authentic du'as for any gathering: the seeking of forgiveness the Prophet ﷺ repeated as he sat, and kaffarat al-majlis, the expiation said before you rise.
gatherings.keywords: kaffarat al-majlis, dua for gatherings, expiation of the gathering, dua before leaving a sitting, dua in a majlis, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Every gathering leaves a residue. A sitting that begins well can drift into idle talk, a careless word, a laugh at someone's expense, and we rarely notice the moment it slips. The Prophet ﷺ knew this about human company, and he gave us two gifts for it: words to keep the heart soft while we sit, and words to wipe the slate clean before we stand.

These are the adhkar of the majlis. Say the first to keep returning to Allah mid-conversation, and the second as you rise, so that whatever slipped in is forgiven and the gathering is sealed in your favour.
intro: The first is what the Prophet ﷺ would quietly repeat as he sat, seeking forgiveness again and again in a single sitting. The second is the kaffarat al-majlis, the expiation of the gathering, said right before you leave.
Both are short. Say them in Arabic where you can, lean on the transliteration while you learn, and make the closing words a habit you reach for as you stand to go.
closing: There is a mercy in being given a way to undo what a gathering may have spoiled. The Prophet ﷺ said that whoever sits in a gathering with much idle talk and then says the words of expiation before rising will have it forgiven. So nothing has to be carried out of the room with you.
O Allah, glory and praise are Yours. We bear witness that there is none worthy of worship but You. Forgive whatever we let slip as we sat, and let us rise lighter than we came.

faq.q: What is kaffarat al-majlis?
faq.a: It is the expiation of the gathering, a short du'a the Prophet ﷺ taught us to say before leaving any sitting. He said that whoever says it will be forgiven whatever slipped into the gathering, such as idle or careless talk. It seals the sitting on a clean note.

faq.q: When exactly do I say it?
faq.a: Right at the end, as you are about to get up and leave the gathering. Aisha reported that the Prophet ﷺ did not sit in a gathering, recite Qur'an, or pray without sealing it with these words.

faq.q: Is there something to say during the gathering too?
faq.a: Yes. Ibn Umar reported that the Prophet ﷺ would seek forgiveness many times in a single sitting, asking his Lord to forgive him and turn to him in mercy. It keeps the heart returning to Allah even in the middle of company.

email.subject: The words that wipe a gathering clean
email.alt_subject: What the Prophet ﷺ said before leaving any sitting
email.preview: Two du'as for any gathering: the istighfar said while you sit, and the expiation said before you rise.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

Every gathering leaves a residue. A sitting that begins well can drift into idle talk or a careless word, and we rarely notice the moment it slips. The Prophet ﷺ knew this about human company, and he gave us two gifts for it: words to keep the heart soft while we sit, and words to wipe the slate clean before we stand.

The first is the seeking of forgiveness he would quietly repeat as he sat. The second is kaffarat al-majlis, the expiation of the gathering, said right before you leave. He taught that whoever says it will be forgiven whatever slipped into the sitting. Both come here with their Arabic, a transliteration, the meaning, and the source.

There is a real mercy in being given a way to undo what a gathering may have spoiled, so that nothing has to be carried out of the room with you. Make the closing words a habit you reach for as you stand to go.

The full collection, formatted to read or recite, is here:

social.hook:
Every gathering leaves a residue: a careless word, a laugh at someone's expense.

The Prophet ﷺ gave us two lines to wipe it clean.
social.caption:
The du'as of the gathering, what to say while you sit and the words that seal it before you rise.

The Prophet ﷺ would quietly seek forgiveness again and again as he sat. And before leaving any sitting he taught the kaffarat al-majlis, the expiation that forgives whatever idle talk slipped in.

Both are short. Make the closing one a habit you reach for as you stand to go, so nothing has to be carried out of the room with you.

Save this, and read the full collection with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/gatherings

gatherings.source_note: Prophetic adhkar transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com; al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih, jayyid/reliable as hasan. Recitation audio: the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com) for the adhkar. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com; framing is Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

items.title: Seeking forgiveness as you sit
items.arabic: عن ابن عمر قال : كان يعد لرسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم في المجلس الواحد مائة مرة من قبل أن يقوم "رب اغفر لي وتب علي إنك أنت التواب الغفور"
items.translit: Ibn Umar (ra) said:
items.en: My Lord, forgive me and accept my repentance; indeed You are the Ever-Relenting, the Most Forgiving.
items.note: Ibn Umar reported that the Prophet ﷺ would repeat this many times in a single sitting, asking his Lord to forgive him and turn to him in mercy. It keeps the heart returning to Allah even mid-conversation.

items.title: Kaffarat al-majlis: the expiation of the gathering
items.arabic: سُبْحـانَكَ اللّهُـمَّ وَبِحَمدِك، أَشْهَـدُ أَنْ لا إِلهَ إِلاّ أَنْتَ أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتوبُ إِلَـيْك
items.translit: Subhānaka Allāhumma wa biḥamdik, ash-hadu an lā ilāha illā ant, astaghfiruka wa atību ilayk.
items.en: Glory is to You, O Allah, and praise is to You. I bear witness that there is none worthy of worship but You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You.
items.note: The words said before you rise. Aisha reported the Prophet ﷺ sealed every sitting, recitation and prayer with them, and he taught that whoever says them is forgiven whatever idle talk slipped into the gathering.

hajj-umrah.name: Hajj & Umrah
hajj-umrah.epithet: The adhkar of the pilgrim through the rites
hajj-umrah.title: Hajj & Umrah Du'as: the adhkar of the pilgrim through the rites in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
hajj-umrah.meta_description: The pilgrim's adhkar through the rites of Hajj and Umrah: talbiyah, tawaf, sa'i, Arafah, Muzdalifah and the jamarat, in Arabic, transliteration and English with sources.
hajj-umrah.keywords: Hajj dua, Umrah dua, talbiyah, dua for tawaf, dua for sa'i, dua on Arafah, Muzdalifah dua, jamarat, pilgrim adhkar, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Hajj is a journey the body makes, but it is the tongue that keeps the heart in step. From the moment you enter ihram, the rites move you from one station to the next, and at each one the Prophet ﷺ left words: a call answered, a verse recited, the oneness of Allah declared on a mountain. To know the adhkar is to walk the path the way it was walked.

This is the wird of the pilgrim, following the rites in order: the talbiyah of ihram, the remembrance of tawaf, the climb of Safa and Marwah, the great standing at Arafah, the night at Muzdalifah, and the stoning at the jamarat. Whether you are on your way or longing from afar, let these words walk you through it.
intro: These follow the order of the rites. The talbiyah begins at ihram and is repeated again and again until you stone the first pillar. At the Black Stone during tawaf you say Allahu Akbar. Between the two corners you recite the verse 'Rabbana atina'. On Safa and Marwah you make the remembrance of the sa'i. On Arafah you say the best of all du'a, at Muzdalifah you stand in remembrance facing the qiblah, and at the jamarat you say Allahu Akbar with each pebble.

Recite the Arabic, lean on the transliteration while you learn it, and read the meaning so your heart is in each station with your feet. Between every prescribed word, the door of personal du'a is wide open: ask Allah for whatever you long for.
closing: The whole of Hajj is one long answer to a call. 'Labbayk Allahumma labbayk', here I am, again and again, from the first step in ihram to the last pebble thrown. These adhkar are how the pilgrim says, at every station, that he came because Allah called and he wants nothing but Him. Learn them, and the journey becomes what it was always meant to be: a body in motion and a heart that never stops returning.
O Allah, write our names among the pilgrims to Your House, accept from us as You accepted from those before us, and let us answer Your call with a heart that means every word of it.

faq.q: What is the talbiyah and when do I say it?
faq.a: The talbiyah is 'Labbayk Allahumma labbayk', the pilgrim's answer to Allah's call: 'Here I am at Your service.' You begin it the moment you enter ihram and repeat it abundantly, raising your voice with it, until you stone the first pillar (Jamratul-Aqabah) on the day of Eid.

faq.q: What do you say during tawaf around the Ka'bah?
faq.a: There is no fixed du'a for the whole of tawaf; you may ask Allah for anything. At the Black Stone you say Allahu Akbar, and between the Yemeni corner and the Black Stone you recite the verse 'Our Lord, grant us good in this world and good in the next, and save us from the punishment of the Fire'.

faq.q: What is the best du'a to say on the Day of Arafah?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ said the best du'a is the du'a of the Day of Arafah, and the best thing he and the prophets before him said was the declaration of Allah's oneness. Arafah is the heart of Hajj; spend it asking, facing the qiblah, with raised hands.

email.subject: The adhkar of the pilgrim, station by station
email.alt_subject: Walking the rites the way they were walked
email.preview: Talbiyah, tawaf, sa'i, Arafah, Muzdalifah and the jamarat, with Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
email.body: Assalamu alaikum, Hajj is a journey the body makes, but it is the tongue that keeps the heart in step. From the moment you enter ihram, the rites move you from one station to the next, and at each one the Prophet ﷺ left words: a call answered, a verse recited, the oneness of Allah declared on a mountain. We have gathered the wird of the pilgrim, following the rites in order, each with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source. The talbiyah of ihram, the remembrance of tawaf, the climb of Safa and Marwah, the great standing at Arafah, the night at Muzdalifah, and the stoning at the jamarat. The whole of Hajj is one long answer to a call: 'Here I am, O Allah, here I am.' These adhkar are how the pilgrim says, at every station, that he came because Allah called. Whether you are on your way or longing from afar, let them walk you through it. The full collection, formatted to read or recite, is here:

The whole of Hajj is one long answer to a call. Labbayk Allahumma labbayk: here I am, again and again, from the first step to the last pebble. Save this, and read or recite the full collection with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/hajj-umrah #Hajj #Umrah #Talbiyah #DayOfArafah #Tawaf #SunnahDua #Dhikr #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

Qur'anic items are grounded via quran.ai (ar-uthmani-minimal + Saheeh International). Recitation audio: the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com) for the adhkar and Mishary Alafasy (everyayah.com) for the Qur'an. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com; framing is Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

items.title: The talbiyah
items.arabic: لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ والنِّعْمَة لَكَ والمُلْكُ، لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ
items.translit: Labbayk-Allāhumma labbayk, labbayka lā sharīka laka labbayk, inna ‘l-ḥamda, wanni`mata, laka wa ‘l-mulk, lā sharīka lak.
items.en: I am here at Your service, O Allah, I am here at Your service. I am here at Your service, You have no partner, I am here at Your service. Surely the praise, and blessings are Yours, and the dominion. You have no partner.
items.note: The pilgrim's answer to Allah's call, 'Here I am at Your service.' Begun at ihram and repeated abundantly, with the voice raised, until the first pillar is stoned. The sound of Hajj itself.

items.title: At the Black Stone
items.arabic: طَافَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى بَعِيرٍ كُلَّمَا أَتَى الرُّكْنَ أَشَارَ إِلَيْهِ بِشَيْءٍ عِنْدَهُ وَكَبَّرَ
items.en: Allah is the Most Great. (Said while facing the Black Stone at the start of each circuit of tawaf.)
items.note: During tawaf, each time he passed the corner of the Black Stone the Prophet ﷺ would point toward it and say Allahu Akbar. The remembrance that marks every circuit around the House.

items.title: Between the two corners
items.arabic: رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِنَا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِى ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ
items.en: Our Lord, give us in this world [that which is] good and in the Hereafter [that which is] good and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
items.note: Recited during tawaf between the Yemeni corner and the Black Stone: the Qur'anic verse asking for good in this world and the next and refuge from the Fire (al-Baqarah 2:201), the most comprehensive request a heart can make.

items.title: On Safa and Marwah
items.arabic: الْحَدِيثُ. وَفِيهِ: فَفَعَلَ عَلَى الْمَرْوَةِ كَمَا فَعَلَ عَلَى الصَّفَا
items.translit: Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) approached Mount Safa, he would recite:
items.en: Indeed, as-Safa and al-Marwah are among the symbols of Allah; I begin with what Allah began with. (Then, facing the qiblah on the hill: Allahu Akbar, and there is no god but Allah alone, with praise and supplication.)
items.note: The remembrance of the sa'i. Facing the Ka'bah on each hill, the Prophet ﷺ declared Allah's oneness and greatness, affirmed that He kept His promise and aided His servant, then made personal du'a, repeating it three times on each.

items.title: The du'a of Arafah
items.arabic: خير الدعاء دعاء يوم عرفة ، وخيرُ ما قلت أنا والنبيُّون من قبلي : لا إله إلا الله وحدهُ لا شريك لهُ ، لهُ الملكُ ولهُ الحمدُ وهو على كل شيء قدير
items.translit: Lā 'ilāha 'illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lahu, lahul-mulku wa lahul-ḥamdu wa huwa `alā kulli shay'in qadīr.
items.en: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah Alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said the best du'a is the du'a of the Day of Arafah, and the best of what he and the prophets before him said was this declaration of Allah's oneness. The heart of the entire Hajj.

items.title: At Muzdalifah
items.arabic: رَكِبَ صلى الله عليه وسلم الْقَصْوَاءَ حَتَّى أَتَى الْمَشْعَرَ الْحَرَامَ فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ فَدَعَاهُ، وَكَبَّرَهُ، وَهَللَّهُ، وَوَحَّدَهُ فَلَمْ يَزَلْ وَاقِفاً حَتَّى أَسْفَرَ جِدَّاً فَدَفَعَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَطْلُعَ الشَّمسُ
items.en: (At al-Mash'ar al-Haram, facing the qiblah: declaring Allah's greatness, His oneness, and supplicating until daybreak.)
items.note: At al-Mash'ar al-Haram the Prophet ﷺ faced the qiblah and stood in du'a, magnifying Allah and declaring His oneness, until the sky grew light, leaving just before sunrise.

items.title: At the jamarat
items.arabic: يُكَبِّرُ كُلَّمَا رَمَى بِحَصَاةٍ عِنْدَ الْجِمَارِ الثَّلاَثِ، ثُمَّ يَتَقَدَّمُ، ويَقِفُ يَدْعُو مُسْتَقْبِلَ الْقِبلَةِ، رَافِعاً يَدَيْهِ بَعْدَ الْجَمْرَةِ الْأُولَى وَالثَّانِيَةِ. أَمَّا جَمْرَةُ الْعَقَبَةِ فَيَرْمِيهَا وَيُكَبِّرُ عِنْدَ كُلِّ حَصَاةٍ وَيَنْصَرِفُ وَلاَ يَقِفُ عِنْدَهَا
items.en: Allah is the Most Great. (Said with each pebble cast at the jamarat.)
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said Allahu Akbar with each pebble thrown at the three pillars. After the first and second he would step forward, face the qiblah, raise his hands and make du'a; after the last he left without standing.

social.name: For Other People
social.title: Du'as for Other People: what to say to thank, bless, and love others for Allah's sake, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
social.meta_description: Authentic du'as to say to and for other people: thanking with jazak Allahu khayran, blessing a host, loving for Allah's sake, and what to say when praised.
social.keywords: jazakallahu khayran, dua to thank someone, may Allah reward you, love for the sake of Allah, dua when praised, barakallahu fik, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Faith is not only what passes between you and Allah in private. It also shows in how you answer the person in front of you: the one who did you a kindness, the one who told you they love you for Allah's sake, the one who prayed for you, the one who praised you to your face. The sunnah gives you words for each of these too.

These are the adhkar of good companionship. Small phrases that turn an ordinary exchange into worship: a thank-you that becomes a du'a, a blessing returned, love met with love. Learn them and you carry a little of the Prophet's ﷺ courtesy into every room.
intro: Each of these belongs to a moment with another person: when someone does you good, when they pray for you, when they say they love you for Allah, when a loan is repaid, when you are praised, and when you praise someone else. Reach for the one that fits the exchange.

They are short by design, meant to be said out loud and in the moment. Use the Arabic where you can and the transliteration while you learn, and let the meaning shape how you treat the person before you.
closing: Notice how often the reward in these du'as is simply handed back: someone blesses you, and you ask Allah to bless them; someone loves you for His sake, and you ask that He love them. Good is kept moving between believers, and Allah is named as its source every time. This is what it means to be easy, grateful company.
O Allah, make us a mercy to those around us, quick to thank, quick to bless, and quick to love for Your sake alone.

faq.q: What do I say to thank someone?
faq.a: Say Jazak Allahu khayran, may Allah reward you with good. The Prophet ﷺ taught that whoever says this to the one who did him a kindness has given the fullest praise. It is a du'a, not just a thank-you, and it returns the favour to the One who can truly repay it.

faq.q: What do I reply when someone says they love me for the sake of Allah?
faq.a: Reply Ahabbakal-ladhi ahbabtani lah, may He for whose sake you love me, love you. The Prophet ﷺ taught us to meet love for Allah's sake with a du'a that the same love be answered by Allah Himself.

faq.q: What should I say when someone praises me?
faq.a: Turn to Allah with a du'a: O Allah, do not take me to account for what they say, forgive me for what they do not know, and make me better than they imagine. Praise can swell the soul, and this keeps the heart honest before Allah.

faq.q: How do I respond if I am the one praising someone?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ warned against absolute praise. Say what you genuinely see as good, and add that Allah is the one who truly knows them and that you do not declare anyone pure before Him. It keeps praise truthful and free of flattery.

email.subject: What to say when someone does you good
email.alt_subject: The du'as of good companionship
email.preview: Short du'as to say to and for other people: thanking, blessing, and loving for Allah's sake.
email.body: Assalamu alaikum, Faith is not only what passes between you and Allah in private. It also shows in how you answer the person in front of you: the one who did you a kindness, the one who told you they love you for Allah's sake, the one who prayed for you, the one who praised you to your face. The sunnah gives you words for each of these. We have gathered the adhkar of good companionship: the thank-you that becomes a du'a (Jazak Allahu khayran), the blessing you return when someone blesses you, what to say when someone loves you for Allah's sake, the words for a repaid loan, and how to answer when you are praised, or when you are the one offering praise. Each comes with its Arabic, a transliteration, the meaning, and its source. Notice how often the reward is simply handed back: someone blesses you, and you ask Allah to bless them; someone loves you for His sake, and you ask that He love them. Good is kept moving between believers, with Allah named as its source every time. The full collection, formatted to read or recite, is here:

social.hook: Someone does you a kindness. The Prophet ﷺ taught a short reply that turns your thank-you into a du'a.
social.caption: The du'as of good companionship, what to say to and for the people around you. How to thank someone so your gratitude becomes a prayer (Jazak Allahu khayran), how to return a blessing, what to reply when someone says they love you for Allah's sake, and how to keep your heart honest when you are praised. Notice the pattern: good is kept moving between believers, and Allah is named as its source every time. items.title: Returning a greeting or kindness: "And you"
items.arabic: وَلَكَ
items.translit: Walaka
items.en: And you.
items.ref: Ahmad 5/82, and An-Nasa'i in 'Amalul-Yawm wal-Laylah p
items.note: A simple reply that turns a kind word back toward the one who offered it.

items.title: Thanking someone: "May Allah reward you with good"
items.arabic: جَزاكَ اللهُ خَـيْراً
items.translit: Jazākallāhu khayra.
items.en: May Allah reward you with good.
items.note: Jazak Allahu khayran. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever says this to the one who did him good has given him the fullest praise, handing the reward to the only One who can truly repay it.

items.title: When someone says "I love you for Allah's sake"
items.arabic: أَحَبَّـكَ الّذي أَحْبَبْـتَني لَه
items.translit: Aḥabbaka ‘lladhī aḥbabtanī lah.
items.en: May He for Whose sake you love me, love you.
items.note: Ahabbakal-ladhi ahbabtani lah. You meet love for Allah's sake with a du'a that the same love be answered by Allah Himself.

items.title: Blessing the one who feeds or hosts you
items.arabic: بارَكَ اللهُ لَكَ في أَهْلِكَ وَمالِك
items.translit: Bārakallāhu laka fī ahlika wa mālik.
items.en: May Allah bless you in your family and your property.
items.note: Asking Allah to bless your host in their family and their wealth, a du'a the Prophet ﷺ would make for those who served him.

items.title: When a loan is repaid
items.arabic: بارَكَ اللهُ لَكَ في أَهْلِكَ وَمالِك، إِنَّما جَـزاءُ السَّلَفِ الْحَمْدُ والأَداء
items.translit: Bārakallāhu laka fī ahlika wa mālik, innamā jazā'us-salafi ‘l-ḥamdu wa‘l-adā'.
items.en: May Allah bless you in your family and your wealth, surely the reward for a loan is praise and returning (what was borrowed).
items.note: A blessing for the one who lent to you: the reward for a loan is heartfelt praise and returning what was borrowed.

items.title: Replying to a blessing: "And may Allah bless you"
items.arabic: وَفيكَ بارَكَ الله
items.translit: Wafīka bārakallāh.
items.en: And may Allah bless you.
items.note: Wa fika barakallah. When someone says barakallahu fik, you return the blessing to them in kind.

items.title: When you must praise someone to their face
items.arabic: قال صلى الله عليه وسلم: "إِذَا كَانَ أَحَدُكُم مَادِحاً صَاحِبَهُ لاَ مَحَالَةَ فَلْيَقُلْ: أَحْسِبُ فُلاَناً وَاللَّهُ حَسِيبُهُ، وَلاَ أُزَكِّي عَلَى اللَّهِ أَحَداً، أَحْسِبُهُ – إِنْ كَانَ يَعْلَمُ ذَاكَ – كَذَا وَكَذَا"
items.translit: If any of you praises his companion then let him say:
items.en: If any of you praises his companion then let him say: I consider (such and such a person), and Allah is his Assessor, (meaning: and I cannot claim anyone to be pious before Allah) if you know of this (good character trait in the person) to be such and such (saying what he thinks is praiseworthy in that person).
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught that if you must praise a companion, say only what you genuinely see and add that Allah is his true assessor, for you cannot declare anyone pure before Allah.

items.title: When you are the one being praised
items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لاَ تُؤَاخِذْنِي بِمَا يَقُولُونَ وَاغْفِرْ لِي مَا لاَ يَعْلَمُونَ [وَاجْعَلْنِي خَيْرًا مِمَّا يَظُّنُّونَ]
items.translit: Allāhumma lā tu'ākhidhnī bimā yaqūlūn, waghfir lī mā lā ya`lamūn [waj`alnī khayran mimmā yaẓunnūn].
items.en: O Allah, do not call me to account for what they say and forgive me for what they have no knowledge of [and make me better than they imagine].
items.note: Turn the praise into a du'a: ask Allah not to hold you to account for what they say, to forgive what they do not know, and to make you better than they imagine.

masjid-adhan.name: The Masjid & the Adhan
masjid-adhan.epithet: Answering the call to the house of Allah
masjid-adhan.title: Masjid & Adhan Adhkar: the du'as for the mosque and the call to prayer, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
masjid-adhan.meta_description: The du'as for walking to the masjid, entering and leaving it, and what to say with the adhan, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source.
masjid-adhan.keywords: masjid dua, dua entering mosque, dua leaving mosque, adhan dua, dua after adhan, wasilah dua, what to say after adhan, repeat after muadhdhin, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Five times a day the call goes out, and a believer answers it. Between hearing the adhan and standing in the rows, there is a path of words the Prophet ﷺ walked: light asked for on the way, mercy sought at the door, and a quiet du'a that the heavens do not turn back.
These are the adhkar of the masjid and the call to prayer. They are how you answer the summons with your whole self, not just your feet.
intro: When the adhan is called, repeat each phrase after the muadhdhin, except at 'come to prayer' and 'come to success', where you say the words of seeking help from Allah. After the adhan, send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ and ask for his station of intercession, then make your own du'a, for the time between the adhan and the iqamah is not turned away.
Say the entering du'a as you step into the masjid, leading with your right foot, and the leaving du'a as you step out, leading with your left.
closing: Every step toward the masjid is written for you, one raising your rank and one wiping away a sin. To walk that path with these words on your tongue is to arrive already in worship, your heart turned before your body bows.
O Allah, open for us the doors of Your mercy, grant our Prophet ﷺ the station You promised him, and make us of those whose feet carry them to Your house in light.

faq.q: What do I say while the adhan is being called?
faq.a: Repeat each phrase quietly after the muadhdhin. The one exception is at 'come to prayer' and 'come to success', where instead you say La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah (there is no might nor power except by Allah).

faq.q: What should I say after the adhan finishes?
faq.a: Send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ, then ask Allah to grant him al-wasilah and the honoured station. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever asks this for him will be granted his intercession on the Day of Resurrection. Then make your own du'a, as the moment between adhan and iqamah is answered.

faq.q: Which foot do I lead with entering and leaving the masjid?
faq.a: Enter leading with your right foot and say the du'a asking Allah to open the doors of His mercy. Leave with your left foot and ask Allah for His favour, since for honoured places the right is given precedence on the way in.

faq.q: Why is the du'a between the adhan and iqamah special?
faq.a: Because the Prophet ﷺ told us that supplication made in that window is not rejected. It is a short, easily missed opening in the day where the door of response is held open, so do not let it pass without asking.

email.subject: Answering the call, all the way to the rows
email.alt_subject: What to say with the adhan and at the masjid
email.preview: The du'as for the masjid and the call to prayer, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

Five times a day the call goes out, and a believer answers it. Between hearing the adhan and standing in the rows, there is a path of words the Prophet ﷺ walked: light asked for on the way, mercy sought at the door, and a quiet du'a that the heavens do not turn back.

We have gathered the adhkar of the masjid and the call to prayer, each with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source. Repeat after the muadhdhin, ask for the Prophet's ﷺ station after the call, and use the moment before the iqamah, the time when du'a is not refused.

Every step to the masjid is written for you. Walk it with these words and arrive already in worship:

social.hook:
There is a moment between the adhan and the iqamah when du'a is not refused.

Most of us let it pass in silence.
social.caption:
The adhkar of the masjid and the call to prayer, how you answer the summons with your whole self, not just your feet.

Repeat after the muadhdhin. Send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ and ask for his station of intercession. Then make your own du'a before the iqamah, the window the Prophet ﷺ said is never turned away. Enter with your right foot asking for mercy, leave with your left asking for His favour.

items.title: Light on the way to the masjid
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ اجْعَـلْ فِي قَلْبـي نُوراً ، وَفي لِسَـانِي نُوراً، وَفِي سَمْعِي نُوراً, وَفِي بَصَرِيِ نُوراً, وَمِنْ فََوْقِي نُوراً , وَ مِنْ تَحْتِي نُوراً, وَ عَنْ يَمِينيِ نُوراَ, وعَنْ شِمَالِي نُوراً, وَمْن أَماَمِي نُوراً, وَمِنْ خَلْفيِ نُوراَ, واجْعَلْ فِي نَفْسِي نُوراً, وأَعْظِمْ لِي نُوراً, وَعظِّمْ لِي نُوراً, وَاجْعَلْ لِي نُوراً, واجْعَلنِي نُوراً, أللَّهُمَّ أَعْطِنِي نُوراً, واجْعَلْ فِي عَصَبِي نُوراً, وَفِي لَحْمِي نُوراً, وَفِي دَمِي نُوراً وَفِي شَعْرِي نُوراً, وفِي بَشَرِي نُوراً أَللَّهُمَّ اجِعَلْ لِي نُوراً فِي قّبْرِي وَ نُوراَ فِي عِظاَمِي وَزِدْنِي نُوراً, وَزِدْنِي نُوراَ , وَزِدْنِي نُوراً وَهَبْ لِي نُوراً عَلَى نُوراً
items.translit: Allāhummaj'al fī qalbī nūran, wa fī lisānī nūran, wa fī sam`ī nūran, wa fī baṣarī nūran, wa min fawqī nūran, wa min taḥtī nūran, wa `an yamīnī nūran, wa `an shimālī nūran, wa min 'amāmī nūran, wa min khalfī nūran, waj`al fī nafsī nūran, wa 'a`ẓim lī nūran, wa `ẓẓim lī nūran, waj`allī nūran, waj`alnī nūran, Allāhumma 'a`tinī nūran, waj'al fī `aṣabī nūran, wa fī laḥmī nūran, wa fī damī nūran, wa fī sha`rī nūran, wa fī basharī nūran. [Allāhummaj`allī nūran fī qabrī wa nūran fī `iẓāmī.] [Wa zidnī nūran, wa zidnī nūran, wa zidnī nūran.] [Wa hab lī nūran `alā nūr.]
items.en: O Allah, place light in my heart, and on my tongue light, and in my ears light and in my sight light, and above me light, and below me light, and to my right light, and to my left light, and before me light and behind me light. Place in my soul light. Magnify for me light, and amplify for me light. Make for me light and make me a light. O Allah, grant me light, and place light in my nerves, and in my body light and in my blood light and in my hair light and in my skin light. [O Allah, make for me a light in my grave... and a light in my bones.] (At-Tirmidhi 5/483 (Hadith no. 3419).) [Increase me in light, increase me in light, increase me in light.] (Al-Bukhari in Al-'Adab Al-Mufrad (Hadith no. 695), p. 258. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Al-'Adab Al-Mufrad(no. 536).) [Grant me light upon light.] (Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/118.)
items.note: The du'a the Prophet ﷺ made setting out for the prayer, asking for light in his heart, his senses, and every direction around him. A beautiful supplication to carry on the walk to Fajr.

items.title: Entering the masjid
items.arabic: أَعوذُ باللهِ العَظيـم وَبِوَجْهِـهِ الكَرِيـم وَسُلْطـانِه القَديـم مِنَ الشّيْـطانِ الرَّجـيم، [ بِسْـمِ الله، وَالصَّلاةُ وَالسَّلامُ عَلى رَسولِ الله]، اللّهُـمَّ افْتَـحْ لي أَبْوابَ رَحْمَتـِك
items.translit: 'A`ūdhu billāhi l-`aẓīm, wa bi-wajhihil-karīm, wa sultānihil-qadīm, min ash-shaytānir-rajīm. [Bismillāhi, wassalātu wassalāmu `alā rasūlillāhi.] Allāhummaftaḥ lī 'abwāba raḥmatik.
items.en: I seek refuge in Almighty Allah, By His Noble Face, By His primordial power, From Satan the outcast. [In the Name of Allah, and blessings and peace be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, open before me the doors of Your mercy.
items.ref: Abu Dawud 1/126, see also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami'As-Saghir 1/528
items.note: Step in with your right foot and ask Allah to open the doors of His mercy.

items.title: Leaving the masjid
items.arabic: بِسمِ الله وَالصّلاةُ وَالسّلامُ عَلى رَسولِ الله، اللّهُـمَّ إِنّـي أَسْأَلُكَ مِـنْ فَضْـلِك، اللّهُـمَّ اعصِمْنـي مِنَ الشَّيْـطانِ الرَّجـيم
items.translit: Bismillāhi waṣṣalātu wassalāmu 'alā Rasūlillāhi, Allāhumma 'innī 'as'aluka min faḍlika, Allāhumma`ṣimnī min ash-shaytānir-rajīm.
items.en: In the Name of Allah, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, I ask for Your favor, O Allah, protect me from Satan the outcast.
items.note: Step out with your left foot and ask Allah for His favour, and for protection from the devil as you return to the world.

items.title: Repeating after the muadhdhin
items.arabic: يقول مثل ما يقول المؤذن إلا في حي على الصلاة ، وحي على الفلاح فيقول لا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله
items.translit: Lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata 'illā billāh.
items.en: There is no might nor power except by Allah. (You repeat what the muezzin says, except after 'Come to prayer, come to success,' when you say this instead.)
items.note: Echo each phrase of the adhan, except at 'come to prayer' and 'come to success', where you say there is no might nor power except by Allah.

items.title: After the two testimonies
items.arabic: وَأَنا أَشْـهَدُ أَنْ لا إِلـهَ إِلاّ اللهُ وَحْـدَهُ لا شَـريكَ لَـه ، وَأَنَّ محَمّـداً عَبْـدُهُ وَرَسـولُه، رَضيـتُ بِاللهِ رَبَّاً ، وَبِمُحَمَّـدٍ رَسـولاً وَبِالإِسْلامِ دينَـاً
items.translit: Wa 'anā 'ash-hadu 'an lā 'ilāha 'illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lahu wa 'anna Muḥammadan 'abduhu wa rasūluhu, raḍītu billāhi rabban, wa bi Muḥammadin rasūlan wa bi 'l-islāmi dīnan.
items.en: I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner, and that Muhammad is His slave and His Messenger. I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Muhammad as my Messenger and with Islam as my religion. [To be recited in Arabic after the mu'adh-dhin's tashahhud or the words of affirmation of Faith]
items.note: Said in Arabic after the muadhdhin's shahadah: a declaration of being pleased with Allah as Lord, Muhammad ﷺ as Messenger, and Islam as religion. The Prophet ﷺ said the one who says it will have his sins forgiven.

items.title: Blessings on the Prophet ﷺ
items.arabic: يُصَلِّي عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بَعْدَ فَرَاغِهِ مِنْ إِجَابَةِ الْمُؤَذِّنِ
items.en: After replying to the call of Mu'adh-dhin. you should recite in Arabic Allah's blessings on the Prophet (ﷺ).
items.note: When the muadhdhin finishes, send your peace and blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ before making your own du'a.

items.title: Asking for the Prophet's ﷺ station
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ رَبَّ هَذِهِ الدّعْـوَةِ التّـامَّة وَالصّلاةِ القَـائِمَة آتِ محَـمَّداً الوَسيـلةَ وَالْفَضـيلَة وَابْعَـثْه مَقـامـاً مَحـموداً الَّذي وَعَـدْتَه [إِنَّـكَ لا تُـخْلِفُ الميـعاد]
items.translit: Allāhumma rabba hādhihi 'd-da'wati 't-tāmmah waṣ-ṣalāti 'l-qā'imah, 'āti Muhammadani 'l-wasīlata walfaḍīlata, wab 'ath-hu maqāma 'm-mahmūdani 'l-ladhī wa`adtahu, ['innaka lā tukhliful-mī'ād]
items.en: O Allah, Lord of this perfect call and established prayer. Grant Muhammad the intercession and favor, and raise him to the honored station You have promised him, [verily You do not neglect promises].
items.ref: Al-Bukhari 1/152, and the addition between brackets is from Al-Bayhaqi 1/410 with a good (Hasan) chain of narration
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said whoever asks Allah to grant him al-wasilah and the honoured station after the adhan will be granted his intercession on the Day of Resurrection.

items.title: Du'a between the adhan and the iqamah
items.arabic: يَدْعُو لِنَفسِهِ بَيْنَ الْأَذَانِ وَالْإِقَامَةِ فَإِنَّ الدُّعَاءَ حِينَئِذٍ لاَ يُرَدُّ
items.en: Between the call to prayer and the 'Iqamah, you should supplicate Allah for yourself. Invocation during this time is not rejected.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught that supplication in this window is not rejected. Use it: ask for what weighs on your heart before the prayer begins.

istikharah.name: The Prayer of Istikharah
istikharah.epithet: Asking Allah to choose for you when you cannot see which way is best
istikharah.title: Du'a al-Istikharah: the prayer for seeking Allah's choice, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
istikharah.meta_description: How to pray istikharah, the prayer for seeking Allah's guidance in a decision: the two rak'ahs and the full du'a in Arabic, transliteration and English. Via Buruja.
istikharah.keywords: salat al-istikharah, istikharah prayer, dua istikharah, how to pray istikhara, prayer for guidance, seeking Allah's choice, making a decision in Islam, istikhara dua, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: There are decisions you cannot reason your way through. The marriage, the move, the job, the matter where every path looks reasonable and you simply cannot see which one is good for you. For exactly these, the Prophet ﷺ gave us istikharah: a way of stepping back and asking the One who knows the unseen to choose on our behalf.

Jabir said the Prophet ﷺ taught them istikharah in all their affairs as he taught them a chapter of the Qur'an. It is not a last resort or a sign of weak resolve. It is the believer's first move when a real choice is in front of them, and no one who sought the counsel of their Creator was ever left to regret it.
intro: Istikharah is a short prayer followed by a du'a. First, when you have a matter you genuinely intend to pursue, pray two rak'ahs of voluntary prayer (not one of the obligatory prayers). Then, while still in that state of turning to Allah, raise your hands and recite the du'a below.

Where the du'a reaches the words 'this matter', name your decision aloud or in your heart. You are asking Allah, if it is good for you in your religion, your life and your end, to make it happen and make it easy and bless it for you; and if it is bad for you, to turn it away and turn you away from it, and to decree for you what is good wherever it lies, then make you content with it.

Then act. Istikharah does not usually arrive as a dream or a feeling; more often Allah opens or closes the way through the unfolding of events and the inclination He places in a heart that has sincerely handed the choice over. Take your decision and trust that He has answered. You can repeat the istikharah as often as you need while a matter is undecided.
closing: The quiet relief of istikharah is this: you no longer have to be certain you chose right. You have asked the One who sees every outcome to steer you toward the good and away from the harm, and you have meant it. Whatever comes after is His choosing, not merely yours, and there is rest in that.

O Allah, You know and we do not, and You are the Knower of all that is hidden. Choose for us what is good in our religion, our lives and our end, make us content with what You decree, and let us never regret having placed our affairs in Your hands.

faq.q: How exactly do I pray istikharah?
faq.a: When you have a matter you intend to go ahead with, pray two rak'ahs of voluntary (nafl) prayer, outside of the obligatory prayers. After the prayer, raise your hands and recite the du'a of istikharah, naming your specific decision where the du'a refers to 'this matter'. Then make your decision and proceed, trusting that Allah has guided you.

faq.q: Will I see a dream or get a clear sign after istikharah?
faq.a: Not usually. Istikharah is not primarily about dreams or feelings. After praying it, you go ahead with what seems best, and Allah guides you through how matters then unfold, easing the way to what is good for you and turning you from what is not. The answer is in your life, not necessarily in your sleep.

faq.q: Can I pray istikharah more than once for the same decision?
faq.a: Yes. If a matter is still undecided or you are still uncertain, there is no harm in repeating the istikharah. Keep asking Allah for guidance until the way becomes clear and your heart settles, and remember to seek the counsel of trustworthy, sincere people alongside it, as the Qur'an encourages consultation in our affairs.

email.subject: When you cannot see which way is best
email.alt_subject: How to pray istikharah
email.preview: The prayer for seeking Allah's choice in a decision: the two rak'ahs and the full du'a.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

There are decisions you cannot reason your way through. The marriage, the move, the job, the matter where every path looks reasonable and you simply cannot see which one is good for you. For exactly these, the Prophet ﷺ gave us istikharah: a way of asking the One who knows the unseen to choose on our behalf.

It is simpler than many imagine. When you have a matter you genuinely intend to pursue, you pray two rak'ahs of voluntary prayer, and then, with your hands raised, you recite the du'a of istikharah, naming your decision where it refers to 'this matter'. You ask Allah, if this is good for you, to bring it about and bless it; and if it is bad for you, to turn it and you away from it, and to decree what is good instead. Then you act, and trust that He has answered, usually not through a dream, but through how matters unfold.

The quiet relief of it is that you no longer have to be certain you chose right. You have handed the choice to the One who sees every outcome, and meant it.

The two rak'ahs, and the full du'a in Arabic, transliteration and meaning, are here:

social.hook:
There are decisions you cannot reason your way through.

The Prophet ﷺ gave us istikharah for exactly these.
social.caption:
How to pray istikharah, the prayer for seeking Allah's choice.

Jabir said the Prophet ﷺ taught them istikharah in all their affairs as he taught them a chapter of the Qur'an. It is not a last resort. It is the believer's first move when a real choice is in front of them.

The how: pray two rak'ahs of voluntary prayer, then raise your hands and recite the du'a, naming your decision. Ask Allah, if it is good for you, to bring it about and bless it; if it is bad, to turn it away and decree what is good instead. Then act, and trust the way He opens or closes.

The quiet relief: you no longer have to be certain you chose right. You have handed it to the One who sees every outcome.

Save this and read the full prayer with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/istikharah

#Istikharah #SalatAlIstikharah #DuaForGuidance #Dhikr #Dua #HisnAlMuslim #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

istikharah.source_note: Prophetic adhkar transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com; al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih, jayyid/reliable as hasan. Recitation audio: the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com) for the adhkar. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com; framing is Buruja's own.
