items.title: The du'a of istikharah
items.arabic: قال جابر بن عبد الله رضي الله عنهما : كان رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ، يُعلمنا الاستخارة في الأمور كلها كما يعلمُنا السورة من القرآن ، يقول : إذا هم أحدكم بالأمر فليركع ركعتين من غير الفريضة ، ثم ليقل: "اللهم إني أستخيرك بعلمك ، وأستقدرك بقدرتك ، وأسألك من فضلك العظيم فإنك تقدِرُ ولا أقدِرُ ، وتعلم ولا أعلم ، وأنت علام الغيوب ، اللهم إن كنت تعلم أن هذا الأمر - يسمي حاجته - خير لي في ديني ومعاشي وعاقبة أمري - أو قال : عاجله وآجله - فاقدره لي ويسره لي ، ثم بارك لي فيه ، وإن كنت تعلم أن هذا الأمر شر لي في ديني ومعاشي وعاقبة أمري - أو قال : عاجله وآجله - فاصرفه عني واصرفني عنه ، واقدر لي الخير حيث كان ، ثم ارضني به " وما ندم من استخار الخالق، وشاور المخلوقين المؤمنين وتثبَّت في أمره، فقد قال سبحانه وَشـاوِرْهُـم في الأمْـرِ فَـإِذا عَـزَمْـتَ فَتَوَكّـلْ عَلـى الله
items.translit: Allāhumma innī astakhīruka bi `ilmik, wa astaqdiruka biqudratik, wa as'aluka min faḍlika ‘l-`Aẓīm, fa'innaka taqdiru wa lā aqdir, wa ta`lamu wa lā a`lam, wa anta `allāmu ‘l-ghuyūb, Allāhumma in kunta ta`lamu anna hādha ‘l-amra - [then mention the thing to be decided] Khayrun lī fī dīnī wa ma`āshī wa `āqibati amrī - [or say] `ājilihi wa ājilih - faqdurhu lī wa yassirhu lī thumma bārik lī fīh, wa in kunta ta`lamu anna hādha ‘l-amra sharrun lī fī dīnī wa ma`āshī wa `āqibati 'amrī - [or say] `ājilihi wa 'ājilihi - faṣrifhu `annī waṣrifnī `anh, waqdur liya ‘l-khayra ḥaythu kān, thumma arḍinī bih.
items.en: "O Allah, I seek the counsel of Your Knowledge, and I seek the help of Your Omnipotence, and I beseech You for Your Magnificent Grace. Surely, You are Capable and I am not. You know and I know not, and You are the Knower of the unseen. O Allah, if You know that this matter [then mention the thing to be decided] is good for me in my religion and in my life and for my welfare in the life to come, - [or say: in this life and the afterlife] - then ordain it for me and make it easy for me, then bless me in it. And if You know that this matter is bad for me in my religion and in my life and for my welfare in the life to come, - [or say: in this life and the afterlife] - then distance it from me, and distance me from it, and ordain for me what is good wherever it may be, and help me to be content with it."
items.note: Recited after the two rak'ahs of istikharah. Where it reaches 'this matter', name your decision to Allah, then ask Him to ordain it and bless it if it is good for you, or turn it away and turn you toward what is better if it is not. Jabir reported that the Prophet ﷺ taught it for all their affairs.

after-salah.name: After Salah
after-salah.epithet: The dhikr that follows every prayer
after-salah.title: After Salah: the adhkar after the obligatory prayer, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
after-salah.meta_description: The adhkar after salah (adhkar ba'd as-salah): istighfar, the tasbih of 33, Ayat al-Kursi and more, each with Arabic, transliteration, meaning, source and count. Qur'an verified via quran.ai.
after-salah.keywords: after salah dua, adhkar after prayer, tasbih 33, Ayat al-Kursi after prayer, dhikr after fardh, subhanallah 33 alhamdulillah 33, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: The prayer ends, but the standing before Allah does not have to. The Prophet ﷺ would turn from the prayer and stay a little longer in remembrance, and he taught a set of words for exactly this moment, while the heart is still soft from sujood.
These are the adhkar after the obligatory prayer: forgiveness, the oneness of Allah, the tasbih that outweighs the world, and the protection of Ayat al-Kursi.
intro: Say these after the salam of each obligatory prayer, before you stand and scatter into the day. A few are kept for after Fajr and Maghrib in particular (the ten declarations and the three Quls); the rest follow every fardh.

Take the tasbih slowly: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar, thirty-three each, then sealing the hundred with the declaration of oneness. The Prophet ﷺ said the one who keeps this after every prayer has their sins forgiven though they be like the foam of the sea.
closing: The prayer is the meeting; the adhkar after it are the few moments you do not rush away. The angels are still listening, the heart is still turned. To sit and remember Him here is to let the prayer keep working in you after the salam.
O Allah, You are Peace and from You comes peace. Let us not pray and then forget You until the next prayer, but carry the remembrance of the salam out into the whole day.

faq.q: What do you say after the obligatory prayer?
faq.a: Begin with istighfar three times and 'Allahumma antas-salam wa minkas-salam...', then the declaration of Allah's oneness, the tasbih (SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar thirty-three each, sealed with La ilaha illallah), and Ayat al-Kursi. These are all gathered here with counts.

faq.q: How many times do you say SubhanAllah after prayer?
faq.a: Thirty-three times SubhanAllah, thirty-three Alhamdulillah, and thirty-three Allahu Akbar, then completing the hundred with La ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lah... The Prophet ﷺ attached enormous reward to this.

faq.q: Which adhkar are only after Fajr and Maghrib?
faq.a: The three Quls are recited three times each after Fajr and Maghrib, and the ten declarations of 'La ilaha illallah... ' are also specified for after those two prayers. Ayat al-Kursi and the tasbih follow every obligatory prayer.

email.subject: Don't rush away after the salam
email.alt_subject: The dhikr that outweighs the world
email.preview: The adhkar after every prayer: istighfar, the tasbih of 33, Ayat al-Kursi.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

The prayer ends with the salam, but the standing before Allah does not have to. The Prophet ﷺ would turn from his prayer and stay a little longer in remembrance, while the heart was still soft from sujood, and he taught us words for exactly that moment.

Istighfar three times. The declaration that He alone is God. The tasbih: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar, thirty-three each, sealed with the hundredth. And Ayat al-Kursi, after which, he said, nothing stands between you and Paradise but death. We have gathered them here with Arabic, transliteration, meaning, counts and audio.

The full set is here:

social.hook:
The prayer ends at the salam.

The standing before Allah doesn't have to.
social.caption:
The adhkar after salah.

Don't rush away after the salam. The Prophet ﷺ stayed a little longer: istighfar, the oneness of Allah, the tasbih of thirty-three, and Ayat al-Kursi, after which nothing stands between you and Paradise but death.

The sins of the one who keeps this, he said, are forgiven though they be like the foam of the sea.

Full set with Arabic, transliteration, meaning, counts and audio at buruja.com/adhkar/after-salah

#AfterSalah #DhikrAfterPrayer #Tasbih #AyatAlKursi #Salah #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

after-salah.source_note: The Qur'anic adhkar (Ayat al-Kursi, Al-Baqarah 2:255; and the three Quls, Al-Ikhlas 112, Al-Falaq 113, An-Nas 114) are grounded via quran.ai: Arabic in Uthmani script (ar-uthmani-minimal) and the Saheeh International translation. The prophetic adhkar, their transliterations, references and gradings are transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Fortress of the Muslim) by Sa'eed al-Qahtani, via sunnah.com; al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih, jayyid/reliable noted as hasan. Recitation audio: Qur'anic items by Mishary Rashid Alafasy (everyayah.com); prophetic adhkar from the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com), aligned by dua number. Selection and ordering follow Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com. Reflections are Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

items.title: Forgiveness, then peace
items.arabic: أَسْـتَغْفِرُ الله اللّهُـمَّ أَنْـتَ السَّلامُ وَمِـنْكَ السَّلام تَبارَكْتَ يا ذا الجَـلالِ وَالإِكْـرام
items.translit: Astaghfirullāh
items.en: I seek the forgiveness of Allah (three times). O Allah, You are Peace and from You comes peace. Blessed are You, O Owner of majesty and honor.

items.title: There is no god but Allah, alone
items.arabic: لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وحدَهُ لا شريكَ لهُ لهُ المُـلْكُ ولهُ الحَمْد وهوَ على كلّ شَيءٍ قَدير اللّهُـمَّ لا مانِعَ لِما أَعْطَـيْت وَلا مُعْطِـيَ لِما مَنَـعْت وَلا يَنْفَـعُ ذا الجَـدِّ مِنْـكَ الجَـد
items.translit: Lā 'ilāha 'illallāh, waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu ‘l-mulku wa lahu ‘l-ḥamd, wa huwa `alā kulli shay'in qadīr, Allāhumma lā māni`a limā 'a`tayt, wa lā mu`tiya limā mana`t, wa lā yanfa`u dhal-jaddi minkal-jadd.
items.en: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, He has no partner, His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things. O Allah, there is none who can withhold what You give, and none may give what You have withheld, and the might of the mighty person cannot benefit him against You.

items.title: No power except in Him
items.arabic: لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّه وحدَهُ لا شريكَ لهُ لهُ الملكُ ولهُ الحَمد وهوَ على كلّ شيءٍ قدير لا حَـوْلَ وَلا قـوَّةَ إِلاّ بِاللهِ لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّـه وَلا نَعْـبُـدُ إِلاّ إيّـاه لَهُ النِّعْـمَةُ وَلَهُ الفَضْل وَلَهُ الثَّـناءُ الحَـسَن لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ مخْلِصـينَ لَـهُ الدِّينَ وَلَوْ كَـرِهَ الكـافِرون
items.translit: Lā 'ilāha 'illallāh, waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahul-mulku, wa lahul-ḥamd, wa huwa `alā kulli shay'in qadīr. Lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata 'illā billāh, lā 'ilāha 'illallāh, wa lā na`budu 'illā 'iyyāh, lahun-ni`matu wa lahul-faḍl, wa lahuth-thanā'ul-ḥasan, lā 'ilāha 'illallāh, mukhliṣīna lahud-dīn, wa law karihal-kāfirūn.
items.en: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, He has no partner, His is the dominion and His is the praise and He is Able to do all things. There is no power and no might except by Allah. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah, and we do not worship any other besides Him. His is grace, and His is bounty, and to Him belongs the most excellent praise. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah. (We are) sincere in making our religious devotion to Him, even though the disbelievers may dislike it.

items.title: The tasbih of thirty-three
items.arabic: سُـبْحانَ اللهِ والحَمْـدُ لله واللهُ أكْـبَر لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وَحْـدَهُ لا شريكَ لهُ لهُ الملكُ ولهُ الحَمْد وهُوَ على كُلّ شَيءٍ قَـدير
items.translit: Subḥānallāh, walḥamdu lillāh, wallāhu 'akbar,
items.en: Glory is to Allah, and praise is to Allah, and Allah is the Most Great. (each said thirty-three times) None has the right to be worshiped but Allah alone, He has no partner, His is the dominion and His is the praise and He is Able to do all things.
items.ref: Muslim 1/418, Whoever says this after every prayer will be forgiven his sins even though they be as the foam of the sea
items.note: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar, thirty-three each, then La ilaha illallah to complete the hundred. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever does this after every prayer is forgiven though his sins be like the foam of the sea.

items.title: The three Quls
items.arabic:
قُلْ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ۝ ٱللَّهُ ٱلصَّمَدُ ۝ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ ۝ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُۥ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌۢ
قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلْفَلَقِ ۝ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ ٱلنَّفَّٰثَٰتِ فِى ٱلْعُقَدِ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ
قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مَلِكِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ إِلَٰهِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مِن شَرِّ ٱلْوَسْوَاسِ ٱلْخَنَّاسِ ۝ ٱلَّذِى يُوَسْوِسُ فِى صُدُورِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ
items.en: Say, "He is Allah, [who is] One, Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, Nor is there to Him any equivalent." / Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak From the evil of that which He created And from the evil of darkness when it settles And from the evil of the blowers in knots And from the evil of an envier when he envies." / Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, The Sovereign of mankind, The God of mankind, From the evil of the retreating whisperer - Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind - From among the jinn and mankind."
items.note: Recited after every prayer, and three times each after Fajr and Maghrib.

items.title: Ayat al-Kursi
items.arabic: ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْحَىُّ ٱلْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُۥ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُۥٓ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَىْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَـُٔودُهُۥ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْعَظِيمُ
items.en: Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said that whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after every obligatory prayer, nothing stands between him and entering Paradise except death.

items.title: Ten declarations after Fajr and Maghrib
items.arabic: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
items.translit: Lā ilāha illallāh waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu‘l-mulku wa lahu‘l-ḥamd yuḥyī wa yumīt wa huwa `alā kulli shay'in qadīr.
items.en: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise. He brings life and He causes death, and He is Able to do all things.

items.title: Asking for beneficial knowledge (after Fajr)
items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْماً نافِعاً وَرِزْقاً طَيِّباً وَعَمَلاً مُتَقَبَّلاً بَعْدَ السّلامِ مِنْ صَلاَةِ الفَجْرِ
items.translit: Allāhumma innī as'aluka `ilman nāfi`an, wa rizqan ṭayyiban, wa `amalam-mutaqabbala.
items.en: "O Allah, I ask You for knowledge which is beneficial, and sustenance which is good, and deeds which are acceptable."

protection-iman.name: Protecting Your Faith
protection-iman.epithet: What to do when doubt whispers, and how to drive Shaytan away
protection-iman.title: Du'as Against Doubt and Shaytan: protecting your faith, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
protection-iman.meta_description: What the Prophet ﷺ taught for whispers of doubt about faith and for repelling Shaytan, in Arabic, transliteration and English. From the sunnah, via Buruja.
protection-iman.keywords: dua against waswas, whispers of doubt, doubt in faith, repel Shaytan, dua against shaitan, waswas of faith, doubts about Allah, seeking refuge from Satan, protecting iman, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Sometimes the attack does not come from outside at all. It comes as a whisper: a thought about Allah you would never speak aloud, a question about your own belief that arrives unbidden and leaves you shaken. Many sincere believers carry this in silence, afraid that the whisper itself is proof their faith has failed.

It is not. The Prophet ﷺ was asked about exactly this, and his companions told him they would sooner fall from the sky than utter what crossed their minds. He answered that feeling the whisper and hating it is itself a sign of clear faith. Here is what he taught us to do when it comes.
intro: The guidance here is as much a practice as a recitation. When a doubt about Allah is thrown at you, the Prophet ﷺ taught two things: seek refuge in Allah, and stop. Do not entertain the thought, do not argue with it, do not chase it down. Turn away and affirm your belief, and the whisper loses its grip.

Alongside these specific responses, the everyday adhkar are your steady defence: the morning and evening remembrance, the verses before sleep, and reciting Surah al-Baqarah in the home, which the Prophet ﷺ said Shaytan flees. Whispers feed on an empty, idle heart; they retreat from one kept busy with the remembrance of Allah.
protection-iman.closing_heading: Do not be afraid of the whisper
closing: The most important thing to know is that being troubled by these thoughts is a mercy, not a verdict. A heart that did not care would not be disturbed. Your distress at the whisper is the very evidence of the faith you are afraid you have lost.

O Allah, make our hearts firm upon Your religion, guard us from the whispering of the retreating whisperer, and let our faith be the last thing taken from us. You are the One who holds the heavens from falling; hold our hearts steady upon belief in You.

faq.q: I keep getting evil or doubting thoughts about Allah and my faith. Have I become a disbeliever?
faq.a: No. The companions came to the Prophet ﷺ distressed by exactly these thoughts, saying they would rather fall from the sky than speak them. He told them that feeling this and hating it is 'clear faith.' The whisper is from Shaytan, not from you, and being disturbed by it is a sign your faith is alive, not gone.

faq.q: What should I actually do when a doubt about Allah or Islam is whispered to me?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ taught a clear response: seek refuge in Allah (say A'udhu billah) and then stop. Do not dwell on the thought or try to argue it out. Turn away from it, affirm your belief in Allah and His messengers, and busy yourself with something else. Refusing to engage is itself the cure.

faq.q: How can I protect myself from Shaytan in general?
faq.a: Keep your heart occupied with the remembrance of Allah. The Prophet ﷺ taught that Shaytan flees the home in which Surah al-Baqarah is recited, that the morning and evening adhkar drive him away, and that saying La ilaha illallah... a hundred times is a protection from him for the day. A heart full of dhikr leaves him no empty room to enter.

email.subject: When doubt whispers
email.alt_subject: The thought you would never say aloud
email.preview: What the Prophet ﷺ taught for whispers of doubt and for driving Shaytan away.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

Sometimes the attack does not come from outside at all. It comes as a whisper: a thought about Allah you would never speak aloud, a question about your own belief that arrives unbidden and leaves you shaken. Many sincere believers carry this in silence, afraid that the whisper itself is proof their faith has failed.

It is not. The companions came to the Prophet ﷺ troubled by exactly these thoughts, and he told them that feeling the whisper and hating it is itself 'clear faith.' A heart that did not care would not be disturbed. His guidance was simple: seek refuge in Allah, then stop. Do not argue with the thought or chase it down. Turn away, affirm your belief, and it loses its grip.

Alongside that, keep your heart occupied: the morning and evening adhkar, the verses before sleep, Surah al-Baqarah in the home that Shaytan flees. The whisper feeds on an idle heart and retreats from one busy with the remembrance of Allah.

The full guidance, with Arabic, transliteration and meaning, is here:

social.hook:
A thought about Allah you would never say aloud.

The Prophet ﷺ said being disturbed by it is a sign of clear faith, not lost faith.
social.caption:
When doubt whispers: what the Prophet ﷺ taught.

His companions came to him troubled by thoughts they would rather fall from the sky than speak. He did not scold them. He told them that hating the whisper is itself 'clear faith.'

The response he taught is simple: seek refuge in Allah, then stop. Do not argue with the thought or chase it down. Turn away, affirm your belief, and keep your heart busy with His remembrance, the adhkar, Surah al-Baqarah in the home that Shaytan flees.

If you carry this quietly, you are not alone, and you are not lost. Save this and read the full guidance with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/protection-iman

#Waswas #DoubtInFaith #ProtectingIman #Dhikr #Dua #HisnAlMuslim #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

protection-iman.source_note: Prophetic adhkar transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com; al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih, jayyid/reliable as hasan. Recitation audio: the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com) for the adhkar. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com; framing is Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

items.title: Seek refuge, and stop
items.arabic: يَسْتَعِيذُ بِاللَّهِ. يَنْتَهِي عَمَّا شك فِيهِ
items.translit: A`ūdhu billāh
items.en: He should seek refuge in Allah and stop pursuing the thought.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said that when Shaytan throws a doubt at you, asking 'who created this, who created that' until he asks 'who created Allah', you should seek refuge in Allah and cease. The whisper dies the moment you refuse to follow it.

items.title: I believe in Allah and His messengers
items.arabic: يقول" آمنت بالله ورسله"
items.translit: Āmantu billāhi wa Rusulih.
items.en: I believe in Allah and His Messengers.
items.note: The other half of the response: instead of engaging the doubt, you answer it with a clear affirmation of faith. Belief stated plainly is the cure for belief questioned.

items.title: He is the First and the Last
items.arabic: يقرأ قوله تعالى (هُوَ الأوَّلُ، وَالآخِـرُ، وَالظّـاهِـرُ، وَالْبـاطِـنُ، وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيءٍ عَلـيم)
items.translit: Huwa ‘l-Awwalu wa ‘l-Ākhir, waẓ-Ẓāhiru wal-Bāṭin, wa huwa bikulli shay'in `Alīm.
items.en: (Recite the Ayat) He is the First and the Last, the Most High and the Most Near. And He is the Knower of all things (in Arabic).
items.note: Reciting this verse (al-Hadid 57:3) is taught as a remedy when persistent whispers will not leave, anchoring the heart in Allah's eternity and nearness when the mind is being pulled in circles.

items.title: Seek refuge from the outcast Shaytan
items.arabic: الاستعاذة بالله منه
items.translit: A`ūdhu billāhi minash-shayṭānir-rajīm.
items.en: Seeking refuge with Allah against him (i.e. by saying I seek refuge in Allah from Satan the outcast).
items.note: The plain weapon against him: A'udhu billahi min ash-shaytan ir-rajim, taught in the Qur'an itself as the response to his provocation.

items.title: The call to prayer
items.arabic: الْأَذَانُ
items.en: The call to prayer - 'Athan.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said that when the adhan is called, Shaytan turns and flees. The very announcement of prayer drives him off.

items.title: Remembrance and reciting the Qur'an
items.arabic: الْأَذْكَارُ وَقِرَاءَةُ الْقُرْآنِ
items.en: Saying words of Allah's remembrance (Thikr) and recitation of the Qur'an.
items.note: Shaytan flees the home in which Surah al-Baqarah is recited, and saying La ilaha illallah... a hundred times is a protection from him for the day. A heart kept busy with dhikr leaves him no room.

items.title: Guarding against the Dajjal
items.arabic: وَالْاسْتِعَاذَةُ بِاللَّهِ مِنْ فِتْنَتِهِ عَقِبَ التَّشَهُّدِ الْأَخِيرِ مِنْ كُلِّ صَلاَةٍ
items.en: Whoever memorizes ten 'Ayat (Verses) from the beginning of Surat Al-Kahf, will be protected from the False Messiah if he recites in every prayer after the final Tashahhud before ending the prayer, seeking the protection of Allah from the trials of the False Messiah.
items.ref: Muslim 1/555, another version mentions the last ten 'Ayat, Muslim 1/556
items.note: Whoever memorises the first ten verses of Surah al-Kahf is protected from the trial of the Dajjal.

items.title: Refuge from hidden shirk
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنّـي أَعـوذُبِكَ أَنْ أُشْـرِكَ بِكَ وَأَنا أَعْـلَمْ، وَأَسْتَـغْفِرُكَ لِما لا أَعْـلَم
items.translit: Allāhumma innī a`ūdhu bika an ushrika bika wa anā a`lam, wa astaghfiruka limā lā a`lam.
items.en: O Allah, I seek refuge in You lest I associate anything with You knowingly, and I seek Your forgiveness for what I know not.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught this against shirk so subtle he likened it to the creeping of an ant, unseen even by the one who falls into it.

items.title: When a bad omen unsettles you
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ لا طَيْـرَ إِلاّ طَيْـرُك، وَلا خَـيْرَ إِلاّ خَـيْرُك، وَلا إِلهَ غَيْـرُك
items.translit: Allāhumma lā ṭayra illā ṭayruk, wa lā khayra illā khayruk, wa lā ilāha ghayruk.
items.en: O Allah there is no portent other than Your portent, no goodness other than Your goodness, and none worthy of worship other than You.
items.note: There is no truth in omens. This du'a hands all good and all power back to Allah and walks on.

salawat.name: Salawat
salawat.title: Salawat: how to send blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
salawat.meta_description: The salawat (durood) the Prophet ﷺ taught: the full Ibrahimi blessing recited in every prayer, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning, with its source. Verified from Hisn al-Muslim.
salawat.keywords: salawat, durood, salat ala an-nabi, Ibrahimi salawat, allahumma salli ala muhammad, blessings on the prophet, how to send salawat, Buruja
lede: Allah and His angels send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ, and the believers are told to do the same. When the Companions heard the command, they asked the obvious question: we know how to greet you with peace, but how do we send blessings upon you? His answer is the salawat we still say in every prayer.
Say it and it returns to you tenfold. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever sends one blessing on him, Allah sends ten upon them.
intro: The fullest place for the salawat is in the sitting of the prayer, after the tashahhud and before the salam, and you can say it any time, as often as you like. Friday especially is a day to increase it.
Two authentic wordings are given here. They are nearly the same; the Prophet ﷺ taught both. Pick the one you know and say it with love, picturing the man you are blessing.
closing: There is no du'a like the salawat, because it is the one prayer guaranteed to be accepted: it is a request for Allah to honour the one He already loves most. And every time it leaves your lips it comes back multiplied, an angel carrying your name to him.
O Allah, send Your blessings and peace upon Muhammad ﷺ as You blessed Ibrahim, and let our tongues never tire of his name.

faq.q: What is the salawat?
faq.a: It is the blessing on the Prophet ﷺ that he himself taught: 'Allahumma salli ala Muhammad wa ala ali Muhammad...' (O Allah, bestow Your favour on Muhammad and the family of Muhammad as You did on Ibrahim...). It is recited in every prayer and may be said any time.

faq.q: When do you say the salawat in prayer?
faq.a: In the final sitting, after the tashahhud and before the closing salam. It is part of every prayer.

faq.q: What is the reward of sending salawat?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ said that whoever sends one blessing upon him, Allah sends ten blessings upon them in return. Increasing it, especially on Fridays, is among the most rewarding and beloved acts.

email.subject: The one du'a that is always accepted
email.alt_subject: How to send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ
email.preview: The salawat he taught when the Companions asked, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

Allah and His angels send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ, and we are told to do the same. When the Companions heard the command, they asked: we know how to greet you with peace, but how do we send blessings upon you? His answer is the salawat we still recite in every prayer.

There is no du'a quite like it, because it is the one prayer guaranteed to be answered: you are asking Allah to honour the one He loves most. And it returns to you tenfold; he ﷺ said whoever sends one blessing on him, Allah sends ten upon them.

The authentic wordings, with Arabic, transliteration and meaning, are here:

social.hook:
The Companions asked: we know how to greet you with peace, but how do we send blessings upon you?

This was his answer.
social.caption:
The salawat: how to send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ.

It is the one du'a guaranteed to be accepted, because you are asking Allah to honour the one He already loves most. And it returns to you multiplied: one blessing from you, ten from Allah.

Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/salawat

#Salawat #Durood #ProphetMuhammad #SallallahuAlayhiWasallam #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

salawat.source_note: The salawat (durood) are transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com; the Prophet's ﷺ teaching of it is in al-Bukhari and Muslim, taken as sahih. Recitation audio is the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com), aligned by dua number. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com. Reflections are Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference and grading before publication.

items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ صَلِّ عَلـى مُحمَّـد، وَعَلـى آلِ مُحمَّد، كَمـا صَلَّيـتَ عَلـى إبْراهـيمَ وَعَلـى آلِ إبْراهـيم، إِنَّكَ حَمـيدٌ مَجـيد ، اللّهُـمَّ بارِكْ عَلـى مُحمَّـد، وَعَلـى آلِ مُحمَّـد، كَمـا بارِكْتَ عَلـى إبْراهـيمَ وَعَلـى آلِ إبْراهيم، إِنَّكَ حَمـيدٌ مَجـيد
items.translit: Allāhumma ṣalli `alā Muḥammadinwa `alā ’āli Muḥammadin, kamā ṣallayta `alā 'Ibrāhīma wa `alā ’āli 'Ibrāhīma, 'innaka ḥamīdum-majīd. Allāhumma bārik `alā Muḥammadin wa `alā ’āli Muḥammadin, kamā bārakta `alā 'Ibrāhīma wa `alā 'āli 'Ibrāhīma, 'innaka ḥamīdum-majīd.
items.en: O Allah, bestow Your favor on Muhammad and on the family of Muhammad as You have bestowed Your favor on Ibrahim and on the family of Ibrahim, You are Praiseworthy, Most Glorious. O Allah, bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad as You have blessed Ibrahim and the family of Ibrahim, You are Praiseworthy, Most Glorious.
items.title: The full salawat (Ibrahimiyyah)
items.note: This is the salawat the Prophet ﷺ taught when the Companions asked how to send blessings on him. It is what you recite in every prayer, in the sitting before the salam.

items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ صَلِّ عَلـى مُحمَّـدٍ وَعَلـى أَزْواجِـهِ وَذُرِّيَّـتِه، كَمـا صَلَّيْـتَ عَلـى آلِ إبْراهـيم وَبارِكْ عَلـى مُحمَّـدٍ وَعَلـى أَزْواجِـهِ وَذُرِّيَّـتِه، كَمـا بارِكْتَ عَلـى آلِ إبْراهـيم إِنَّكَ حَمـيدٌ مَجـيد
items.translit: Allāhumma ṣalli `alā Muḥammadin wa `alā 'azwājihi wa dhurriyyatihi, kamā ṣallayta `alā 'āli 'Ibrāhīma. Wa bārik `alā Muḥammadin wa `alā 'azwājihi wa dhurriyyatihi, kamā bārakta `alā 'āli 'Ibrāhīma. 'Innaka ḥamīdum-majīd.
items.en: O Allah, bestow Your favor on Muhammad and upon his wives and progeny as You have bestowed Your favor upon the family of Ibrahim. And bless Muhammad and his wives and progeny as You have blessed the family of Ibrahim, You are full of praise, Most Glorious.
items.title: A second narration of the salawat
items.note: A slightly different wording of the same blessing, naming his wives and offspring. Both are authentic; you may use either.

death-grief.name: Death & Grief
death-grief.epithet: What to say when someone is dying, when they pass, and after
death-grief.title: Death & Grief Du'as: what to say when someone is dying and after they pass, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
death-grief.meta_description: Du'as for death and grief: comforting the dying, what to say when someone passes, the funeral prayer, condolence and visiting graves. Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
death-grief.keywords: death duas, grief dua, what to say when someone dies, inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi rajiun, funeral prayer dua, janazah dua, dua for the deceased, condolence dua islam, talqin, visiting graves dua, Buruja
lede: If you are reading this beside a hospital bed, or in the first raw hours after losing someone you love, then before anything else: may Allah be gentle with you, and gentle with the one you are carrying in your heart. There is nothing you have to perform here. These are simply the words the Prophet ﷺ left us for exactly this, so that when grief takes the words away, you have his to lean on.

Death in Islam is not the end of the person, only a turning toward the One they came from. These du'as walk that road with you: how to comfort someone as they near it, what to say in the moment they pass, how to pray over them, how to console those left behind, and how to keep the dead in your prayers long after the funeral. Take whichever one your situation needs. You do not need all of them today.
intro: These are arranged in the order grief tends to move: words for the one who is dying, then the moment of death, then the funeral prayer, burial, condolence, and visiting the grave. Find where you are, and start there.

Some of these du'as name the person; where you see [name], say their name in your heart or aloud. The Arabic is masculine in form, but every one of them is said for a woman just the same. And if all you can manage right now is 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un' (we belong to Allah, and to Him we return), that alone is enough. Allah knows what the heart cannot say.
closing: The bond does not break when they are buried. A righteous du'a from you still reaches them; it is one of the few gifts that crosses from this world into the grave. So keep making it, on the quiet days and the heavy ones, long after others have moved on. Asking forgiveness for the one you lost is also a mercy you are storing up for yourself.

And grieve. The Prophet ﷺ wept at the grave of his own son and said the heart grieves and the eyes shed tears, but we say only what pleases our Lord. Your tears are not weakness or complaint; they are love. O Allah, forgive our dead and our living, widen their graves and fill them with light, and reunite us with those we love in a Garden where there is no more parting.
O Allah, make our last words la ilaha illa Allah.

faq.q: What do you say when someone dies in Islam?
faq.a: Say 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un' (we belong to Allah, and to Him we shall return), from Surah al-Baqarah. It is the believer's response to any loss or calamity. You then make du'a for the one who has passed, asking Allah to forgive them and have mercy on them.

faq.q: What should I say to someone who is dying?
faq.a: Gently encourage them to say 'la ilaha illa Allah' (there is none worthy of worship but Allah), as the Prophet ﷺ taught, so it may be among their last words. Do not pressure or frighten them; remind them softly, sit with them, and make du'a for ease. The Prophet ﷺ also taught us to speak only good around the dying, for the angels say amin to what is said.

faq.q: What is the du'a for the deceased in the funeral prayer?
faq.a: The most complete one taught by the Prophet ﷺ begins 'Allahumma-ghfir lahu warhamhu' (O Allah, forgive him and have mercy on him). Several wordings are authentic and are gathered here, including a shorter one and a specific du'a for a child. Any of them fulfils the sunnah.

faq.q: Can my du'a still help someone after they have died?
faq.a: Yes. The Prophet ﷺ taught that the dead are reached by the du'a of the living, and that a righteous child praying for a parent is among the deeds that continue after death. Asking Allah to forgive them, raise their rank and ease their grave is one of the most loving things you can still do for them.

faq.q: What do you say when visiting a grave?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ taught us to greet the people of the graves with peace: 'Assalamu alaykum, people of this abode, from the believers and the Muslims...' It is a greeting and a du'a at once, and a reminder that we will one day join them. Visiting graves is encouraged because it softens the heart and reminds us of the hereafter.

email.subject: When grief takes the words away
email.alt_subject: What the Prophet ﷺ said in the face of death
email.preview: The du'as for dying, for loss, for the funeral prayer and the grave, gathered gently in one place.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

If this email finds you in grief, or beside someone you are about to lose, then before anything else: may Allah be gentle with you, and with the one you are holding in your heart. There is nothing here you have to perform. These are simply the words the Prophet ﷺ left us for exactly these moments, so that when grief takes your own words away, you can lean on his.

We have gathered them in the order grief tends to move: how to comfort the one who is dying, what to say when they pass, how to pray over them and lay them to rest, how to console those left behind, and how to keep the dead in your du'a long after the funeral. Take only the one your moment needs. If all you can manage is 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un', that alone is enough; Allah knows what the heart cannot say.

And know that the bond does not break at the grave. Your du'a still reaches them. Keep making it, on the quiet days and the heavy ones, long after others have moved on.

The full collection, with Arabic, transliteration and meaning, is here:

social.hook:
When someone you love is dying, grief can take all your words away.

The Prophet ﷺ left us his, for exactly that moment.
social.caption:
Du'as for death and grief.

Gathered gently in the order grief moves: how to comfort the one who is dying, what to say the moment they pass, the funeral prayer, burial, consoling the bereaved, and visiting the grave.

If all you can say is 'Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un', we belong to Allah and to Him we return, that alone is enough. Allah knows what the heart cannot.

And the bond does not break at the grave: your du'a still reaches them. Keep making it.

Save this for when you, or someone you love, will need it. Full collection with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/death-grief

#DeathInIslam #Grief #InnaLillahi #JanazahDua #DuaForTheDeceased #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

death-grief.source_note: Prophetic adhkar transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com; al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih, jayyid/reliable as hasan. Recitation audio: the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com) for the adhkar. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com; framing is Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

items.title: The dying person's own prayer
items.arabic: أللّهُـمَّ اغْفِـرْ لي وَارْحَمْـني وَأَلْحِقْـني بِالرَّفـيقِ الأّعْلـى
items.translit: Allāhumma’ghfir lī warḥamnī wa alḥiqnī bir-rafīqi ‘l-'A`alā.
items.en: O Allah, forgive me and have mercy upon me, and join me with the highest companions (in Paradise).
items.note: Among the last words of the Prophet ﷺ. If the one who is dying can still speak, these are beautiful words for them to repeat; the highest companions are the prophets and the righteous in Paradise.

items.title: What the Prophet ﷺ said as he was dying
items.arabic: جعل النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم عند موته يدخل يديه في الماء فيمسح بهما وجههُ ويقول : لا إله إلا الله إن للموت لسكرات
items.translit: As he was dying, the Prophet (ﷺ) dipped his hands in water and wiped his face saying:
items.en: There is none worthy of worship but Allah, surely death has agonies.
items.note: In his final moments he dipped his hand in water, wiped his face, and acknowledged that death has its agonies, yet still affirmed there is none worthy of worship but Allah. Even the best of creation found death hard; if it is hard for you or your loved one, you are not failing.

items.title: Gently reminding the dying to affirm Allah
items.arabic: لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وَاللّهُ أَكْبَـر، لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وحْـدَهُ، لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وحْـدَهُ لا شَريكَ لهُ، لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ لهُ المُلكُ ولهُ الحَمْد، لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وَلا حَـوْلَ وَلا قُـوَّةَ إِلاّ بِالله
items.translit: Lā ilāha illallāh wallāhu Akbar, lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdah, lā ilāha illallāh waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lā ilāha illallāh lahu ‘l-mulku wa lahu ‘l-ḥamd, lā ilāha illallāh wa lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata illā billāh.
items.en: There is none worthy of worship but Allah, Allah is the Most Great. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. There is none worthy of worship but Allah, His is the dominion and His is the praise. There is none worthy of worship but Allah, there is no power and no might but by Allah.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said: prompt your dying to say 'la ilaha illa Allah'. Remind them softly and without pressure, hoping it will be among their last words; you need not make them repeat it once they have said it.

items.title: Whoever's last words are these enters Paradise
items.arabic: من كان آخر كلامه "لا إله إلا الله" دخل الجنة
items.translit: Lā ilāha illallāh
items.en: Whoever's last words are 'There is no god but Allah' will enter Paradise.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ promised that whoever's final words in this life are 'la ilaha illa Allah' will enter Paradise. This is why we long for these words at the end, for ourselves and for those we love.

items.title: When loss strikes: we belong to Allah
items.arabic: إِنّا للهِ وَإِنَا إِلَـيْهِ راجِعـون ، اللهُـمِّ اْجُـرْني في مُصـيبَتي، وَاخْلُـفْ لي خَيْـراً مِنْـها
items.translit: Innā lillāhi wa innā ilayhi rāji`ūn, Allāhumma-jurni fī muṣībatī wa 'khluf lī khayran minhā.
items.en: O Allah, reward me in my affliction and replace it for me with something better than it.
items.note: The du'a of the one struck by calamity, from the Qur'an. The Prophet ﷺ taught that whoever says it and seeks reward in their affliction, Allah will reward them and replace what was lost with something better.

items.title: Du'a for the one who has passed
items.arabic: اللهُـمِّ اغْفِـرْ لِـفلان وَارْفَعْ دَرَجَتَـهُ في المَهْـدِييـن ، وَاخْـلُفْـهُ في عَقِـبِهِ في الغابِـرين، وَاغْفِـرْ لَنـا وَلَـهُ يا رَبَّ العـالَمـين، وَافْسَـحْ لَهُ في قَبْـرِهِ وَنَـوِّرْ لَهُ فيه
items.translit: Allāhumma’ghfir li (name of the person) warfa` darajatahu fi ‘l-mahdiyyīn, wakhlufhu fī `aqibihi fi ‘l-ghābirīn, wagh’fir-lanā wa lahu yā Rabba ‘l-`ālamīn, wafsaḥ lahu fī qabrihi wa nawwir lahu fīh.
items.en: O Allah, forgive [name of the person] and elevate his station among those who are guided. Send him along the path of those who came before, and forgive us and him, O Lord of the worlds. Enlarge for him his grave, and shed light upon him in it.
items.note: Say their name where indicated. A complete, tender prayer for the deceased: forgiveness, a raised rank, a widened and illuminated grave.

items.title: The funeral prayer: forgive him and have mercy on him
items.arabic: اللهُـمِّ اغْفِـرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْـه ، وَعافِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنْـه ، وَأَكْـرِمْ نُزُلَـه ، وَوَسِّـعْ مُدْخَـلَه ، وَاغْسِلْـهُ بِالْمـاءِ وَالثَّـلْجِ وَالْبَـرَدْ ، وَنَقِّـهِ مِنَ الْخطـايا كَما نَـقّيْتَ الـثَّوْبَ الأَبْيَـضَ مِنَ الدَّنَـسْ ، وَأَبْـدِلْهُ داراً خَـيْراً مِنْ دارِه ، وَأَهْلاً خَـيْراً مِنْ أَهْلِـه ، وَزَوْجَـاً خَـيْراً مِنْ زَوْجِه ، وَأَدْخِـلْهُ الْجَـنَّة ، وَأَعِـذْهُ مِنْ عَذابِ القَـبْر [وَعَذابِ النّـار]
items.translit: Allāhumma’ghfir lahu warḥamh, wa `āfihi, wa`fu `anh, wa akrim nuzulah, wa wassi` mudkhalah, wagh’silhu bi ‘lmā'i wath-thalji walbarad, wa naqqihi mina ‘l-khaṭāyā kamā naqqaytath-thawba ‘l-abyaḍa minad-danas, wa abdilhu dāran khayran min dārih, wa ahlan khayran min ahlih, wa zawjan khayran min zawjih, wa adkhilhu ‘l-jannah, wa a`idhhu min `adhābi ‘l-qabri [wa `adhābin-nār].
items.en: O Allah, forgive him and have mercy on him, and give him strength and pardon him. Be generous to him, and cause his entrance to be wide, and wash him with water and snow and hail. Cleanse him of his transgressions as white cloth is cleansed of stains. Give him an abode better than his home, and a family better than his family, and a wife better than his wife. Take him into Paradise, and protect him from the punishment of the grave [and from the punishment of Hell-fire].
items.note: The fullest du'a the Prophet ﷺ taught for the deceased in the janazah prayer. Said for a woman, the pronouns simply change; the meaning is the same.

items.title: The funeral prayer: forgive our living and our dead
items.arabic: اللهُـمِّ اغْفِـرْ لِحَيِّـنا وَمَيِّتِـنا، وَشـاهِدِنا وَغائِبِـنا ، وَصَغيـرِنا وَكَبيـرِنا ، وَذَكَـرِنا وَأُنْثـانا اللهُـمِّ مَنْ أَحْيَيْـتَهُ مِنّا فَأَحْيِـهِ عَلى الإِسْلام ، وَمَنْ تَوَفَّـيْتَهُ مِنّا فَتَوَفَّـهُ عَلى الإِيـمان ، اللهُـمِّ لا تَحْـرِمْنـا أَجْـرَه ، وَلا تُضِـلَّنا بَعْـدَه
items.translit: Allāhumma’ghfir liḥayyinā, wa mayyitinā, wa shāhidinā, wa ghā'ibinā, wa ṣaghīrinā wa kabīrinā, wa dhakarinā wa unthānā. Allāhumma man aḥyaytahu minnā fa aḥyihi `ala ‘l-Islām, wa man tawaffaytahu minnā fatawaffahu `alal-īmān, Allāhumma lā taḥrimnā ajrah wa lā tuḍillanā ba`dah.
items.en: O Allah forgive our living and our dead, those who are with us and those who are absent, our young and our old, our menfolk and our womenfolk. O Allah, whomever you give life from among us give him life in Islam, and whomever you take away from us take him away in Faith. O Allah, do not forbid us their reward and do not send us astray after them.
items.note: A du'a that gathers the whole community, present and absent, young and old, into one plea for mercy, and asks that we ourselves be kept on faith until we meet Allah.

items.title: The funeral prayer: under Your protection
items.arabic: اللهُـمِّ إِنَّ فُلانَ بْنَ فُلانٍ في ذِمَّـتِك ، وَحَبْـلِ جِـوارِك ، فَقِـهِ مِنْ فِتْـنَةِ الْقَـبْرِ وَعَذابِ النّـار ، وَأَنْتَ أَهْلُ الْوَفـاءِ وَالْـحَقِّ ، فَاغْفِـرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْـهُ ، إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الغَـفورُ الـرَّحيم
items.translit: Allāhumma inna [name the person] fī dhimmatik, wa ḥabli jiwārik, faqihi min fitnati ‘l-qabri wa `adhābin-nār, wa anta ahlu ‘l-wafā'i wa ‘l-ḥaqq. Faghfir lahu warḥamh innaka anta ‘l-Ghafūrur-Raḥīm.
items.en: O Allah, surely [name the person] is under Your protection, and in the rope of Your security, so save him from the trial of the grave and from the punishment of the Fire. You fulfill promises and grant rights, so forgive him and have mercy on him. Surely You are Most Forgiving, Most Merciful.
items.note: Placing the deceased in Allah's care and covenant, and asking to be spared the trial of the grave and the Fire.

items.title: The funeral prayer: in need of Your mercy
items.arabic: اللهُـمِّ عَبْـدُكَ وَابْنُ أَمَـتِك، احْتـاجَ إِلى رَحْمَـتِك، وَأَنْتَ غَنِـيٌّ عَنْ عَذابِـه، إِنْ كانَ مُحْـسِناً فَزِدْ في حَسَـناتِه، وَإِنْ كانَ مُسـيئاً فَتَـجاوَزْ عَنْـه
items.translit: Allāhumma `abduka wabnu 'amatik iḥtāja ilā raḥmatik, wa anta ghaniyyun `an `adhābih, in kāna muḥsinan fazid fī ḥasanātih, wa in kāna musī'an fatajāwaz `anh.
items.en: O Allah, Your male slave and the child of Your female slave is in need of Your mercy, and You are not in need of his torment. If he was pious then increase his rewards and if he was a transgressor then pardon him.
items.ref: Al-Hakim 1/359 who graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed with him
items.note: A short, humbling du'a: Allah has no need to punish His servant, so we ask Him to increase the good of the righteous and pardon the one who fell short.

items.title: The funeral prayer for a child
items.arabic: "اللهم أعذه من عذاب القبر " وإن قال: "اللهم اجعله فرطاً وذخراً لوالديه ، وشفيعاً مجاباً اللهم ثقل به موازينها وأعظم به أجورهما ، وألحقهُ بصالح المؤمنين ، واجعلهُ في كفالة إبراهيم ، وقه برحمتك عذاب الجحيم ، وأبدله داراً خيراً من داره ، وأهلاً خيراً من أهله ، اللهم اغفر لاسلافنا ، وأفراطنا ، ومن سبقنا بالإيمان " فحسن
items.translit: Allāhumma a`idh’hu min `adhābi ‘l-qabr [or say:
items.en: O Allah, protect him from the torment of the grave. [It is also good to say:] O Allah, make him a precursor, a forerunner and a treasure for his parents and an answered intercessor. O Allah, make him weigh heavily in their scales (of good) and magnify their reward. Make him join the righteous of the believers. Place him in the care of Ibrahim. Save him by Your mercy from the torment of Hell. Give him a home better than his home, and a family better than his family. O Allah, forgive those who have gone (i.e. passed away) before us, our children lost (by death), and those who have preceded us in Faith.
items.ref: Ibn Qudamah, Al-Mughni 3/416 and Ad-Duroosul-Muhimmah li-Aammatil-'Ummah, pg
items.note: For the loss of a little one. It asks that the child be made a treasure and an awaiting intercessor for the parents, a weight in their scales and a reason for reward, and placed in the care of Ibrahim, who tends the children of the believers in Paradise.

items.title: A shorter prayer for a child
items.arabic: اللهُـمِّ اجْعَلْـهُ لَنا فَرَطـاً، وَسَلَـفاً وَأَجْـراً
items.translit: Allāhumma’j`alhu lanā faraṭan, wa salafan, wa ajra.
items.en: O Allah, make him for us a precursor, a forerunner and a cause of reward.
items.ref: Al-Hasan (Al-Basri) used to recite Surat Al-Fatihah for a child's funeral and then say this
items.note: Al-Hasan al-Basri would recite al-Fatihah at a child's funeral and then say this brief du'a, asking that the child be sent ahead as a reward and a treasure stored with Allah.

items.title: Consoling someone who is grieving
items.arabic: "إن لله ما أخذ وله ما أعطى وكل شيء عنده بأجل مُسمى ... فلتصبر ولتحتسب " وإن قال : "أعظم الله أجرك ، وأحسن عزاءك وغفر لميتك" فحسن
items.translit: Inna lillāhi mā akhadh, wa lahu mā a`tā, wa kullu shay'in `indahu bi ajalin musammā . . . faltaṣbir wa ‘l-taḥtasib . [Also good to say:
items.en: Surely, Allah takes what is His, and what He gives is His, and to all things He has appointed a time... so have patience and be rewarded. May Allah magnify your reward, and make perfect your bereavement, and forgive your departed.
items.note: The words the Prophet ﷺ sent to console a grieving family: everything belongs to Allah, who lends and takes back in His wisdom, so be patient and seek reward. The added line is a beautiful way to offer condolences directly.

items.title: Laying the deceased in the grave
items.arabic: بِسْـمِ اللهِ وَعَلـى سُـنَّةِ رَسـولِ الله
items.translit: Bismillāh wa `alā sunnati Rasūlillāh.
items.en: With the Name of Allah, and according to the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah.
items.ref: Abu Dawud 3/314 with an authentic chain
items.note: Said as the body is placed into the grave: in the name of Allah and upon the way of His Messenger ﷺ.

items.title: After the burial: asking that they be made firm
items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لَهُ اللَّهُمَّ ثَبِّتْهُ
items.translit: Allāhumma’ghfir lah Allāhumma thabbit’h.
items.en: O Allah, forgive him. O Allah, strengthen him.
items.ref: Abu Dawud 3/315, and Al-Hakim 1/370 who graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ would pause after a burial and tell those present to seek forgiveness for their brother and ask that he be made steadfast, for he is now being questioned. A reminder to keep praying even as the soil is settled.

items.title: Greeting the people of the graves
items.arabic: السَّلامُ عَلَـيْكُمْ أَهْلَ الدِّيارِ مِنَ المؤْمِنيـنَ وَالْمُسْلِمين، وَإِنّا إِنْ شاءَ اللهُ بِكُـمْ لاحِقـون، نَسْـاَلُ اللهَ لنـا وَلَكُـمْ العـافِيَة
items.translit: Assalāmu `alaykum ahlad-diyāri minal-mu'minīna wa ‘l-muslimīn, wa innā in shā' Allāhu bikum lāḥiqūn nas'alullāha lanā wa lakumul-`āfiyah.
items.en: Peace be upon you, people of this abode, from among the believers and those who are Muslims, and we, by the Will of Allah, shall be joining you. [May Allah have mercy on the first of us and the last of us] I ask Allah to grant us and you well-being.
items.ref: Muslim 2/671, Ibn Majah 1/494, the portion brackets is from Muslim 2/671
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught this to say when visiting a graveyard: peace upon its people, and the quiet acknowledgement that we too will follow. A greeting, a du'a, and a softening of the heart all at once.

sunnah-duas.name: Sunnah Du'as
sunnah-duas.epithet: The du'as the Prophet ﷺ carried
sunnah-duas.title: Sunnah Du'as: the comprehensive personal supplications of the Prophet ﷺ in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
sunnah-duas.meta_description: The heart-supplications the Prophet ﷺ carried: for anxiety and grief, after sinning, at wonder and joy, in Arabic, transliteration and English with sources.
sunnah-duas.keywords: sunnah dua, dua for anxiety, dua for grief, Allahumma inni abduka, dua after sin, dua for distress, prophetic supplications, comprehensive dua, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Some du'as belong to a time or a place: a morning, a journey, a meal. These do not. These are the supplications the Prophet ﷺ carried inside himself, reached for when grief closed in, when a sin needed washing away, when joy struck and the heart turned at once to its Maker. They are the words of a heart that lived in conversation with Allah.

This is a small gathering of those du'as: the famous plea for the one weighed down by anxiety, the refuge from worry and grief, what to do after you have sinned, and the response of wonder and gratitude when good news arrives. Learn them, and you carry a piece of the Prophet's ﷺ own inner life.
intro: These are not tied to a clock. Reach for 'O Allah, I am Your slave' when distress and sadness have settled on you; the Prophet ﷺ promised Allah would replace the grief with relief for whoever says it. There is his standing refuge from worry, debt, and being overpowered. And there is what to do when you have fallen into sin: purify yourself, pray two rak'ahs, and seek forgiveness.

There are also words for the other end of the heart, the moment of awe and joy: 'Subhanallah', 'Allahu Akbar', or for great news, a prostration of gratitude to the One who sent it. Recite the Arabic, lean on the transliteration, and read the meaning so the words are truly yours.
closing: What these du'as teach is not a set of phrases but a habit of the heart: in grief you turn to Allah, in sin you turn back to Allah, in wonder you turn toward Allah. The Prophet ﷺ met every state of the soul by carrying it straight to its Lord. Take these few into your chest, and let them be the reflex that meets your own highs and lows, so that whatever the day brings, your heart already knows the way home.

O Allah, make the Qur'an the spring of our hearts and the light of our chests, lift our grief and our worry, accept our return after every fall, and let our first turn in joy and in sorrow always be to You.

faq.q: What is the du'a for anxiety and grief?
faq.a: The most comprehensive is 'Allahumma inni abduka', in which you call upon Allah by every Name He has and ask Him to make the Qur'an the spring of your heart and the remover of your sadness and distress. The Prophet ﷺ said Allah will take away the grief of whoever says it and replace it with relief. There is also a shorter, related refuge from worry and grief.

faq.q: What should I do after committing a sin?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ taught that any servant who sins, then purifies himself well, prays two rak'ahs, and seeks Allah's forgiveness, will be forgiven. The door of return is never closed; the sunnah is to walk through it at once.

faq.q: Is there a sunnah for moments of joy or good news?
faq.a: Yes. At a sight or moment of wonder the Prophet ﷺ would say 'Subhanallah' or 'Allahu Akbar', and on receiving news that delighted him he would fall into a prostration of gratitude to Allah (sajdat ash-shukr). Joy, like sorrow, is met by turning to Him.

These are the supplications the Prophet ﷺ carried inside himself, reached for when grief closed in, when a sin needed washing away, when joy struck and the heart turned at once to its Maker. We have gathered a small handful of them, each with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source. The famous plea for the one weighed down by anxiety, the refuge from worry and grief, what to do after you have sinned, and the response of wonder and gratitude when good news arrives. What they teach is not a set of phrases but a habit of the heart: in grief you turn to Allah, in sin you turn back to Allah, in wonder you turn toward Allah. Carry these few, and your heart will already know the way home, whatever the day brings. The full collection, formatted to read or recite, is here:

items.title: O Allah, I am Your slave
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّي عَبْـدُكَ ابْنُ عَبْـدِكَ ابْنُ أَمَتِـكَ نَاصِيَتِي بِيَـدِكَ مَاضٍ فِيَّ حُكْمُكَ عَدْلٌ فِيَّ قَضَاؤكَ أَسْأَلُـكَ بِكُلِّ اسْمٍ هُوَ لَكَ سَمَّـيْتَ بِهِ نَفْسَكَ أِوْ أَنْزَلْتَـهُ فِي كِتَابِكَ أَوْ عَلَّمْـتَهُ أَحَداً مِنْ خَلْقِـكَ أَوِ اسْتَـأْثَرْتَ بِهِ فِي عِلْمِ الغَيْـبِ عِنْـدَكَ أَنْ تَجْـعَلَ القُرْآنَ رَبِيـعَ قَلْبِـي وَنورَ صَـدْرِي وجَلَاءَ حُـزْنِي وذَهَابَ هَمِّـي
items.translit: Allāhumma innī `abduk, ibnu `abdik, ibnu amatik, nāsiyatī biyadik, māḍin fiyya ḥukmuk, `adlun fiyya qaḍā'uk, as'aluka bikullis’min huwa lak, sammayta bihi nafsak, aw anzaltahu fī kitābik, aw `allamtahu aḥadan min khalqik, aw‘ista'tharta bihi fī `ilmil-ghaybi `indak, an taj`ala ‘l-Qur'āna rabī`a qalbī, wa nūra ṣadrī, wa jalā'a ḥuznī, wa dhahāba hammī.
items.en: O Allah, I am Your slave, and the son of Your male slave, and the son of your female slave. My forehead is in Your Hand (i.e. you have control over me). Your Judgment upon me is assured, and Your Decree concerning me is just. I ask You by every Name that You have named Yourself with, revealed in Your Book, taught any one of Your creation, or kept unto Yourself in the knowledge of the unseen that is with You, to make the Qur'an the spring of my heart, and the light of my chest, the banisher of my sadness, and the reliever of my distress.
items.ref: Ahmad 1/391, and Al-Albani graded it authentic
items.note: The great du'a of anxiety and grief. You call on Allah by every Name He has and ask Him to make the Qur'an the spring of your heart and the remover of your sorrow. The Prophet ﷺ said Allah removes the grief of whoever says it and gives relief in its place.

items.title: Refuge from worry and grief
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّي أَعْوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الهَـمِّ وَ الْحُـزْنِ، والعًجْـزِ والكَسَلِ والبُخْـلِ والجُـبْنِ، وضَلْـعِ الـدَّيْنِ وغَلَبَـةِ الرِّجال
items.translit: Allāhumma 'innī 'a`ūdhu bika mina ‘l-ḥammi wa ‘l-ḥuzn, wa ‘l-`ajzi wa ‘l-kasal, wa ‘l-bukhli wa ‘l-jubn, wa ḍala`id-dayn, wa ghalabatir-rijāl.
items.en: O Allah, I seek refuge in you from grief and sadness, from weakness and from laziness, from miserliness and from cowardice, from being overcome by debt and overpowered by men (i.e. others).
items.note: The Prophet's ﷺ standing refuge from worry and sadness, weakness and laziness, miserliness and cowardice, and from being crushed by debt or overpowered by others.

items.title: After you have sinned
items.arabic: مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ يُذنِبُ ذَنْباً فَيُحْسِنُ الطُّهُورَ، ثُمَّ يَقُومُ فَيُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ إِلاَّ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ
items.en: There is not any slave of Allah who commits a sin, then he perfects his purification and stands to pray two Rak'ahs of prayer, then seeks Allah's forgiveness, except that Allah will forgive him.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught that any servant who sins, then purifies himself well, prays two rak'ahs, and seeks Allah's forgiveness, will be forgiven. The way back, the moment you need it.

items.title: At a sight of wonder: Subhanallah
items.arabic: سُـبْحانَ الله
items.translit: Subḥānallāh!
items.en: (Glory is to Allah).
items.note: 'Glory is to Allah.' Said at a moment of awe, when something draws the heart upward toward its Maker.

items.title: At a moment of awe: Allahu Akbar
items.arabic: اللهُ أَكْـبَر
items.translit: Allāhu Akbar!
items.en: (Allah is the Most Great)
items.note: 'Allah is the Most Great.' The Prophet ﷺ would magnify Allah at the sight of what was vast or astonishing.

items.title: The prostration of gratitude
items.arabic: كَانَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ أَمْرٌ يَسُرُّهُ أَوْ يُسَرُّ بِهِ خَرَّ سَاجِداً شُكْراً لِلَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى
items.en: The Prophet (ﷺ), upon receiving news which pleased him or which caused pleasure, would prostrate in gratitude to Allah blessed and exalted.
items.note: On receiving news that pleased him, the Prophet ﷺ would fall into prostration in thanks to Allah (sajdat ash-shukr). The body's answer to joy: straight to the ground before the One who sent it.

items.title: Guarding the household at nightfall
items.arabic: إِذَا كَانَ جُنْحُ اللَّيْلِ – أَوْ أَمْسَيْتُم – فَـكُفُّوا صِبْيانَـكُم، فَإِنَّ الشَّيَاطِينَ تَنْتَشِرُ حِينَئِذٍ، فَإِذَا ذَهَبَ سَاعَةٌ مِنَ اللَّيلِ فَخَلُّوهُمْ، وَأَغْلِقُوا الأَبْوَابَ وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ؛ فَإِنَّ الشَّيطَانَ لاَ يَفْتَحُ بَاباً مُغلَقاً، وَأَوْكُوا قِرَبَكُمْ، وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ، وَخَمِّرُوا آنِيَتَكُم، وَاذْكُرُوا اسْمَ اللَّهِ، وَلَوْ أَنْ تَعْرُضُوا عَلَيْهَا شَيْئاً، وَأَطْفِئُوا مَصَابِيحَكُمْ
items.en: When night falls, restrain your children (from going out) because at such time the devils spread about. After a period of time has passed, let them be. Shut your doors and mention Allah’s name, for verily the devil does not open a shut door, tie up your water-skins and mention Allah’s name, cover your vessels with anything and mention Allah’s name and put out your lamps.
items.note: As night falls the Prophet ﷺ taught us to keep the children in, shut the doors, cover the vessels and tie the water-skins, and put out the lamps, mentioning Allah's name over each, for the shaytan cannot open what is closed in His name.

distress.name: Difficulty & Distress
distress.epithet: The words the Prophet ﷺ turned to when the weight came down
distress.title: Du'as for Difficulty and Distress: anxiety, grief and hardship in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
distress.meta_description: Authentic du'as for anxiety, grief, severe distress and hard times, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source. From the sunnah, via Buruja.
distress.keywords: dua for anxiety, dua for distress, dua for grief, dua for hard times, dua for sadness, dua when worried, dua for relief from hardship, dua against oppression, dua karb, La ilaha illa anta, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Some days the weight is real. The diagnosis, the loss, the matter you cannot see your way out of, the people who mean you harm. The Prophet ﷺ knew those days, and he did not meet them with silence. He met them with words, taught to him by the One who alone can lift what presses on a heart.

These are those words. Not slogans to push the feeling away, but du'as that take the weight to its proper place: into the hands of Allah, the only One who can carry it. Say them when you have no other words left. They were given for exactly that.
intro: There is no fixed number and no fixed time. When distress comes, these are what you reach for. Some, like the du'a of Yunus, you can repeat as many times as your heart needs. Say the Arabic where you can, and read the meaning slowly so the words become your own and not just sounds.

A few of these are for a specific kind of distress: fear of a person, of oppressors, of those who plot against you. They are placed here together. Where a du'a asks you to name someone, name them quietly to Allah, and leave the matter with Him.
distress.closing_heading: When the weight does not lift at once
closing: Relief is promised, but its timing belongs to Allah, not to us. Sometimes the du'a is answered by the matter changing, and sometimes by your heart being made wide enough to carry it. Both are an answer. Keep asking, and do not read your delay as His refusal.

O Allah, You ease what You will, and nothing is hard for You. Lift from us what we cannot lift ourselves, widen our hearts in the waiting, and let us find that You were nearer to us in the difficulty than we knew.

faq.q: What is the best du'a to say when I am anxious or distressed?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ taught several. A central one is the du'a of distress (du'a al-karb): La ilaha illallah al-'Azim al-Halim... declaring Allah's greatness over whatever is pressing on you. The du'a of Yunus, La ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu min az-zalimin, is also reported as the supplication by which a believer's call is answered. Reach for whichever your heart can hold, and repeat it.

faq.q: Is there a du'a for when someone is harming me or treating me unjustly?
faq.a: Yes. Several here are for exactly that: asking Allah to be your support against an oppressor, to restrain those who wish you harm, and to suffice you against them however He wills. Where the du'a leaves a space, name the person quietly to Allah. You are not taking revenge into your own hands; you are handing the matter to the One who judges with perfect justice.

faq.q: What should I say when something I did not want has already happened?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ taught us to say Qaddarallahu wa ma sha'a fa'al, 'It is the decree of Allah, and He does what He wills,' and warned against saying 'if only I had...', because that opens the door to regret and to Shaytan. Accepting the decree does not mean you stop striving; it means you refuse to be tormented by what is already past.

email.subject: When the weight comes down
email.alt_subject: The words the Prophet ﷺ said in hard times
email.preview: Authentic du'as for anxiety, grief and severe distress, with Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

Some days the weight is real. The diagnosis, the loss, the matter you cannot see your way out of, the people who mean you harm. The Prophet ﷺ knew those days, and he did not meet them with silence. He met them with words, taught to him by the One who alone can lift what presses on a heart.

We have gathered those words: the du'a of distress, the du'a of Yunus by which a believer's call is answered, and the du'as for when you fear someone's harm or face injustice. They are not slogans to push the feeling away. They take the weight to its proper place, into the hands of Allah.

Relief is promised, though its timing belongs to Him. Sometimes the answer is the matter changing; sometimes it is your heart being made wide enough to carry it. Keep asking, and do not read delay as refusal.

The full collection, with Arabic, transliteration and meaning, is here:

These are not slogans to push the feeling away. They take the weight to its proper place: into the hands of the One who alone can lift it. Say them when you have no other words left. They were given for exactly that.
