items.title: The du'a of distress
items.arabic: لَا إلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ الْعَظـيمُ الْحَلِـيمْ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ رَبُّ العَـرْشِ العَظِيـمِ، لَا إِلَـهَ إِلَّا اللَّهْ رَبُّ السَّمَـوّاتِ ورّبُّ الأَرْضِ ورَبُّ العَرْشِ الكَـريم
items.translit: Lā ilāha illallāh al-`Aẓīmul-Ḥalīm, lā ilāha illallāh Rabbu ‘l-`Arshi ‘l-'Aẓīm, lā ilāha illallāh Rabbus-samāwāti wa Rabbu ‘l-arḍ wa Rabbu ‘l-`Arshi ‘l-Karīm.
items.en: There is none worthy of worship but Allah, the Mighty, the Forbearing. There is none worthy of worship but Allah, Lord of the Magnificent Throne. There is none worthy of worship but Allah, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, and Lord of the Noble Throne.
items.note: The du'a al-karb. The Prophet ﷺ would say these words at times of great distress, declaring Allah's greatness and forbearance over whatever is pressing down. When everything feels larger than you, this names the One who is larger than it all.

items.title: I hope for Your mercy
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ رَحْمَتَـكَ أَرْجـو فَلا تَكِلـني إِلى نَفْـسي طَـرْفَةَ عَـيْن، وَأَصْلِـحْ لي شَأْنـي كُلَّـه لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أنْـت
items.translit: Allāhumma raḥmataka arjū falā takilnī ilā nafsī ṭarfata `ayn, wa aṣliḥ lī sha'nī kullah, lā ilāha illā ant.
items.en: O Allah, I hope for Your mercy. Do not leave me to myself even for the blinking of an eye (i.e. a moment). Correct all of my affairs for me. There is none worthy of worship but You.
items.note: A du'a for the moment you feel you cannot trust yourself to manage your own affairs. You ask Allah not to leave you to yourself for even a blink, and to set right everything that has gone wrong.

items.title: The du'a of Yunus
items.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أنْـت سُـبْحانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْـتُ مِنَ الظّـالِميـن
items.translit: Lā ilāha illā anta subḥānaka innī kuntu minaẓ-ẓālimīn.
items.en: There is none worthy of worship but You, glory is to You. Surely, I was among the wrongdoers.
items.note: The words the Prophet Yunus called out from inside the whale's darkness. The Prophet ﷺ said no Muslim ever supplicates with them for anything without Allah answering him. Repeat it when you are in your own dark place.

items.title: Allah, Allah is my Lord
items.arabic: اللهُ اللهُ رَبِّي لا أُشْـرِكُ بِهِ شَيْـئاً
items.translit: Allāh, Allāhu Rabbī lā ushriku bihi shay'a.
items.en: Allah, Allah is my Lord. I do not associate anything with Him.
items.note: Taught for moments of anguish and trouble: a simple, firm declaration that returns your heart to the only Lord, refusing to associate anything with Him no matter how the situation shakes you.

items.title: Against those who wish you harm
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنا نَجْـعَلُكَ في نُحـورِهِـم، وَنَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شُرورِهـمْ
items.translit: Allāhumma innā naj`aluka fī nuḥūrihim wa na`ūdhu bika min shurūrihim.
items.en: O Allah, we place You before them, and we seek refuge in You from their evil.
items.ref: Abu Dawud 2/89, and Al-Hakim graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed 2/142
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ would say this when he feared a people: asking Allah to restrain them and seeking refuge in Him from their evil, rather than meeting their hostility with his own.

items.title: You are my strength and my support
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ أَنْتَ عَضُـدي، وَأَنْتَ نَصـيري، بِكَ أَجـولُ وَبِكَ أَصـولُ وَبِكَ أُقـاتِل
items.translit: Allāhumma anta `aḍudī, wa anta naṣīrī, bika ajūlu, wa bika aṣūlu, wa bika uqātil.
items.en: O Allah, You are my strength and You are my support. For Your sake, I go forth and for Your sake, I advance and for Your sake, I fight.
items.note: A du'a for facing an opponent or a struggle: you go forward, advance and stand not by your own power but by Allah's.

items.title: Allah is sufficient for us
items.arabic: حَسْبُـنا اللهُ وَنِعْـمَ الوَكـيل
items.translit: Ḥasbunallāhu wa ni`mal-wakīl.
items.en: Allah is sufficient for us and the best of those on whom to depend.
items.note: Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal-wakil. The words said by Ibrahim when he was thrown into the fire, and by the believers when they were warned of a gathered enemy. When people threaten and you feel outnumbered, this is enough.

items.title: Be my support against an oppressor
items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ ربَّ السَّمَوَاتِ السَّبْعِ، وَرَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، كُنْ لِي جَاراً مِنْ فُلاَنِ بْنِ فُلاَنٍ، وَأَحْزَابِهِ مِنْ خَلاَئِقِكَ، أَنْ يَفْرُطَ عَلَيَّ أَحَدٌ مِنْهُمْ أَوْ يَطْغَى، عَزَّ جَارُكَ، وَجَلَّ ثَنَاؤُكَ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ
items.translit: Allāhumma Rabbas-samāwātis-sab`, wa Rabba ‘l-`Arshi ‘l-`Aẓīm, kun lī jāran min [here you mention the person's name], wa aḥzābihi min khalā'iqik, an yafruṭa `alayya aḥadun minhum aw yaṭghā, `azza jāruk, wa jalla thanā'uk, wa lā ilāha illā ant.
items.en: O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens, Lord of the Magnificent Throne, be for me a support against [such and such a person] and his helpers from among your creatures, lest any of them abuse me or do me wrong. Mighty is Your patronage, and glorious are Your praises. There is none worthy of worship but You.
items.note: For when you fear the wrong or abuse of a specific person. Name them quietly to Allah where the du'a leaves the space, and ask the Lord of the seven heavens to stand between you and their harm.

items.title: Allah is mightier than what I fear
items.arabic: الله أكبر، الله أعز من خلقه جميعاً ، الله أعز مما أخاف وأحذر ، أعوذ بالله الذي لا إله إلا هو ، الممسك السموات السبع أن يقعن على الأرض إلا بإذنه ، من شر عبدك فلان ، وجنوده وأتباعه وأشياعه ، من الجن والأنس ، اللهم كن لي جاراً من شرهم ، جل ثناؤك وعز جارك ، وتبارك اسمك ، ولا إله غيرك
items.translit: Allāhu Akbar, Allāhu a`azzu min khalqihi jamī`a, Allāhu a`azzu mimmā akhāfu wa aḥdhar, a`ūdhu billāhi ‘l-ladhī lā ilāha illā hū, almumsikis-samāwātis-sab`i an yaqa`na `ala ‘l-arḍi illā bi idhnih, min sharri `abdika [name of the person], wa junūdihi wa atbā`ihi wa ashyā`ih, mina ‘l-jinni wa ‘l-ins, Allāhumma kun lī jāran min sharrihim, jalla thanā'uk, wa `azza jāruk, wa tabāraka-smuk, wa lā ilāha ghayruk.
items.en: Allah is the Most Great, Mightier than all His creation. He is Mightier than what I fear and dread. I seek refuge in Allah, Who there is none worthy of worship but Him. He is the One Who holds the seven heavens from falling upon the earth except by His command. [I seek refuge in You Allah] from the evil of Your slave [name of the person], and his helpers, his followers, and his supporters from among the jinn and mankind. O Allah, be my support against their evil. Glorious are Your praises and mighty is Your patronage. Blessed is Your Name, there is no true God but You. (Recite three times in Arabic.)
items.note: A fuller refuge against the evil of a person you dread, and their helpers, from among the jinn and mankind. It reminds you, before it asks for anything, that Allah is mightier than the very thing you are afraid of.

items.title: O Revealer of the Book, against the gathered foe
items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ مُنْزِلَ الْكِتَاب سَرِيعَ الْحِسَاب اهْزِم الأَحْزَاب اللَّهُمَّ اهْزِمْهُمْ وَ زَلْزِلْهُم
items.translit: Allāhumma munzila ‘l-kitāb, sarī`a ‘l-ḥisāb, ihzimi ‘l-aḥzāb, Allāhumma-hzimhum wa zalzilhum.
items.en: O Allah, Revealer of the Book, Swift to account, defeat the groups (of disbelievers). O Allah, defeat them and shake them.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ called on Allah with these words against the confederates who came against Madinah. A du'a for when forces seem arrayed against the truth.

items.title: Suffice me against them however You wish
items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اكْفِنِيهِم بمَا شِئْت
items.translit: Allāhummak-finīhim bimā shi't.
items.en: O Allah, suffice (i.e. protect) me against them however You wish.
items.note: A short, total handing-over: you do not dictate the how, you simply ask Allah to deal with those who threaten you in whatever way He chooses.

items.title: There is no ease but what You make easy
items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لاَ سَهْلَ إِلاَّ مَا جَعَلْتَهُ سَهْلاً، وَأَنْتَ تَجْعَلُ الْحَزْنَ إِذَا شِئْتَ سَهْلاً
items.translit: Allāhumma lā saḥla illā ma ja`altahu saḥla wa anta taj`alu ‘l-ḥazna idhā shi'ta saḥla.
items.en: O Allah, there is no ease other than what You make easy. If You please You ease sorrow.
items.ref: Ibn Hibban in his Sahih , and Ibn As- Sunni
items.note: For the matter that looks impossible. You admit that ease itself is in Allah's hands, and that He can turn the very hardship you are facing into something soft and passable.

items.title: It is the decree of Allah
items.arabic: قَدَّرَ اللهُ وَما شـاءَ فَعَـل
items.translit: Qaddarallāhu wa mā shā'a fa`al.
items.en: It is the Decree of Allah and He does whatever He wills.
items.note: For when something unwanted has already happened. The Prophet ﷺ taught us to say this rather than 'if only I had...', which only opens the door to regret. It is acceptance, not defeat: the past is in His hands, so you turn your strength toward what is still ahead.

morning-adhkar.name: Morning Adhkar
morning-adhkar.epithet: The remembrances that open your day
morning-adhkar.title: Morning Adhkar: the remembrances to begin your day, with Arabic, transliteration and meaning
morning-adhkar.meta_description: The complete morning adhkar (adhkar as-sabah) in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source and how many times to recite. Qur'an verified via quran.ai, hadith from Hisn al-Muslim.
morning-adhkar.keywords: morning adhkar, adhkar as-sabah, morning dhikr, morning remembrance, Hisn al-Muslim, fortress of the muslim, Ayat al-Kursi morning, what to say in the morning, daily wird, Buruja
lede: There is a short window after Fajr, before the day grabs you, when a handful of words can set the whole day on a different footing. The Prophet ﷺ never let it pass empty. He had words for the morning: praise, protection, and the quiet handing of the day back to the One who owns it.
This is the morning wird, gathered from the Qur'an and the authentic sunnah. Read slowly. You are not ticking a list; you are armouring a day.
morning-adhkar.intro_heading: How to pray the morning wird
intro: The time is from dawn (after Fajr) until sunrise, and if you miss it, the scholars allow you to make it up until around midday. Evening adhkar run from after Asr until sunset, with a little room after. The two sets are almost identical: in the morning you say asbahna (we have entered the morning), in the evening amsayna (we have entered the evening).

You do not have to do all of it from day one. If today feels heavy, take three: Ayat al-Kursi, the three Quls, and Sayyid al-Istighfar. The Prophet ﷺ loved the deed that is small and constant over the large one that burns out. Build from there.

Where a dhikr has a number beside it, that is how many times to say it. The reward is tied to the Arabic words themselves, so recite the Arabic (lean on the transliteration while you learn), and read the meaning so your heart is in the room with your tongue.
closing: None of these words are long. Said with presence, the whole set is ten quiet minutes. But they are ten minutes in which you have asked for protection, admitted your need, thanked the One who carried you through the night, and put the day in hands that will not drop it.

O Allah, let us be among those who remember You at both ends of the day, who begin in Your Name and end in Your praise, and who never meet a morning or an evening that finds the heart forgetful of You.

faq.q: When should I read the morning adhkar?
faq.a: The best time is after the Fajr prayer until sunrise. If you miss that window, most scholars allow you to make them up until around midday. The point is to anchor the start of your day in remembrance, so the earlier the better.

faq.q: Do I have to read every single one every day?
faq.a: No. These are the gathered morning adhkar of the sunnah, not an obligation to complete in full. Start with a few you can keep, for example Ayat al-Kursi, the three Quls, and Sayyid al-Istighfar, and let it grow. The Prophet ﷺ taught that the most beloved deeds to Allah are the most consistent, even if small.

faq.q: Can I just read the English instead of the Arabic?
faq.a: The specific reward of these adhkar is attached to the Arabic words the Prophet ﷺ used, so recite the Arabic (the transliteration is there to help you learn it). Read the meaning alongside it so your heart understands what your tongue is saying.

faq.q: What is the difference between the morning and evening adhkar?
faq.a: They are nearly the same words. The main change is that in the morning you say asbahna and Allahumma bika asbahna (we have entered the morning), and in the evening you swap these for amsayna (we have entered the evening). A few adhkar are specified for only one of the two times.

faq.q: Where do these adhkar come from?
faq.a: From the Qur'an and the authentic sunnah. The Qur'anic portions (Ayat al-Kursi and the three Quls) are verified here against quran.ai, and the prophetic adhkar are taken from the well-known collection Hisn al-Muslim (Fortress of the Muslim) by Sa'eed al-Qahtani, with their references and gradings noted on each.

morning-adhkar.source_note: The Qur'anic adhkar (Ayat al-Kursi, Al-Baqarah 2:255; and the three Quls, Al-Ikhlas 112, Al-Falaq 113, An-Nas 114) are grounded via quran.ai: Arabic in Uthmani script (ar-uthmani-minimal) and the Saheeh International translation. The prophetic adhkar, their transliterations, references and gradings are transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Fortress of the Muslim) by Sa'eed bin Ali al-Qahtani, as hosted on sunnah.com; gradings are as reported there (al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih). The selection and ordering follow the classic morning-adhkar arrangement, with thanks to lifewithallah.com whose dhikr and du'a library prompted this collection. Recitation audio: the Qur'anic items are recited by Mishary Rashid Alafasy (everyayah.com); the prophetic adhkar use the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com), aligned to each dua by its Hisn al-Muslim number. Reflections and framing are Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: please confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

email.subject: The ten minutes that guard the whole day
email.alt_subject: What the Prophet ﷺ said every morning
email.preview: The morning wird, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning, each with its source and count.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

There is a short window after Fajr, before the messages and the to-do list arrive, when a handful of words can change the footing of your whole day. The Prophet ﷺ never let it pass empty. He had words for the morning: praise, a plea for protection, and the quiet handing of the day back to the One who owns it.

We have gathered the morning wird, from the Qur'an and the authentic sunnah, each dhikr with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, its source, and how many times to say it. If today feels heavy, take just three: Ayat al-Kursi, the three Quls, and Sayyid al-Istighfar, the chief way of seeking forgiveness. Build from there.

None of it is long. Said with presence, the whole set is about ten minutes. But they are ten minutes in which you have asked for shelter, admitted your need, thanked the One who carried you through the night, and put the day in hands that will not drop it.

The full wird, formatted to read or recite, is here:

social.hook:
There is a 10-minute window after Fajr that sets the tone for your entire day.

The Prophet ﷺ never let it pass empty.
social.caption:
The morning adhkar, the wird of the believer at the start of the day.

Gathered from the Qur'an and the authentic sunnah: Ayat al-Kursi and the three Quls for protection, Sayyid al-Istighfar (the chief way of seeking forgiveness), the praise that hands the day back to Allah, and the tasbih that is light on the tongue and heavy on the scale.

You do not have to do all of it on day one. Take three and keep them. The most beloved deeds to Allah are the small and the constant.

Save this, and read or recite the full wird with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/morning

#MorningAdhkar #AdhkarAsSabah #Dhikr #Dua #HisnAlMuslim #SunnahOfTheProphet #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

items.title: Ayat al-Kursi
items.arabic: ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْحَىُّ ٱلْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُۥ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُۥٓ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَىْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَـُٔودُهُۥ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْعَظِيمُ
items.en: Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.
items.note: The greatest verse in the Qur'an. The Prophet ﷺ taught that whoever recites it in the morning is guarded until evening, and whoever recites it in the evening is guarded until morning. Say it once, slowly, and let it land.

items.title: The three Quls
items.arabic:
قُلْ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ۝ ٱللَّهُ ٱلصَّمَدُ ۝ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ ۝ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُۥ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌۢ
قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلْفَلَقِ ۝ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ ٱلنَّفَّٰثَٰتِ فِى ٱلْعُقَدِ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ
قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مَلِكِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ إِلَٰهِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مِن شَرِّ ٱلْوَسْوَاسِ ٱلْخَنَّاسِ ۝ ٱلَّذِى يُوَسْوِسُ فِى صُدُورِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ
items.en: Say, "He is Allah, [who is] One, Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, Nor is there to Him any equivalent." / Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak From the evil of that which He created And from the evil of darkness when it settles And from the evil of the blowers in knots And from the evil of an envier when he envies." / Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, The Sovereign of mankind, The God of mankind, From the evil of the retreating whisperer - Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind - From among the jinn and mankind."
items.note: Al-Ikhlas, Al-Falaq and An-Nas, three times each. The Prophet ﷺ said they suffice you against everything. He would cup his hands, recite them with a breath into his palms, and wipe over his body.

items.title: The kingdom is Allah's
items.arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، لاَ إِلَهَ إلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، رَبِّ أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ مَا فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ وَخَيرَ مَا بَعْدَهُ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ وَشَرِّ مَا بَعْدَهُ، رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكَسَلِ وَسُوءِ الْكِبَرِ، رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ فِي النَّارِ وَعَذَابٍ فِي القَـبْر
items.translit: Aṣbaḥnā wa aṣbaḥal-mulku lillāh, walḥamdu lillāh, lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-ḥamd, wa huwa `alā kulli shay'in Qadīr. Rabbi as'aluka khayra mā fī hādha ‘l-yawmi wa khayra mā ba`dahu wa a`ūdhu bika min sharri mā fī hātha ‘l-yawmi wa sharri mā ba`dahu, Rabbi a`ūdhu bika minal-kasali, wa sū'il-kibar, Rabbi a`ūdhu bika min `adhābin fin-nāri wa `adhābin fil-qabr.
items.en: We have entered a new day and with it all dominion is Allah's. Praise is to Allah. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. To Allah belongs the dominion, and to Him is the praise and He is Able to do all things. My Lord, I ask You for the goodness of this day and of the days that come after it, and I seek refuge in You from the evil of this day and of the days that come after it. My Lord, I seek refuge in You from laziness and helpless old age. My Lord, I seek refuge in You from the punishment of Hell-fire, and from the punishment of the grave.

items.title: By You we enter morning and evening
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ بِكَ أَصْـبَحْنا وَبِكَ أَمْسَـينا وَبِكَ نَحْـيا وَبِكَ نَمـوتُ وَإِلَـيْكَ النِّـشور وإذا أمسي فليقل : اللهم بك أمسينا وبك أصبحنا وبك نحيا وبك نموت وإليك المصير
items.translit: Allāhumma bika aṣbaḥnā, wa bika amsaynā, wa bika naḥyā, wa bika namūt, wa ilaykan-nushūr. When you say this in the evening you should say:
items.en: O Allah, by You we enter the morning and by You we enter the evening, by You we live and by You we die, and to You is the Final Return. O Allah, You bring us the end of the day as You bring us its beginning, You bring us life and you bring us death, and to You is our fate.

items.title: Sayyid al-Istighfar
items.arabic: اللّهـمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّـي لا إلهَ إلاّ أَنْتَ خَلَقْتَنـي وَأَنا عَبْـدُك وَأَنا عَلـى عَهْـدِكَ وَوَعْـدِكَ ما اسْتَـطَعْـت أَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَـرِّ ما صَنَـعْت أَبـوءُ لَـكَ بِنِعْـمَتِـكَ عَلَـيَّ وَأَبـوءُ بِذَنْـبي فَاغْفـِرْ لي فَإِنَّـهُ لا يَغْـفِرُ الذُّنـوبَ إِلاّ أَنْتَ
items.translit: Allāhumma anta Rabbī lā ilāha illā ant, khalaqtanī wa anā `abduk, wa anā `alā `ahdika wa wa`dika mastaṭa`t, a`ūdhu bika min sharri mā ṣana`t, abū'u laka bi ni`matika `alay, wa abū'u bidhanbī faghfir lī fa'innahu lā yaghfirudh-dhunūba illā ant.
items.en: O Allah, You are my Lord, there is none worthy of worship but You. You created me and I am your slave. I keep Your covenant, and my pledge to You so far as I am able. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I admit to Your blessings upon me, and I admit to my misdeeds. Forgive me, for there is none who may forgive sins but You.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ called this the chief way of seeking forgiveness: whoever says it by day with certainty and dies before evening is among the people of Paradise, and likewise by night. Slow down for this one.

items.title: Bearing witness before Allah
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّـي أَصْبَـحْتُ أَُشْـهِدُك ، وَأُشْـهِدُ حَمَلَـةَ عَـرْشِـك ، وَمَلائِكَتِك ، وَجَمـيعَ خَلْـقِك ، أَنَّـكَ أَنْـتَ اللهُ لا إلهَ إلاّ أَنْـتَ وَحْـدَكَ لا شَريكَ لَـك ، وَأَنَّ ُ مُحَمّـداً عَبْـدُكَ وَرَسـولُـك
items.translit: Allāhumma innī aṣbaḥtu ush-hiduka wa ush-hidu ḥamalata `arshik, wa malā'ikataka wajamī`a khalqik, annaka antallāhu lā ilāha illā ant, waḥdaka lā sharīka lak, wa anna Muḥammadan `abduka wa rasūluk.
items.en: O Allah, I have entered a new morning and call upon You and upon the bearers of Your Throne, upon Your angels and all creation to bear witness that surely You are Allah, there is none worthy of worship but You alone, You have no partners, and that Muhammad is Your slave and Your Messenger. (Recite four times in Arabic.)

items.title: Every blessing is from You
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ ما أَصْبَـَحَ بي مِـنْ نِعْـمَةٍ أَو بِأَحَـدٍ مِـنْ خَلْـقِك ، فَمِـنْكَ وَحْـدَكَ لا شريكَ لَـك ، فَلَـكَ الْحَمْـدُ وَلَـكَ الشُّكْـر
items.translit: Allāhumma mā aṣbaha bī min ni`matin aw bi aḥadin min khalqik, fa minka waḥdaka lā sharīka lak, falaka ‘l-ḥamdu wa lakash-shukr.
items.en: O Allah, whatever blessing has been received by me or anyone of Your creation is from You alone, You have no partner. All praise is for you and thanks is to You.
items.ref: When you say this in the evening, you should say: Allāhumma mā 'amsā bī...: "O Allah, as I

items.title: Soundness in body, hearing and sight
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ عافِـني في بَدَنـي، اللّهُـمَّ عافِـني في سَمْـعي، اللّهُـمَّ عافِـني في بَصَـري، لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّه أَنْـتَ. اللّهُـمَّ إِنّـي أَعـوذُبِكَ مِنَ الْكُـفر، وَالفَـقْر، وَأَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذابِ القَـبْر، لا إلهَ إلاّ أَنْـتَ
items.translit: Allāhumma `āfinī fī badanī, Allāhumma `āfinī fī sam`ī, Allāhumma `āfinī fī baṣarī, lā ilāha illā ant. Allāhumma innī a`ūdhu bika mina ‘l-kufri, wa ‘l-faqr, wa a`ūdhu bika min `adhābi ‘l-qabr, lā ilāha illā ant.
items.en: O Allah, make me healthy in my body. O Allah, preserve for me my hearing. O Allah, preserve for me my sight. There is none worthy of worship but You. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from disbelief and poverty, and I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the grave. There is none worthy of worship but You (Recite three times in Arabic.)

items.title: Allah is sufficient for me
items.arabic: حَسْبِـيَ اللّهُ لا إلهَ إلاّ هُوَ عَلَـيهِ تَوَكَّـلتُ وَهُوَ رَبُّ العَرْشِ العَظـيم
items.translit: Ḥasbiyallāhu lā ilāha illā huwa `alayhi tawakkalt, wa huwa Rabbu ‘l-`Arshi ‘l-'Aẓīm.
items.en: Allah is sufficient for me. There is none worthy of worship but Him. I have placed my trust in Him, He is Lord of the Majestic Throne. (Recite seven times in Arabic.)
items.note: Seven times, morning and evening. Whoever says it, Allah is enough for him in everything that weighs on him, in this world and the next.

items.title: Forgiveness and shelter, here and hereafter
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّـي أسْـأَلُـكَ العَـفْوَ وَالعـافِـيةَ في الدُّنْـيا وَالآخِـرَة ، اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّـي أسْـأَلُـكَ العَـفْوَ وَالعـافِـيةَ في ديني وَدُنْـيايَ وَأهْـلي وَمالـي ، اللّهُـمَّ اسْتُـرْ عـوْراتي وَآمِـنْ رَوْعاتـي ، اللّهُـمَّ احْفَظْـني مِن بَـينِ يَدَيَّ وَمِن خَلْفـي وَعَن يَمـيني وَعَن شِمـالي ، وَمِن فَوْقـي ، وَأَعـوذُ بِعَظَمَـتِكَ أَن أُغْـتالَ مِن تَحْتـي
items.translit: Allāhumma innī as'aluka 'l-`afwa wal-`āfiyah fid-dunyā wal-ākhirah, Allāhumma innī as'aluka 'l-`afwa wal-`āfiyah fī dīnī wa dunyāya, wa ahlī, wa mālī, Allāhummastur `awrātī, wa āmin raw`ātī, Allāhummaḥfaẓnī min bayni yadayya, wa min khalfī, wa `an yamīnī, wa `an shimālī, wa min fawqī, wa a`ūdhu bi`aẓamatika an 'ughtāla min taḥtī.
items.en: O Allah, I seek Your forgiveness and Your protection in this world and the next. O Allah, I seek Your forgiveness and Your protection in my religion, in my worldly affairs, in my family and in my wealth. O Allah, conceal my secrets and preserve me from anguish. O Allah, guard me from what is in front of me and behind me, from my left, and from my right, and from above me. I seek refuge in Your Greatness from being struck down from beneath me.

items.title: Refuge with the Knower of the unseen
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ عالِـمَ الغَـيْبِ وَالشّـهادَةِ فاطِـرَ السّماواتِ وَالأرْضِ رَبَّ كـلِّ شَـيءٍ وَمَليـكَه ، أَشْهَـدُ أَنْ لا إِلـهَ إِلاّ أَنْت ، أَعـوذُ بِكَ مِن شَـرِّ نَفْسـي وَمِن شَـرِّ الشَّيْـطانِ وَشِـرْكِه ، وَأَنْ أَقْتَـرِفَ عَلـى نَفْسـي سوءاً أَوْ أَجُـرَّهُ إِلـى مُسْـلِم
items.translit: Allāhumma `ālima ‘l-ghaybi wash-shahādah fātir as-samāwāti wa ‘l'arḍ, Rabba kulli shay'in wa malīkah, ash-hadu an lā ilāha illā ant, a`ūdhu bika min sharri nafsī, wa min sharrish-shayṭāni wa shirkih, wa an aqtarifa `alā nafsī sū'an, aw ajurrahu ilā Muslim.
items.en: O Allah, Knower of the unseen and the evident, Maker of the heavens and the earth, Lord of everything and its Possessor, I bear witness that there is none worthy of worship but You. I seek refuge in You from the evil of my soul, and from the evil of Satan and his helpers. (I seek refuge in You) from bringing evil upon my soul and from harming any Muslim.

items.title: The Name with which nothing can harm
items.arabic: بِسـمِ اللهِ الذي لا يَضُـرُّ مَعَ اسمِـهِ شَيءٌ في الأرْضِ وَلا في السّمـاءِ وَهـوَ السّمـيعُ العَلـيم
items.translit: Bismillāhi ‘l-ladhī lā yaḍurru ma`a-smihi shay'un fil-'arḍi wa lā fis-samā' wa huwas-Samī `ul-`Alīm.
items.en: In the Name of Allah, Who with His Name nothing can cause harm in the earth nor in the heavens, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing. (Recite three times in Arabic).
items.ref: Abu Dawud 4/323, At-Tirmidhi 5/465, Ibn Majah 2/332, Ahmad
items.note: Three times. The Prophet ﷺ promised that whoever says it, nothing on earth or in the sky will harm him until he reaches the next morning or evening.

items.title: Pleased with Allah, Islam, and His Messenger ﷺ
items.arabic: رَضيـتُ بِاللهِ رَبَّـاً وَبِالإسْلامِ ديـناً وَبِمُحَـمَّدٍ نَبِيّـاً
items.translit: Raḍītu billāhi Rabba, wa bil-Islāmi dīna, wa bi-Muḥammadin (ṣallallāhu `alayhi wa sallama) nabiyya.
items.en: I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Islam as my religion, and with Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) as my Prophet. (Recite three times in Arabic.)

items.title: O Ever-Living, O Sustainer
items.arabic: يا حَـيُّ يا قَيّـومُ بِـرَحْمَـتِكِ أَسْتَـغـيث ، أَصْلِـحْ لي شَـأْنـي كُلَّـه ، وَلا تَكِلـني إِلى نَفْـسي طَـرْفَةَ عَـين
items.translit: Yā Ḥayyu yā Qayyūmu biraḥmatika astaghīth aṣlih lī sha'nī kullah wa lā takilnī ilā nafsī ṭarfata `ayn.
items.en: O Ever-Living One, O Eternal One, by Your mercy I call on You to set right all my affairs. Do not place me in charge of my soul even for the blinking of an eye (i.e. a moment).

items.title: Upon the dominion of Allah, Lord of all
items.arabic: أَصْبَـحْـنا وَأَصْبَـحْ المُـلكُ للهِ رَبِّ العـالَمـين ، اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّـي أسْـأَلُـكَ خَـيْرَ هـذا الـيَوْم ، فَـتْحَهُ ، وَنَصْـرَهُ ، وَنـورَهُ وَبَـرَكَتَـهُ ، وَهُـداهُ ، وَأَعـوذُ بِـكَ مِـنْ شَـرِّ ما فـيهِ وَشَـرِّ ما بَعْـدَه وإذا أمسى قال: أَمْسَيْـنا وَأَمْسـى المُـلكُ للهِ رَبِّ العـالَمـين ، اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّـي أسْـأَلُـكَ خَـيْرَ هـذهِ اللَّـيْلَة ، فَتْحَهـا ، وَنَصْـرَهـا ، وَنـورَهـا وَبَـرَكَتَـهـا ، وَهُـداهـا ، وَأَعـوذُ بِـكَ مِـنْ شَـرِّ ما فـيهـاِ وَشَـرِّ ما بَعْـدَهـا
items.translit: Aṣbaḥnā wa aṣbaḥal-mulku lillāhi Rabbi ‘l-a`lāmīn, Allāhumma innī as'aluka khayra hādha ‘l-yawm:
items.en: We have entered a new day and with it all the dominion which belongs to Allah, Lord of all that exists. O Allah, I ask You for the goodness of this day,2 its victory, its help, its light, its blessings, and its guidance. I seek refuge in You from the evil that is in it, and from the evil that follows it.

items.title: Upon the fitrah of Islam
items.arabic: أَصْـبَحْنا[أَمْسَـينا] علـى فِطْـرَةِ الإسْلام، وَعَلـى كَلِـمَةِ الإخْـلاص، وَعلـى دينِ نَبِـيِّنا مُحَـمَّدٍ وَعَلـى مِلَّـةِ أبينـا إِبْـراهيـمَ حَنيـفاً مُسْلِـماً وَمـا كـانَ مِنَ المُشـرِكيـن
items.translit: Aṣbahnā `alā fiṭrati ‘l-Islām, wa `alā kalimati ‘l-ikhlās, wa `alā dīni nabiyyinā Muḥammadin (ṣallallāhu `alayhi wa sallam), wa `alā millati abīnā Ibrāhīm, ḥanīfan Musliman wa mā kāna mina ‘l-mushrikīn.
items.en: We have entered a new day upon the natural religion of Islam, the word of sincere devotion, the religion of our Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), and the faith of our father Ibrahim. He was upright (in worshipping Allah), and a Muslim. He was not of those who worship others besides Allah.

items.title: Glory and praise to Allah
items.arabic: سُبْحـانَ اللهِ وَبِحَمْـدِهِ
items.translit: Subḥānallāhi wa biḥamdih.
items.en: Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him. (Recite one hundred times in Arabic).
items.note: A hundred times. The Prophet ﷺ said no one will arrive on the Day of Judgement with anything better, except someone who said the same or more. Light on the tongue, heavy on the scale.

items.title: There is no god but Allah, alone
items.arabic: لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وحْـدَهُ لا شَـريكَ لهُ، لهُ المُـلْكُ ولهُ الحَمْـد، وهُوَ على كُلّ شَيءٍ قَدير
items.translit: Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu ‘l-mulku walahu ‘l-ḥamd, wa huwa `alā kulli shay'in qadīr.
items.en: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise and He is Able to do all things. (Recite ten times in Arabic or one time to ward off laziness.)

items.title: There is no god but Allah, a hundredfold
items.arabic: لا إلهَ إلاّ اللّهُ وحْـدَهُ لا شَـريكَ لهُ، لهُ المُـلْكُ ولهُ الحَمْـد، وهُوَ على كُلّ شَيءٍ قَدير
items.translit: Lā ilāha illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lah, lahu ‘l-mulku walahu ‘l-ḥamd, wa huwa `alā kulli shay'in qadīr.
items.en: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise and He is Able to do all things. (Recite 100 times in Arabic upon rising in the morning).

items.title: Glory to Allah, by the number of His creation
items.arabic: سُبْحـانَ اللهِ وَبِحَمْـدِهِ عَدَدَ خَلْـقِه ، وَرِضـا نَفْسِـه ، وَزِنَـةَ عَـرْشِـه ، وَمِـدادَ كَلِمـاتِـه
items.translit: Subḥānallāhi wa biḥamdih:
items.en: Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him, by the multitude of His creation, by His Pleasure, by the weight of His Throne, and by the extent of His Words. (Recite three times in Arabic upon rising in the morning.)

items.title: Asking for beneficial knowledge
items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْماً نَافِعاً، وَرِزْقاً طَيِّباً، وَعَمَلاً مُتَقَبَّلاً
items.translit: Allāhumma innī as'aluka `ilman nāfi`a, wa rizqan ṭayyiba, wa `amalan mutaqabbala.
items.en: O Allah, I ask You for knowledge that is of benefit, a good provision, and deeds that will be accepted. (Recite in Arabic upon rising in the morning.)
items.ref: Ibn As-Sunni, no. 54, Ibn Majah no. 925. Its chain of transmission is good (Hasan), Ibn Al-Qayyim 2/375

items.title: I seek Allah's forgiveness and turn to Him
items.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ
items.translit: Astaghfirullāha wa atūbu ilayh.
items.en: I seek the forgiveness of Allah and repent to Him. (Recite one hundred times in Arabic during the day.)

items.title: Refuge in the perfect words of Allah
items.arabic: أَعـوذُ بِكَلِمـاتِ اللّهِ التّـامّـاتِ مِنْ شَـرِّ ما خَلَـق
items.translit: A`ūdhu bikalimāti-llāhit-tāmmāti min sharri mā khalaq.
items.en: I seek refuge in the Perfect Words of Allah from the evil of what He has created. (Recite three times in Arabic in the evening.)

items.title: Blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ
items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ وَ سَلِّمْ عَلَى نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ
items.translit: Allāhumma ṣalli wa sallim `alā nabiyyinā Muḥammad.
items.en: O Allah, we ask for your peace and blessings upon our Prophet Muhammad. (Recite ten times in Arabic.)
items.note: Ten times in the morning and ten in the evening. Whoever sends blessings on the Prophet ﷺ this way will be reached by his intercession on the Day of Resurrection.

nature-weather.name: Nature & Weather
nature-weather.epithet: The remembrances for wind, rain, and the turning sky
nature-weather.title: Nature & Weather Du'as: what to say for wind, thunder, rain and the new moon, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
nature-weather.meta_description: Authentic du'as for wind, thunder, seeking rain, when it rains, after rain, and the new moon, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source.
nature-weather.keywords: dua for rain, dua for wind, dua when it thunders, istisqa dua, dua for the new moon, dua after rain, weather dua, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: The weather was never just weather to the Prophet ﷺ. A wind that picked up, a roll of thunder, the first heavy drops on the roof, the thin curve of a new moon over the rooftops: each one moved him to turn to Allah. Not with worry, but with words. He read the sky as a message and answered it with du'a.

These are those answers. Short lines for the moments the natural world reaches into your day and asks to be noticed. Keep them close, and the ordinary sky becomes a reason to remember the One who holds it up.
intro: There is one for each moment: when the wind rises, when thunder sounds, when you need rain and when too much has come, the moment the first drops fall, the words to say once the rain has passed, and the greeting for the new moon. Reach for whichever the sky hands you.
Most are a single line. Say them in Arabic where you can, lean on the transliteration while you learn, and read the meaning so your heart understands what your tongue is doing.
closing: A storm can make a heart small with fear or large with remembrance, and these words tip it toward the second. The Prophet ﷺ would change colour when the wind blew hard, afraid it carried punishment, and beg for its good while seeking refuge from its harm. That is the posture: never indifferent to the sky, always turning to the One above it.
O Allah, You send the wind and the rain and raise the moon over us. Make every weather a caller to Your remembrance, give us its good, and shelter us from its harm.

faq.q: What do I say when it is raining?
faq.a: The sunnah is to say Allahumma sayyiban nafi'a, asking Allah to make it beneficial rain. Rain is a time when du'a is answered, so it is also a moment to ask Allah for whatever you need.

faq.q: Is there a du'a for thunder?
faq.a: When Abdullah ibn az-Zubayr heard thunder he would stop speaking and glorify Allah, Whom the thunder and the angels praise out of awe of Him. It is reported as his practice, and many keep it when thunder rolls.

faq.q: What should I say when I want it to rain, or to stop?
faq.a: To seek rain there are the du'as of istisqa, asking Allah for beneficial rain that is swift and not delayed. When the rain becomes too heavy, the Prophet ﷺ asked Allah to send it around them and not upon them, onto the hills, valleys and pastures instead.

faq.q: Why greet the new moon with a du'a?
faq.a: A new month is a fresh page. The Prophet ﷺ welcomed each new moon by asking Allah to bring it in with security, faith, peace and safety, and in harmony with what Allah loves and is pleased with.

email.subject: What the Prophet ﷺ said when the sky changed
email.alt_subject: Du'as for wind, thunder, and rain
email.preview: Short remembrances for the wind, thunder, rain, and the new moon, each with its source.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

The weather was never just weather to the Prophet ﷺ. A wind that picked up, a roll of thunder, the first drops on the roof, the thin new moon over the rooftops: each one turned him to Allah, not with worry but with words.

We have gathered those words, the short du'as for the moments the natural world reaches into your day: what to say when the wind rises, when thunder sounds, when you need rain and when too much has come, when the first drops fall, after the rain passes, and when you greet a new moon. Each one comes with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source.

Learn one or two and the ordinary sky becomes a reason to remember the One who holds it up. A storm can make a heart small with fear or large with remembrance; these words tip it toward the second.

The full collection, formatted to read or recite, is here:

social.hook:
The Prophet ﷺ never saw weather as just weather.

Wind, thunder, rain, a new moon: each one turned him to Allah.
social.caption:
Du'as for nature and weather, the words the Prophet ﷺ said when the sky changed.

What to say when the wind rises and when thunder rolls, how to ask for rain and how to ask when there is too much of it, the words for the first drops and for after the rain passes, and the greeting for a new moon.

Most are a single line. Learn one or two and the ordinary sky becomes a reason to remember the One who holds it up.

Save this, and read the full collection with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/nature-weather

#DuaForRain #Istisqa #Dhikr #Dua #HisnAlMuslim #SunnahOfTheProphet #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

nature-weather.source_note: Prophetic adhkar transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com; al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih, jayyid/reliable as hasan. Recitation audio: the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com) for the adhkar. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com; framing is Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

items.title: When the wind blows
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنَّـي أَسْـأَلُـكَ خَيْـرَها، وَأَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَـرِّها
items.translit: Allāhumma innī as'aluka khayrahā, wa a`ūdhu bika min sharrihā.
items.en: O Allah, I ask You for the good of it, and seek refuge in You against its evil.
items.note: When the wind picked up, the Prophet ﷺ would ask Allah for its good and seek refuge from its harm, afraid it might carry punishment. A short line for the moment a gust unsettles you.

items.title: When the wind blows (the fuller du'a)
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنَّـي أَسْـأَلُـكَ خَيْـرَها، وَخَيْـرَ ما فيهـا، وَخَيْـرَ ما اُرْسِلَـتْ بِه، وَأَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَـرِّها، وَشَـرِّ ما فيهـا، وَشَـرِّ ما اُرْسِلَـتْ بِه
items.translit: Allāhumma innī as'aluka khayrahā, wa khayra mā fīhā, wa khayra mā ursilat bih, wa a`ūdhu bika min sharrihā, wa sharri mā fīhā, wa sharri mā ursilat bih.
items.en: O Allah, I ask You for the good of it, for the good of what it contains, and for the good of what is sent with it. I seek refuge in You from the evil of it, from the evil of what it contains, and from the evil that is sent with it.
items.note: A longer version: asking for the good of the wind, the good within it, and the good of what it was sent with, and seeking refuge from the matching evils.

items.title: When you hear thunder
items.arabic: سُبْـحانَ الّذي يُسَبِّـحُ الـرَّعْدُ بِحَمْـدِهِ، وَالملائِكـةُ مِنْ خيـفَته
items.translit: Subḥāna ‘l-ladhī yusabbiḥur-ra`du bi ḥamdihi wa ‘l-malā'ikatu min khīfatih.
items.en: Glory is to Him Whom thunder and angels glorify due to fear of Him.
items.ref: Whenever Abdullah bin Zubair (RA) would hear thunder, he would abandon all conversation and say this supplication
items.note: Reported as the practice of Abdullah ibn az-Zubayr (a Companion): when he heard thunder he would leave off speaking and say this, glorifying the One whom even the thunder extols.

items.title: Asking for rain
items.arabic: اللّهُمَّ اسْقِـنا غَيْـثاً مُغيـثاً مَريئاً مُريـعاً، نافِعـاً غَيْـرَ ضار، عاجِـلاً غَـيْرَ آجِل
items.translit: Allāhumma ‘asqinā ghaythan mughīthan marī'an murī`a, nāfi`an ghayra ḍārr, `ājilan ghayra ājil.
items.en: O Allah, shower upon us abundant rain, beneficial not harmful, swiftly and not delayed.
items.note: A du'a of istisqa: asking Allah for abundant, beneficial rain that comes swiftly and is not delayed.

items.title: O Allah, send us rain
items.arabic: اللّهُمَّ أغِثْنـا، اللّهُمَّ أغِثْنـا، اللّهُمَّ أغِثْنـا
items.translit: Allāhumma aghithnā, Allāhumma aghithnā, Allāhumma aghithnā.
items.en: O Allah, send us rain. O Allah, send us rain. O Allah, send us rain.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said this three times when the people came to him in drought, raising his hands in plea.

items.title: Rain for Your servants and Your land
items.arabic: اللّهُمَّ اسْقِ عِبادَكَ وَبَهـائِمَك، وَانْشُـرْ رَحْمَـتَكَ وَأَحْيِي بَلَـدَكَ المَيِّـت
items.translit: Allāhumma’sqi `ibādaka wa bahā'imak, wanshur raḥmatak, wa aḥyi baladaka ‘l-mayyit
items.en: O Allah, give water to Your slaves, and Your livestock, and spread Your mercy, and revive Your dead land.
items.note: Asking Allah to water His servants and their livestock, spread His mercy, and bring the dead earth back to life.

items.title: When the rain begins
items.arabic: اللّهُمَّ صَيِّـباً نافِـعاً
items.translit: Allāhumma ṣayyiban nāfi`a.
items.en: O Allah, (bring) beneficial rain clouds.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said this as the rain started falling, asking that it be made beneficial. Rain is a time when du'a is answered, so ask freely.

items.title: After the rain falls
items.arabic: مُطِـرْنا بِفَضْـلِ اللهِ وَرَحْمَـتِه
items.translit: Muṭirnā bifaḍlillāhi wa raḥmatih.
items.en: We have been given rain by the grace and mercy of Allah.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught us to credit the rain to Allah's grace and mercy, not to the stars or the season, returning every blessing to its true Giver.

items.title: When the rain is too heavy
items.arabic: اللّهُمَّ حَوالَيْنا وَلا عَلَيْـنا، اللّهُمَّ عَلى الآكـامِ وَالظِّـراب، وَبُطـونِ الأوْدِية، وَمَنـابِتِ الشَّجـر
items.translit: Allāhumma ḥawālaynā wa lā `alaynā. Allāhumma `ala ‘l-ākāmi waẓ-ẓirāb, wa buṭūni ‘l-awdiyati, wa manābitish-shajar.
items.en: O Allah, let the rain fall around us and not upon us, O Allah, (let it fall) on the pastures, hills, valleys, and the roots of trees.
items.note: When the downpour threatened harm, the Prophet ﷺ did not ask for it to stop, but to be turned aside onto the hills, valleys and pastures, away from the people.

items.title: On sighting the new moon
items.arabic: اللهُ أَكْـبَر، اللّهُمَّ أَهِلَّـهُ عَلَيْـنا بِالأمْـنِ وَالإيمـان، والسَّلامَـةِ والإسْلام، وَالتَّـوْفيـقِ لِما تُحِـبُّ رَبَّنـا وَتَـرْضـى، رَبُّنـا وَرَبُّكَ الله
items.translit: Allāhu Akbar, Allāhumma ahillahu `alayna bi ‘l-amni wa ‘l-īmān, was-salāmati wa ‘l-'Islām, wat-tawfīqi limā tuḥibbu Rabbanā wa tarḍā, Rabbunā wa Rabbukallāh.
items.en: Allah is the Most Great. O Allah, bring us the new moon with security and Faith, with peace and in Islam, and in harmony with what our Lord loves and what pleases Him. Our Lord and your Lord is Allah.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ greeted each new moon with this, welcoming the new month with security, faith and peace, and in harmony with what Allah loves.

quranic-duas.name: Qur'anic Du'as
quranic-duas.epithet: The supplications Allah taught in His Book
quranic-duas.title: Qur'anic Du'as: the Rabbana supplications of the Qur'an, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
quranic-duas.meta_description: The du'as of the Qur'an (the Rabbana supplications): for good in both worlds, steadfast prayer, a righteous family, forgiveness and more. Arabic and Saheeh International, verified via quran.ai.
quranic-duas.keywords: quranic duas, rabbana dua, rabbana atina, dua of yunus, dua of musa, dua of adam, supplications in the quran, rabbana hab lana, Buruja
lede: When Allah wanted to teach us how to ask, He put the words in His own Book. The Qur'an is full of du'as: the cry of Adam after he slipped, of Yunus from inside the whale, of Ibrahim for his children, of ordinary believers asking for good in both worlds.
To make these du'as is to ask Allah in the very words He chose. There is no safer way to speak to Him than with the speech He revealed.
intro: These are not tied to a time. Reach for them in sujood, after prayer, in the last third of the night, or whenever your heart needs words. Many begin with 'Rabbana' (Our Lord) or 'Rabbi' (My Lord), the gentle, intimate way the Qur'an teaches us to open.
Each card shows the supplication from its verse, with the meaning and the reference. The recitation plays the full ayah. Learn a few by heart; they will come to you exactly when you need them.
quranic-duas.closing_heading: Why we ask in His words
closing: Every one of these du'as was answered. Adam was forgiven, Yunus was lifted from the dark, Musa was given work and a home and a family within days of his prayer. When you borrow their words, you join a line of the answered stretching back to the first human being.
Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the next, and protect us from the Fire. Our Lord, accept it from us; indeed You are the Hearing, the Knowing.

faq.q: What are the Qur'anic du'as?
faq.a: They are the supplications that appear in the Qur'an itself, many beginning with 'Rabbana' (Our Lord). They include the du'a of Adam, of Yunus in the whale, of Ibrahim, of Musa, and the prayers of the believers for good in both worlds, for steadfastness, for a righteous family and for forgiveness.

faq.q: Why make du'a in the words of the Qur'an?
faq.a: Because they are the words Allah Himself chose and praised. Asking with them is asking in speech that is guaranteed to be pure, complete and beloved to Him. They are also easy to memorise and stay with you for life.

faq.q: What is the most comprehensive du'a in the Qur'an?
faq.a: Many scholars point to 'Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanah wa fil-akhirati hasanah wa qina adhab an-nar' (2:201), 'Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter and protect us from the Fire.' The Prophet ﷺ made this du'a more than any other.

email.subject: Ask Allah in His own words
email.alt_subject: The du'as of the Qur'an
email.preview: The Rabbana supplications: Adam, Yunus, Ibrahim, Musa, and the believers.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

When Allah wanted to teach us how to ask, He put the words in His own Book. The Qur'an is full of du'as: the cry of Adam after he slipped, of Yunus from inside the whale, of Ibrahim for his children, of ordinary believers asking for good in both worlds.

Every one of them was answered. Adam was forgiven. Yunus was lifted from the dark. Musa, who prayed alone and broke with nothing, had work and a home and a wife within days. To borrow their words is to join a line of the answered stretching back to the first human being.

We have gathered the Rabbana supplications here, each with Arabic, meaning, reference and recitation. Learn a few; they will come to you exactly when you need them:

social.hook:
When Allah wanted to teach us how to ask...

He put the words in His own Book.
social.caption:
The du'as of the Qur'an.

The cry of Adam after he slipped. Of Yunus from inside the whale. Of Ibrahim for his children. Of Musa, who prayed alone and broke, and had a home within days.

Every one was answered. To ask Allah in His own words is the safest way there is to speak to Him.

Full set with Arabic, meaning and recitation at buruja.com/adhkar/quranic-duas

#QuranicDuas #Rabbana #Dua #Quran #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

quranic-duas.source_note: Every supplication is grounded via quran.ai: Arabic in Uthmani script (ar-uthmani-minimal) and the Saheeh International translation. The recitable supplication shown on each card is selected from its verse (the supplication clause to the end of the ayah); this is a verified substring of the fetched ayah, never a rewrite, and the recitation audio (Mishary Rashid Alafasy, everyayah.com) plays the full ayah. With thanks to lifewithallah.com; framing is Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm each verse selection before publication.

items.title: Good in this world and the next
items.arabic: رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِنَا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِى ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ
items.en: Our Lord, give us in this world [that which is] good and in the Hereafter [that which is] good and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
items.note: The du'a the Prophet ﷺ made more than any other.

items.title: Patience and steadfastness
items.arabic: رَبَّنَآ أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَٱنصُرْنَا عَلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْكَٰفِرِينَ
items.en: Our Lord, pour upon us patience and plant firmly our feet and give us victory over the disbelieving people.

items.title: Keep my heart from going astray
items.arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً ۚ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْوَهَّابُ
items.en: Our Lord, let not our hearts deviate after You have guided us and grant us from Yourself mercy. Indeed, You are the Bestower.
items.note: A du'a for the gift you can lose: guidance itself.

items.title: We have believed, so forgive us
items.arabic: رَبَّنَآ إِنَّنَآ ءَامَنَّا فَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ
items.en: Those who say, 'Our Lord, indeed we have believed, so forgive us our sins and protect us from the punishment of the Fire,'

items.title: The du'a of Adam, turning back after sin
items.arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَآ أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَٰسِرِينَ
items.en: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers.
items.note: The words Adam said after he slipped. Carry them when you fall.

items.title: Make me steadfast in prayer, the du'a of Ibrahim
items.arabic: رَبِّ ٱجْعَلْنِى مُقِيمَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِى ۚ رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَآءِ رَبَّنَا ٱغْفِرْ لِى وَلِوَٰلِدَىَّ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلْحِسَابُ
items.en: My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer, and [many] from my descendants. Our Lord, and accept my supplication. Our Lord, forgive me and my parents and the believers the Day the account is established.

items.title: Increase me in knowledge
items.arabic: رَّبِّ زِدْنِى عِلْمًا
items.en: My Lord, increase me in knowledge.
items.note: The only thing Allah told His Prophet ﷺ to ask for more of.

items.title: The du'a of Yunus, from the depths
items.arabic: لَّآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنتَ سُبْحَٰنَكَ إِنِّى كُنتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
items.en: There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said no Muslim ever supplicates with it for anything but that Allah answers him.

items.title: Forgive us and have mercy
items.arabic: رَبَّنَآ ءَامَنَّا فَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَٱرْحَمْنَا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ
items.en: Indeed, there was a party of My servants who said, 'Our Lord, we have believed, so forgive us and have mercy upon us, and You are the best of the merciful.'

items.title: Coolness of the eyes, for spouse and children
items.arabic: رَبَّنَا هَبْ لَنَا مِنْ أَزْوَٰجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّٰتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٍ وَٱجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
items.en: Our Lord, grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us a leader for the righteous.

items.title: In need of whatever good You send, the du'a of Musa
items.arabic: رَبِّ إِنِّى لِمَآ أَنزَلْتَ إِلَىَّ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَقِيرٌ
items.en: My Lord, indeed I am, for whatever good You would send down to me, in need.
items.note: Musa said it alone, broke, and a stranger with nothing. Days later he had work, a home and a wife.

items.title: For those who came before us in faith
items.arabic: رَبَّنَا ٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَلِإِخْوَٰنِنَا ٱلَّذِينَ سَبَقُونَا بِٱلْإِيمَٰنِ وَلَا تَجْعَلْ فِى قُلُوبِنَا غِلًّا لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
items.en: Our Lord, forgive us and our brothers who preceded us in faith and put not in our hearts [any] resentment toward those who have believed. Our Lord, indeed You are Kind and Merciful.

items.title: Complete our light
items.arabic: رَبَّنَآ أَتْمِمْ لَنَا نُورَنَا وَٱغْفِرْ لَنَآ ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
items.en: Our Lord, perfect for us our light and forgive us. Indeed, You are over all things competent.

home.name: Leaving & Entering the Home
home.title: Leaving & Entering the Home: the du'as to say at your door, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
home.meta_description: The du'as for leaving and entering your home, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source. Step out in Allah's protection and return in His Name.
home.keywords: dua leaving home, dua entering home, dua before leaving house, dua coming home, Bismillah tawakkaltu, home dua, protection dua, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: The door of your home is a threshold you cross many times a day, often without noticing. The Prophet ﷺ taught us not to cross it empty-handed: a few words on the way out place the whole journey in Allah's keeping, and a few on the way in shut the door on the devil behind you.
These are the du'as for leaving and returning. Short enough to say in the time it takes to find your keys, and weighty enough to change how the moment is met.
intro: Say the leaving du'a as you step out of your home, and the entering du'a as you come back in. The sunnah adds that you greet those inside with salam as you enter, and that mentioning Allah's Name at the door leaves no place for the devil to lodge for the night.
closing: When you leave with these words, the Prophet ﷺ said it is announced for you: you are guided, sufficed and protected, and the devil turns away from a person so guarded. Few sentences carry that much shelter.
O Allah, guard our going out and our coming in, and let our homes be houses in which Your Name is remembered.

faq.q: What do I say when I leave my house?
faq.a: Say the du'a of leaving, placing your trust in Allah and acknowledging that there is no power except by Him. The Prophet ﷺ taught that whoever says it is told: you are guided, defended and protected, and the devil is turned away from him.

faq.q: What do I say when I come home?
faq.a: Say the du'a of entering, mentioning the Name of Allah, and greet those inside with salam. When a person mentions Allah's Name on entering, the devil finds no place to spend the night in that home.

faq.q: Do I need to say these out loud?
faq.a: A gentle voice is good, especially the salam to your household, but the heart is what matters. Say them with awareness as you cross the threshold, even quietly, and the sunnah is kept.

email.subject: The words for your doorway, coming and going
email.alt_subject: What to say when you leave and enter your home
email.preview: The du'as for leaving and entering your home, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

The door of your home is a threshold you cross many times a day, usually without a thought. The Prophet ﷺ taught us not to cross it empty-handed: a few words on the way out place the whole journey in Allah's keeping, and a few on the way in shut the door on the devil behind you.

We have gathered the du'as for leaving and returning, each with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source. They are short enough to say while you find your keys, and weighty enough to change how the moment is met.

Leave in His protection, return in His Name, and greet your household with peace:

social.hook:
There are words for the moment you walk out your front door.

Say them, and you step out guided, sufficed and protected.
social.caption:
The du'as for leaving and entering your home, words for a threshold you cross every single day.

The Prophet ﷺ said that whoever leaves with the du'a of trust is told: you are guided, defended and protected, and the devil turns away from him. And mentioning Allah's Name as you enter leaves the devil no place to lodge for the night.

Save this, and read the full set with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/home

#DuaForLeavingHome #HomeDua #Protection #Dhikr #Dua #HisnAlMuslim #SunnahOfTheProphet #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

home.source_note: Prophetic adhkar transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com; al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih, jayyid/reliable as hasan. Recitation audio: the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com) for the adhkar. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com; framing is Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

items.title: Leaving the home
items.arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ ، تَوَكَّلْـتُ عَلى اللهِ وَلا حَوْلَ وَلا قُـوَّةَ إِلاّ بِالله
items.translit: Bismillāhi, tawakkaltu `alallāhi, wa lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata illā billāh.
items.en: In the Name of Allah, I have placed my trust in Allah, there is no might and no power except by Allah.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said whoever says this on leaving is told: you are guided, sufficed and protected, and the devil withdraws from him.

items.title: Refuge from misguiding and being wronged
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّـي أَعـوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أَضِـلَّ أَوْ أُضَـل ، أَوْ أَزِلَّ أَوْ أُزَل ، أَوْ أَظْلِـمَ أَوْ أَُظْلَـم ، أَوْ أَجْهَلَ أَوْ يُـجْهَلَ عَلَـيّ
items.translit: Allāhumma 'innī 'a`ūdhu bika 'an 'aḍilla, 'aw 'uḍalla, 'aw 'azilla, 'aw 'uzalla, 'aw 'aẓlima, 'aw 'uẓlama, 'aw 'ajhala 'aw yujhala `alayya.
items.en: O Allah, I seek refuge in You lest I misguide others, or I am misguided by others, lest I cause others to err or I am caused to err, lest I abuse others or be abused, and lest I behave foolishly or meet with the foolishness of others.
items.note: A second du'a for setting out, seeking refuge from straying or being led astray, from wronging or being wronged, and from acting or being treated foolishly.

items.title: Entering the home
items.arabic: بِسْـمِ اللهِ وَلَجْنـا، وَبِسْـمِ اللهِ خَـرَجْنـا، وَعَلـى رَبِّنـا تَوَكّلْـن
items.translit: Bismillāhi walajnā, wa bismillāhi kharajnā, wa `ala Rabbinā tawakkalnā
items.en: In the Name of Allah we enter, in the Name of Allah we leave, and upon our Lord we depend [then say As-Salāmu `Alaykum to those present].
items.note: Mention Allah's Name on entering and greet your household with salam. When His Name is said at the door, the devil finds no shelter inside for the night.

wudu-bathroom.name: Wudu & Bathroom
wudu-bathroom.epithet: Remembrance at the edge of purity
wudu-bathroom.title: Wudu & Bathroom Adhkar: what to say entering and leaving the bathroom and around wudu, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
wudu-bathroom.meta_description: Short du'as for entering and leaving the bathroom, and the words to say before and after wudu, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source.
wudu-bathroom.keywords: bathroom dua, wudu dua, dua entering bathroom, dua leaving bathroom, dua before wudu, dua after wudu, Bismillah wudu, ablution dhikr, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Some of the smallest moments of the day are the ones we pass through without a thought: stepping into the bathroom, turning on the tap, finishing a wash. The Prophet ﷺ filled even these with the Name of Allah, so that no part of the day was lived heedless of Him.
These are the words for the threshold of the bathroom and for the act of wudu. A few syllables, but they turn an ordinary routine into worship and ask for shelter where the heart is most exposed.
intro: Say the entering du'a before you step in, and the leaving du'a once you are out. Say Bismillah as you begin to wash, and the words of witness once your wudu is complete. The bathroom du'as are said outside the bathroom itself, or silently within the heart while inside.
closing: Wudu is not only washing; it is the light a believer carries to the prayer, and the sunnah is that minor sins fall away with the water. To begin it with His Name and seal it with the testimony of faith is to make the whole act count twice.
O Allah, make us among those who are purified, and let no corner of our day be empty of Your remembrance.

faq.q: Do I recite the bathroom du'as inside the bathroom?
faq.a: No. Say the entering du'a just before you step in, and the leaving du'a as soon as you come out. Inside, the Name of Allah is not spoken aloud, so these words belong at the door, not within.

faq.q: What do I say before wudu?
faq.a: Begin with Bismillah (In the Name of Allah) as you start to wash. When you have finished, say the testimony of faith, and the sunnah du'a asking to be made among those who repent and those who are purified.

faq.q: Why say a du'a for something as ordinary as wudu?
faq.a: Because the Prophet ﷺ taught us that nothing is too ordinary for the remembrance of Allah. Beginning wudu with His Name and ending it with the shahadah turns a daily routine into an act of worship that lifts the soul along with the body.

email.subject: The words for the smallest moments of your day
email.alt_subject: What to say around wudu and the bathroom
email.preview: Short du'as for the bathroom and for wudu, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

We pass through some moments of the day without a second thought: stepping into the bathroom, turning on the tap, finishing a wash. The Prophet ﷺ filled even these with the Name of Allah, so that no part of his day was lived forgetful of Him.

We have gathered the short du'as for the threshold of the bathroom and for the act of wudu, each with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source. They are only a few words, but they turn an ordinary routine into worship and ask for shelter where the heart is most exposed.

Begin wudu with His Name, seal it with the testimony of faith, and let the light of it carry you to the prayer:

social.hook:
There is a du'a for stepping into the bathroom.

The Prophet ﷺ left no part of the day empty of Allah's Name.
social.caption:
The adhkar of the bathroom and of wudu, the words for the smallest moments of your day.

A du'a for entering, a du'a for leaving, the Name of Allah to begin your wash, and the testimony of faith to seal it. Wudu is the light a believer carries to the prayer, and beginning it this way makes the whole act count twice.

Save this, and read the full set with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/wudu-bathroom

#WuduDua #BathroomDua #Ablution #Dhikr #Dua #HisnAlMuslim #SunnahOfTheProphet #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

items.title: Entering the bathroom
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ إِنِّـي أَعـوذُ بِـكَ مِـنَ الْخُـبْثِ وَالْخَبائِث
items.translit: [Bismillāhi] Allāhumma 'innī 'a`ūdhu bika minal-khubthi walkhabā'ith.
items.en: [In the Name of Allah]. O Allah, I seek protection in You from evil and the evil ones (or, from the evil male and female Jinn).
items.note: Said before you step in. The Prophet ﷺ sought refuge in Allah from harm at the very threshold, knowing these are places where the soul is exposed.

items.title: Leaving the bathroom
items.arabic: غُفْـرانَك
items.translit: Ghufrānaka
items.en: I seek Your forgiveness.
items.ref: Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah and At-Tirmidhi
items.note: A single word, Ghufranaka, asking forgiveness. A beautiful sunnah: having been relieved of harm, the first thing on the tongue is a plea for pardon.

items.title: Before wudu
items.arabic: بِسْمِ الله
items.translit: Bismillāhi
items.en: In the Name of Allah
items.ref: Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah, and Ahmad
items.note: Begin your wash with the Name of Allah, and the whole act becomes worship.

items.title: The testimony after wudu
items.arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لا إِلَـهَ إِلاّ اللهُ وَحْدَهُ لا شَريـكَ لَـهُ وَأَشْهَدُأَنَّ مُحَمّـداً عَبْـدُهُ وَرَسـولُـه
items.translit: 'Ash-hadu 'an lā 'ilāha 'illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lahu wa 'ash-hadu 'anna Muḥammadan `abduhu wa Rasūluhu.
items.en: I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner; and I bear witness that Muhammad is His slave and His Messenger.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said that whoever completes his wudu well and then says this will have the eight gates of Paradise opened for him, to enter by whichever he wishes.

items.title: Among the repentant and the purified
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ اجْعَلنـي مِنَ التَّـوّابينَ وَاجْعَـلْني مِنَ المتَطَهّـرين
items.translit: Allāhummaj`alnī minat-tawwābīna waj`alnī minal-mutaṭahhirīn.
items.en: O Allah, make me among those who turn to You in repentance, and make me among those who are purified.
items.note: A second sunnah du'a after wudu, asking to be counted with those who turn back to Allah and those whom He keeps clean.

items.title: Glory and praise after wudu
items.arabic: سُبْحـانَكَ اللّهُـمَّ وَبِحَمدِك أَشْهَـدُ أَنْ لا إِلهَ إِلاّ أَنْتَ أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتوبُ إِلَـيْك
items.translit: Subḥānaka Allāhumma wa biḥamdika, 'ash-hadu 'an lā 'ilāha 'illā 'Anta, 'astaghfiruka wa 'atūbu 'ilayk.
items.en: Glory is to You, O Allah, and praise; I bear witness that there is none worthy of worship but You. I seek Your forgiveness and turn to You in repentance.
items.note: The words of tasbih and seeking forgiveness, reported as a seal upon the wudu, written and kept until the Day of Resurrection.

ruqyah-illness.name: Ruqyah & Illness
ruqyah-illness.epithet: Healing words for the sick, for your own pain, and against the evil eye
ruqyah-illness.title: Ruqyah for Illness: healing du'as for the sick and the Qur'anic ruqyah, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
ruqyah-illness.meta_description: The ruqyah for illness: al-Fatihah, Ayat al-Kursi and the three Quls, with the prophetic du'as for the sick, for your own pain, and against the evil eye. Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
ruqyah-illness.keywords: ruqyah, ruqyah for illness, dua for the sick, dua for healing, dua for pain, evil eye dua, al-Fatihah cure, Ayat al-Kursi, the three Quls, self ruqyah, Buruja
lede: When illness comes, to you or to someone you love, the believer does two things at once: takes the medicine, and turns to the One who made the medicine work. Seeking a cure is not a lack of trust in Allah; the Prophet ﷺ told us to seek treatment, for Allah has sent down a cure for every illness. These words are that turning. They sit alongside the doctor, never instead of them.

Ruqyah simply means seeking refuge and healing in the words of Allah. This collection opens with its heart: al-Fatihah, Ayat al-Kursi, and the three Quls, the Qur'an the Prophet ﷺ and his Companions used to heal. After them come his du'as for visiting the sick, for your own aches and pains, and against the evil eye. Read them over yourself, or over the one who is unwell.
intro: Begin with the Qur'an: recite al-Fatihah, then Ayat al-Kursi, then the three Quls (al-Ikhlas, al-Falaq and an-Nas) three times each. The sunnah is to cup your hands, recite into them, and wipe over the body, or over the part that hurts, beginning with the head and face. You can do this for yourself or, with gentle permission, for someone who is ill.

Then make the du'as that follow. The Arabic words carry the healing, so recite them in Arabic and lean on the transliteration as you learn. And remember the spirit behind ruqyah: it is sincere reliance on Allah, not a transaction. The cure is from Him; the words are how we ask.
closing: Recite these with the certainty that the One you are asking can lift any sickness in an instant, and with the surrender to accept whatever He decrees as good for you. Sometimes the cure is recovery. Sometimes the healing is patience, and a quiet washing-away of sins, for the Prophet ﷺ said no fatigue, illness or even the prick of a thorn touches a believer without Allah wiping away some of their wrongs through it. Either way, you are held.

So keep reciting, keep taking your treatment, and keep your heart with Allah. O Allah, Lord of all people, remove the harm and heal, for You are the Healer; there is no healing but Your healing, a healing that leaves no illness behind.

faq.q: What is ruqyah?
faq.a: Ruqyah is seeking protection and healing through the words of Allah: reciting the Qur'an and the authentic du'as of the Prophet ﷺ over oneself or someone who is ill. The core is al-Fatihah, Ayat al-Kursi and the three Quls. It is a sunnah remedy that goes alongside ordinary medical treatment, not instead of it.

faq.q: How do I perform ruqyah on myself?
faq.a: Recite al-Fatihah, Ayat al-Kursi, and the three Quls (three times each), cupping your hands, reciting into them and wiping over your body or the place of pain. Then make the prophetic du'as for illness gathered here. Do it with sincerity and trust that the cure is from Allah.

faq.q: Is the evil eye real in Islam?
faq.a: Yes. The Prophet ﷺ said the evil eye is true. He also taught the remedy: when you see something that impresses you, in someone else or in your own blessings, invoke blessing for it (say 'barakAllahu fih' or 'masha Allah'), so that admiration does not bring harm. The three Quls are also a protection against it.

faq.q: Does reciting ruqyah mean I shouldn't see a doctor?
faq.a: Not at all. The Prophet ﷺ told us to seek treatment, and said Allah has not sent down an illness without sending down a cure for it. Ruqyah and medicine work together: take your treatment, and turn to Allah, the One who makes any treatment work.

email.subject: Healing words, for when illness comes
email.alt_subject: The ruqyah the Prophet ﷺ used to heal

email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

When illness comes, to us or to someone we love, the believer does two things at once: takes the medicine, and turns to the One who makes the medicine work. Seeking a cure is not a lack of trust; the Prophet ﷺ told us to seek treatment, for Allah has sent down a cure for every illness. These words are that turning. They sit alongside the doctor, never instead of them.

Ruqyah simply means seeking refuge and healing in the words of Allah. This collection opens with its heart: al-Fatihah, Ayat al-Kursi, and the three Quls, the Qur'an the Prophet ﷺ used to heal. After them come his du'as for visiting the sick, for your own aches and pains, and against the evil eye, which he confirmed is real, and taught us to guard against by invoking blessing on what we admire.

Recite them with certainty that the One you are asking can lift any sickness in a moment, and with surrender to whatever He decrees. Sometimes the cure is recovery; sometimes the healing is patience and a quiet washing-away of sins. Either way, you are held.

The full collection, with Arabic, transliteration and meaning, is here:

takes the medicine, and turns to the One who makes the medicine work.

The heart of it is the Qur'an: al-Fatihah, Ayat al-Kursi, and the three Quls, recited into cupped hands and wiped over the body. Then the Prophet's du'as for the sick, for your own pain, and against the evil eye, which he told us is real.

Ruqyah is not instead of the doctor; it sits alongside the treatment. The Prophet ﷺ said Allah has sent down a cure for every illness.

And if the cure is delayed, the healing may be patience: no illness touches a believer without Allah washing away some of their sins through it.
