items.title: Al-Fatihah, the opening and a cure
items.arabic:
بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ
ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ
مَٰلِكِ يَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ
إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَإِيَّاكَ نَسْتَعِينُ
ٱهْدِنَا ٱلصِّرَٰطَ ٱلْمُسْتَقِيمَ
صِرَٰطَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ غَيْرِ ٱلْمَغْضُوبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا ٱلضَّآلِّينَ
items.en: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful. [All] praise is [due] to Allah, Lord of the worlds. The Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful. Sovereign of the Day of Recompense. It is You we worship and You we ask for help. Guide us to the straight path. The path of those upon whom You have bestowed favor, not of those who have earned [Your] anger or of those who are astray.

items.title: Ayat al-Kursi, the greatest verse
items.arabic: ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْحَىُّ ٱلْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُۥ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُۥٓ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَىْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَـُٔودُهُۥ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْعَظِيمُ
items.en: Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.

items.title: The three Quls, refuge from every harm
items.arabic:
قُلْ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ۝ ٱللَّهُ ٱلصَّمَدُ ۝ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ ۝ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُۥ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌۢ
قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلْفَلَقِ ۝ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ ٱلنَّفَّٰثَٰتِ فِى ٱلْعُقَدِ ۝ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ
قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مَلِكِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ إِلَٰهِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مِن شَرِّ ٱلْوَسْوَاسِ ٱلْخَنَّاسِ ۝ ٱلَّذِى يُوَسْوِسُ فِى صُدُورِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۝ مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ
items.en: Say, "He is Allah, [who is] One, Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born, Nor is there to Him any equivalent." / Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak From the evil of that which He created And from the evil of darkness when it settles And from the evil of the blowers in knots And from the evil of an envier when he envies." / Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, The Sovereign of mankind, The God of mankind, From the evil of the retreating whisperer - Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind - From among the jinn and mankind."

items.title: Comforting words for the sick: a purification
items.arabic: لا بأْسَ طَهـورٌ إِنْ شـاءَ الله
items.translit: Lā ba'sa ṭahūrun in shā Allāh.
items.en: Do not worry, it will be a purification (for you), Allah willing.
items.note: What the Prophet ﷺ would say when visiting someone unwell. It reframes the illness gently as a cleansing, not a punishment, and plants hope without false promises.

items.title: Du'a for the sick, said seven times
items.arabic: أَسْـأَلُ اللهَ العَـظيـم، رَبَّ العَـرْشِ العَـظيـم أَنْ يَشْفـيك
items.translit: As'alullāha ‘l-`Aẓīma Rabba ‘l-`Arshil-`Aẓīmi an yashfiyak.
items.en: I ask Almighty Allah, Lord of the Magnificent Throne, to make you well. (Recite seven times in Arabic.)
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught that whoever visits a sick person whose time has not yet come and says this seven times, Allah will cure them of that illness. Say it over the one who is ill, by their bedside.

items.title: For your own pain: hand on the hurt
items.arabic: ضَعْ يَدَكَ عَلَى الَّذِي تَألَّمَ مِنْ جَسَدِكَ وَقُلْ: "بِسْمِ اللَّهِ" ثَلاَثاً، وَقُلْ سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ: "أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ"
items.translit: Put your hand on the place where you feel pain and say:
items.en: Put your hand on the place where you feel pain and say: With the Name of Allah (three times). Then say seven times: I seek refuge in Allah and in His Power from the evil of what I find and of what I guard against.
items.note: When the Companion Uthman ibn Abi al-As complained of a pain in his body, the Prophet ﷺ taught him this. Place your hand where it hurts, say 'Bismillah' three times, then the words of refuge seven times.

items.title: Guarding against the evil eye
items.arabic: إِذَا رَأَى أَحَدُكُم مِنْ أَخِيهِ، أَوْ مِنْ نَفْسِهِ، أَوْ مِنْ مَالِهِ مَا يُعْجِبُهُ [فَلْيَدْعُ لَهُ بِالْبَرَكَةِ] فَإِنَّ الْعَيْنَ حَقٌّ
items.en: If you see something from your brother, yourself or wealth which you find impressing, then invoke blessings for it, for the evil eye is indeed true.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said the evil eye is real, and gave the cure: when something impresses you, in another person or in your own blessings, invoke blessing for it (say 'barakAllahu fih' or 'masha Allah') so admiration brings no harm.

praises-of-allah.name: Praises of Allah
praises-of-allah.epithet: The words light on the tongue, heavy on the scale
praises-of-allah.title: Praises of Allah: tasbih, tahmid, tahlil and takbir, the words the Prophet ﷺ loved, with meaning and source
praises-of-allah.meta_description: The praises of Allah (tasbih, tahmid, tahlil, takbir): SubhanAllah wa bihamdihi, La ilaha illallah, the four most beloved words and al-baqiyat as-salihat, with Arabic, meaning, virtue and source.
praises-of-allah.keywords: subhanallah wa bihamdihi, tasbih, tahmid, tahlil, takbir, la hawla wala quwwata illa billah, baqiyat salihat, praises of Allah, dhikr, Buruja
lede: Some words cost nothing to say and weigh more than the world on the Day of Judgement. The Prophet ﷺ kept pointing to them: short, simple phrases of praise that a tired tongue can carry all day and that fill the scale when everything else is light.
These are the praises of Allah, the dhikr he loved and urged us to repeat: glory, praise, the declaration that there is no god but Him, and His magnification.
intro: These are not tied to a time or a place. They are the dhikr you say walking, waiting, working, in the gaps of the day. Where a number is given, that is the count the Prophet ﷺ attached a specific reward to; the rest you simply repeat.

Each card shows the words to say. The note beneath carries the Prophet's ﷺ own promise about them: sins wiped away like sea foam, a palm tree planted in Paradise, a treasure from from the treasures of Paradise. Say them, and mean them.
praises-of-allah.closing_heading: Why these are the enduring deeds
closing: The Qur'an calls them al-baqiyat as-salihat, the enduring good deeds: when wealth and children and everything else falls away, these words remain, still counting in your favour. They are the cheapest currency to earn and the heaviest to hold.
Glory is to Allah, and praise is to Him; there is no god but Allah; Allah is the Most Great; and there is no power except with Allah. Fill your day with them.

faq.q: What is the best dhikr to say?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ said the dearest words to Allah are four: SubhanAllah (Glory is to Allah), Alhamdulillah (praise is to Allah), La ilaha illallah (there is no god but Allah), and Allahu Akbar (Allah is the Most Great). He also singled out SubhanAllah wa bihamdihi as light on the tongue and heavy on the scale.

faq.q: What does SubhanAllah wa bihamdihi mean and what is its reward?
faq.a: It means 'Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him.' The Prophet ﷺ said whoever says it a hundred times a day has their sins wiped away even if they were like the foam of the sea.

faq.q: What are al-baqiyat as-salihat?
faq.a: The 'enduring good deeds' mentioned in the Qur'an, explained by the Prophet ﷺ as: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, La ilaha illallah, Allahu Akbar, and La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah. They remain in your record when everything worldly is gone.

email.subject: Words light on the tongue, heavy on the scale
email.alt_subject: The cheapest deeds with the heaviest reward
email.preview: The praises the Prophet ﷺ loved: glory, praise, the oneness of Allah, His magnification.
email.body: Assalamu alaikum, Some words cost nothing to say and outweigh the world on the Day of Judgement. The Prophet ﷺ kept pointing to them: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, La ilaha illallah, Allahu Akbar. Light on the tongue, he said, heavy on the scale, beloved to the Most Merciful. He attached astonishing promises to them: say 'Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him' a hundred times and your sins are wiped away like the foam of the sea; say it and a palm tree is planted for you in Paradise. The Qur'an calls them the enduring good deeds, the ones still counting in your favour when everything else has fallen away. We have gathered them here with Arabic, meaning, virtue and audio, so you can fill the gaps of your day with the cheapest currency that buys the most:

social.hook: Some words cost nothing to say. And they outweigh the world on the Day of Judgement.
social.caption: The praises of Allah. Light on the tongue, heavy on the scale, beloved to the Most Merciful: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, La ilaha illallah, Allahu Akbar. Say 'Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him' a hundred times: sins wiped away like sea foam. Say it once: a palm tree planted in Paradise. The Qur'an calls them the enduring good deeds, the ones that remain when everything else is gone. Full set with Arabic, meaning, virtue and audio at buruja.com/adhkar/praises-of-allah #Tasbih #SubhanAllah #Dhikr #PraisesOfAllah #IslamicReminders #BaqiyatSalihat #Buruja #Islam

praises-of-allah.source_note: The dhikr in each card is extracted from the verbatim Hisn al-Muslim text (Sa'eed al-Qahtani, via sunnah.com), where this chapter is narrated as virtue-hadiths with the dhikr given in parentheses; the surrounding hadith narration is kept as the virtue. al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih. Recitation audio is the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com), aligned by dua number. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com. Reflections are Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

items.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
items.en: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: Whoever says: Glorified is Allah and praised is He. one hundred times a day, will have his sins forgiven even if they are like the foam of the sea.
items.title: Glory and praise, a hundred times

items.arabic: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
items.en: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: Whoever says: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is Able to do all things. ten times, will have the reward for freeing four slaves from the Children of Isma'il.
items.title: There is no god but Allah (ten times)

items.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ ، سُبْحانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ
items.en: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: Two words are light on the tongue, weigh heavily in the balance, and are loved by the Most Merciful One: Glorified is Allah and praised is He, Glorified is Allah the Most Great.
items.title: Two words light on the tongue
items.note: Light on the tongue, heavy on the scale, beloved to the Most Merciful: the Prophet ﷺ singled these two out.

items.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
items.en: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: For me to say: Glory is to Allah, and praise is to Allah, and there is none worthy of worship but Allah, and Allah is the Most Great. is dearer to me than all that the sun rises upon (i.e. the whole world).
items.title: Dearer to the Prophet ﷺ than all the world

items.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: أَيَعْجِزُ أَحَدُكُم أَنْ يَكْسِبَ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ؟ فَسَأَلَهُ سَائِلٌ مِنْ جُلَسَائِهِ كَيْفَ يَكْسِبُ أَحَدُنَا أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ؟ قَالَ: يُسَبِّحُ مِائَةَ تَسْبِيحَةٍ، فَيُكتَبُ لَهُ أَلْفُ حَسَنَةٍ أَوْ يُحَطُّ عَنْهُ أَلْفُ خَطِيئَةٍ
items.en: Glory is to Allah and praise is to Him.
items.title: A hundred tasbih, a thousand good deeds
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said: whoever says it a hundred times in a day has a thousand good deeds written for him, or a thousand of his sins wiped away.

items.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
items.en: Whoever says: Glorified is Allah the Most Great and praised is He. will have a date palm planted for him in Paradise.
items.ref: At-Tirmidhi 5/511, and Al-Hakim who graded it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed 1/501
items.title: A palm tree planted in Paradise

items.arabic: لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ
items.en: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: "O Abdullah bin Qais, should I not point you to one of the treasures of Paradise?" I said, "Yes, O Messenger of Allah." So he told me to say: There is no power and no might except by Allah.
items.title: A treasure from the treasures of Paradise
items.note: La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah: the Prophet ﷺ called it a treasure from the treasures of Paradise.

items.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ ، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
items.en: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: The most beloved words to Allah are four: Glorified is Allah, and The praise is for Allah, and There is none worthy of worship but Allah, and Allah is the Most Great.
items.title: The four most beloved words
items.note: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, La ilaha illallah, Allahu Akbar: begin with whichever you like.

items.arabic: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ كَبِيراً، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ كَثِيراً، سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ رَبِّ العَالَمِينَ، لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ ، اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَارْزُقْنِي
items.en: A desert Arab came to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and said, "Teach me a word that I can say. " The Prophet told him to say: There is none worthy of worship but Allah, Who has no partner, Allah is the Great, the Most Great, and praise is to Allah in abundance, glory is to Allah, Lord of the worlds. There is no power and no might but by Allah the Mighty, the Wise. He said, "That is for my Lord, but what about me?" The Prophet (ﷺ) told him to say: O Allah forgive me, and have mercy on me and guide me, and provide for me.
items.ref: Muslim 4/2072, Abu Dawud reports the same Hadith with the addition: and when the Arab left, the Prophet (ﷺ) said: "He has filled his hands with goodness
items.title: A word to hold onto

items.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرِ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَعَافِنِي وَارْزُقْنِي
items.en: Whenever anyone accepted Islam, the Prophet (ﷺ) used to teach him how to pray then he would instruct him to invoke Allah with the following words: O Allah forgive me, and have mercy on me and guide me and give me good health and provide for me.
items.ref: Muslim 4/2073, and in one of Muslim's reports there is the addition: 'For these words combine [the goodness of] this world and the next
items.title: What the Prophet ﷺ taught a new Muslim

items.arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ
items.en: The most excellent invocation is: Praise is for Allah. and the most excellent words of remembrance are: There is none worthy of worship but Allah.
items.ref: At-Tirmidhi 5/462, Ibn Majah 2/1249, and Al-Hakim who graded it authentic and Ath-Tbahabi agreed 1/503
items.title: The best remembrance, the best praise

items.arabic: سبحان الله ، الحمد لله ، لا إله إلا الله ، الله أكبر ، لا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله
items.en: The good deeds which endure are: Glorified is Allah, and The praise is for Allah, and There is none worthy of worship but Allah, and Allah is the Most Great, and There is no power and no might except by Allah.
items.ref: Ahmad (Ahmad Shakir, ed.) and its chain of narration is authentic
items.title: The enduring good deeds (al-baqiyat as-salihat)
items.note: The deeds that outlast the world: glory, praise, the declaration of oneness, magnification, and there is no power except with Allah.

istighfar.name: Istighfar
istighfar.title: Istighfar: how to seek Allah's forgiveness, the formulas of the sunnah, with meaning and source
istighfar.meta_description: Istighfar (seeking forgiveness): Sayyid al-Istighfar (the chief of seeking forgiveness), Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh, and more, with Arabic, transliteration, meaning and source. From Hisn al-Muslim.
istighfar.keywords: istighfar, astaghfirullah, sayyidul istighfar, seeking forgiveness, tawbah, astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh, how to seek forgiveness, Buruja
lede: The Prophet ﷺ was forgiven his past and future, and still he sought Allah's forgiveness more than seventy, even a hundred times a day. Istighfar is not only for after sin; it is the constant turning of a heart that knows it can never thank Allah enough.
These are the formulas of seeking forgiveness from the sunnah, from the short word you can repeat all day to the chief of them all.
intro: Istighfar needs no special time or place. Say it walking, working, lying awake; after every prayer, and especially in the last third of the night when Allah descends and asks who is seeking His forgiveness so He may forgive them.

Begin with the simplest, 'Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh,' and grow into Sayyid al-Istighfar, the chief of seeking forgiveness. And remember the Prophet's ﷺ words: the closest a servant comes to his Lord is in prostration, so make much du'a there.
closing: Allah does not tire of forgiving; we only tire of asking. Istighfar is the door He left open on purpose, the one He promised is never shut while the sun has not yet risen from the west. Whoever keeps to it, the Prophet ﷺ said, Allah grants a way out of every distress and provides for him from where he never expected.
Astaghfirullah, astaghfirullah, astaghfirullah. O Allah, You are my Lord; there is no god but You; forgive me, for none forgives sins but You.

faq.q: What is Sayyid al-Istighfar?
faq.a: It is the 'chief' or 'master' way of seeking forgiveness, taught by the Prophet ﷺ: 'Allahumma anta Rabbi la ilaha illa anta, khalaqtani wa ana abduk...' He said whoever says it during the day with firm faith and dies that day, or says it at night and dies that night, is among the people of Paradise.

faq.q: What is the simplest istighfar?
faq.a: 'Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh' (I seek the forgiveness of Allah and turn to Him in repentance). It is short enough to repeat throughout the day. The Prophet ﷺ, though already forgiven, said it more than a hundred times daily.

faq.q: When is the best time for istighfar?
faq.a: Any time, but especially after each prayer and in the last third of the night, the time of Allah's descent, when He invites those seeking forgiveness. Prostration is also described as the closest a servant is to his Lord, so it is a powerful place to ask.

email.subject: The door He left open on purpose
email.alt_subject: How to seek Allah's forgiveness
email.preview: Istighfar: from the short word you repeat all day to the chief of seeking forgiveness.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

The Prophet ﷺ was forgiven his past and his future, and still he sought Allah's forgiveness more than seventy, even a hundred times a day. Istighfar is not only for after sin. It is the constant turning of a heart that knows it can never thank Allah enough.

Begin with the simplest, 'Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh,' the word you can repeat in the gaps of the day. Grow into Sayyid al-Istighfar, the chief of seeking forgiveness, of which he ﷺ said: whoever says it with certainty and dies that day is among the people of Paradise.

Allah does not tire of forgiving; we only tire of asking. The formulas, with Arabic, meaning, source and audio, are here:

social.hook:
He was forgiven his past and his future.

And still he sought forgiveness 100 times a day.
social.caption:
Istighfar: turning back to Allah.

Not only for after sin, but the constant turning of a heart that knows it can never thank Him enough. From the simple 'Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh' to Sayyid al-Istighfar, the chief of seeking forgiveness: say it with certainty and die that day, and you are among the people of Paradise.

Allah does not tire of forgiving. We only tire of asking.

Full set with Arabic, meaning and audio at buruja.com/adhkar/istighfar

#Istighfar #Astaghfirullah #Tawbah #SeekingForgiveness #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

istighfar.source_note: The istighfar formulas are transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com (Sayyid al-Istighfar is in al-Bukhari, taken as sahih). This collection is curated: the Hisn 'istighfar' chapter is largely virtue-hadiths and encouragements, so the three core recitable formulas of the sunnah are gathered here, with the encouragements (the Prophet ﷺ seeking forgiveness 70-100x a day; nearness in prostration) carried in the framing. Recitation audio is the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com). With thanks to lifewithallah.com; reflections are Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference and grading before publication.

items.title: Sayyid al-Istighfar, the chief of seeking forgiveness
items.arabic: اللّهـمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّـي لا إلهَ إلاّ أَنْتَ خَلَقْتَنـي وَأَنا عَبْـدُك وَأَنا عَلـى عَهْـدِكَ وَوَعْـدِكَ ما اسْتَـطَعْـت أَعـوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَـرِّ ما صَنَـعْت أَبـوءُ لَـكَ بِنِعْـمَتِـكَ عَلَـيَّ وَأَبـوءُ بِذَنْـبي فَاغْفـِرْ لي فَإِنَّـهُ لا يَغْـفِرُ الذُّنـوبَ إِلاّ أَنْتَ
items.translit: Allāhumma anta Rabbī lā ilāha illā ant, khalaqtanī wa anā `abduk, wa anā `alā `ahdika wa wa`dika mastaṭa`t, a`ūdhu bika min sharri mā ṣana`t, abū'u laka bi ni`matika `alay, wa abū'u bidhanbī faghfir lī fa'innahu lā yaghfirudh-dhunūba illā ant.
items.en: O Allah, You are my Lord, there is none worthy of worship but You. You created me and I am your slave. I keep Your covenant, and my pledge to You so far as I am able. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I admit to Your blessings upon me, and I admit to my misdeeds. Forgive me, for there is none who may forgive sins but You.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ called this the best way to ask forgiveness: whoever says it by day with certainty and dies before evening, or by night and dies before morning, is among the people of Paradise.

items.title: Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh
items.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ
items.translit: Astaghfirullāha wa atūbu ilayh.
items.en: I seek the forgiveness of Allah and repent to Him. (Recite one hundred times in Arabic during the day.)
items.note: The simple, endless istighfar. The Prophet ﷺ, though forgiven, sought forgiveness more than a hundred times a day. Say it in the gaps of yours.

items.title: Astaghfirullah al-Azim, and I turn to Him
items.arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الْعَظيمَ الَّذِي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ الْحَيُّ القَيّوُمُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيهِ
items.en: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said: Whoever says: I seek the forgiveness of Allah the Mighty, Whom there is none worthy of worship except Him, the Living, the Eternal, and I repent to Him, Allah will forgive him even if he has deserted the army's ranks.
items.ref: Abu Dawud 2/85, At-Tirmidhi 5/569, and Al-Hakim who declared it authentic and Ath-Thahabi agreed with him 1/511
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said whoever says it is forgiven, even if he had fled from the battlefield, one of the gravest sins.
